issues.cause: The connected database is unavailable or experiencing critical errors, preventing the serverless function from completing its operations.
issues.title: Serverless Function Experiencing Downstream Database Failure
issues.rootReason: Downstream dependency outage or severe degradation.
issues.impactSummary: The serverless function will return errors to its upstream callers, leading to service disruption. Data persistence or retrieval will fail.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and availability of the database service. Check database logs, resource utilization, and connection pool status.

issues.cause: The connected cache service is responding slowly, leading to increased execution times for operations requiring cache access.
issues.title: Serverless Function Experiencing Downstream Cache Latency
issues.rootReason: Downstream dependency performance degradation.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency for serverless function invocations, potentially leading to degraded user experience or upstream timeouts. Could result in higher load on the database due to cache misses.
issues.recommendation: Examine the cache service's metrics for high latency, capacity issues, or network problems. Consider optimizing cache usage patterns or scaling the cache.

issues.cause: An excessive volume of requests from an upstream component is exceeding the serverless function's processing capacity or autoscaling limits.
issues.title: Serverless Function Overloaded by Upstream Traffic
issues.rootReason: Upstream traffic surge or misconfiguration, or inadequate serverless scaling.
issues.impactSummary: Serverless invocations may be throttled or fail, leading to errors and increased latency for upstream callers. This can cascade into broader service degradation.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the upstream component's traffic patterns. Review serverless concurrency limits, memory allocation, and autoscaling policies. Consider implementing rate limiting upstream.

issues.cause: Infrequent invocations or scaling down events lead to new instances needing to initialize, causing a temporary delay for the first few requests to those instances.
issues.title: Serverless Function Experiencing Cold Start Latency
issues.rootReason: Serverless platform operational characteristic combined with traffic patterns.
issues.impactSummary: The first request to a newly initialized serverless instance will experience higher latency, potentially impacting user experience for intermittent or low-traffic services.
issues.recommendation: Consider enabling provisioned concurrency or 'warm-up' mechanisms if the latency impact is critical for specific endpoints. Optimize function initialization code.

issues.cause: The serverless function's high fanout pattern, combined with minor degradation or intermittent errors in a downstream dependency, leads to a magnified impact on the function's overall performance or error rate.
issues.title: Serverless High Fanout Amplifying Downstream Incidents
issues.rootReason: Architectural pattern (high fanout) exacerbating minor downstream issues.
issues.impactSummary: Even small issues in downstream services become significant, causing elevated error rates or unacceptable latency for the serverless function and its upstream callers.
issues.recommendation: Review downstream dependency resilience and error handling within the serverless function. Consider circuit breakers, retries with backoff, or batching requests to reduce fanout pressure.

issues.cause: Being on a synchronous request path means that any processing delay within the serverless function or its downstream dependencies directly delays the response to the upstream caller.
issues.title: Serverless Function Synchronous Path Latency Impact
issues.rootReason: Inherent characteristic of synchronous request-response flow.
issues.impactSummary: Performance degradation in the serverless function or its downstreams immediately translates to increased end-to-end latency for upstream consumers, potentially causing timeouts or poor user experience.
issues.recommendation: Optimize serverless function execution time and downstream dependency interactions. Consider asynchronous patterns if strict real-time response is not always required, or utilize fast, low-latency downstream services.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Database Failure Propagates as Serverless Errors
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: A failure or outage in the {downstream} database dependency causes the {component} serverless function to fail processing requests, leading to an increase in its error rate, which then propagates to the {upstream} caller.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Cache Latency Increases Serverless Response Time
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Elevated latency from the {downstream} cache service directly extends the processing time of {component} serverless function requests, resulting in a higher overall response latency perceived by the {upstream}.

ruleIntents.name: Autoscaling Mitigates Upstream Traffic Surge for Serverless
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: A sudden surge in traffic from the {upstream} load balancer triggers the autoscaling mechanism of {component} serverless function. While this helps absorb the load, there might be a brief period of elevated latency or dropped requests until new instances are provisioned.

ruleIntents.name: High Fanout Magnifies Downstream Errors/Latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Due to its high fanout characteristic, even a minor issue (e.g., latency or transient errors) in one of the multiple parallel calls to a {downstream} dependency (cache or database) can significantly increase the overall error rate or latency of the {component} serverless function.

ruleIntents.name: Synchronous Path Latency Directly Impacts Upstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: As the {component} serverless function is part of a synchronous request path (`is_sync_request_path` trait), any latency within its execution or its calls to {downstream} dependencies will directly and immediately contribute to the response time observed by the {upstream} caller.

issues.cause: One or more downstream compute targets connected to the load balancer are performing poorly or are unhealthy.
issues.title: Downstream Compute Target Degradation
issues.rootReason: Insufficient downstream capacity, faulty compute instances, or application issues on targets.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency and error rates for requests processed by the load balancer, potentially leading to reduced availability.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance of the downstream compute targets. Scale up or replace unhealthy instances. Check application logs on compute targets.

issues.cause: The volume of incoming traffic from upstream services is exceeding the processing capacity of the network load balancer.
issues.title: Upstream Traffic Volume Exceeds Load Balancer Capacity
issues.rootReason: Unexpected traffic surge, insufficient load balancer provisioning, or misconfigured upstream clients.
issues.impactSummary: Load balancer experiences elevated resource utilization, leading to increased request latency, connection timeouts, and potential request drops.
issues.recommendation: Review upstream traffic patterns. Consider scaling out the load balancer capacity or implementing rate limiting at an earlier stage. Optimize downstream application performance to reduce LB load.

issues.cause: The network load balancer itself is running out of critical resources such as CPU, memory, or connection limits.
issues.title: Network Load Balancer Resource Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: High traffic volume, configuration inefficiencies, software bugs, or insufficient resource allocation for the load balancer instance.
issues.impactSummary: Severe degradation in load balancer performance, resulting in high latency, elevated error rates, and connection failures across all connected services.
issues.recommendation: Monitor load balancer resource metrics (CPU, memory, connections). Increase the load balancer's resource allocation or scale to more instances. Review load balancer configuration for optimizations.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Compute Failure Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing increased latency or errors due to degraded performance or failure of its {downstream} compute targets.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Traffic Overload
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing overload from its {upstream} traffic source, potentially leading to increased latency and dropped connections.

ruleIntents.name: Local Resource Exhaustion
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing resource exhaustion (e.g., CPU, memory, or connection limits), leading to performance degradation.

issues.cause: Network or processing delays within the load balancer or its downstream targets.
issues.title: Excessive Request Latency
issues.rootReason: Increased load, network congestion, or slow response times from backend compute instances.
issues.impactSummary: Users experience slow response times, potentially leading to timeouts or abandoned requests.
issues.recommendation: Investigate downstream compute health and performance, review load balancer resource utilization, or consider scaling out backend services.

issues.cause: The number of active connections to the load balancer has reached its configured or intrinsic limit.
issues.title: Load Balancer Connection Capacity Exhausted
issues.rootReason: Spike in incoming traffic, misconfigured connection limits, or prolonged open connections.
issues.impactSummary: New incoming connections are dropped or rejected, leading to service unavailability for new requests.
issues.recommendation: Increase load balancer connection limits, scale out the load balancer, or optimize downstream services to close connections faster.

issues.cause: One or more registered backend compute instances are failing health checks or not responding to requests.
issues.title: Unhealthy Downstream Targets
issues.rootReason: Backend service errors, resource exhaustion on compute instances, or network issues between the load balancer and compute.
issues.impactSummary: Reduced capacity for serving requests, leading to increased load on remaining healthy instances and potential service degradation.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and logs of affected downstream compute instances, ensure proper health check configurations, and verify network connectivity.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Latency Propagation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High latency observed in {downstream} compute instances is propagating through {component}, causing elevated response times for {upstream} traffic.

ruleIntents.name: Load Balancer Connection Saturation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing high connection utilization, leading to new connection rejections from {source} and affecting overall service availability.

ruleIntents.name: Impact of Unhealthy Downstream Capacity
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Reduced capacity due to unhealthy {downstream} compute instances is impacting the effective request handling capacity of {component}, potentially leading to increased load on remaining healthy instances or upstream request failures.

issues.cause: A downstream compute service connected to the API Gateway is experiencing high error rates or is unavailable.
issues.title: API Gateway Experiencing Downstream Service Errors
issues.rootReason: Unhealthy state or failure of a dependent backend service.
issues.impactSummary: Users may experience failed requests or increased latency when interacting with services behind the API Gateway.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and logs of downstream compute services. Check for recent deployments, resource exhaustion, or misconfigurations in the dependent services.

issues.cause: A downstream compute service connected to the API Gateway is responding slowly.
issues.title: API Gateway Experiencing Downstream Service Latency
issues.rootReason: Performance degradation or resource contention in a dependent backend service.
issues.impactSummary: Users may experience increased request latency, or timeouts when interacting with services behind the API Gateway.
issues.recommendation: Investigate performance metrics (CPU, memory, database queries, external calls) of downstream compute services. Look for bottlenecks or recent changes impacting response times.

issues.cause: The API Gateway is experiencing resource exhaustion (e.g., CPU, memory) due to high request volume or inefficient processing.
issues.title: API Gateway Resource Overload
issues.rootReason: Insufficient capacity, traffic spike, or inefficient configuration of the API Gateway.
issues.impactSummary: Users will experience increased latency, timeouts, and errors when accessing any service routed through this API Gateway.
issues.recommendation: Investigate traffic patterns and resource usage on the API Gateway. Consider scaling up or out, optimizing routing rules, or implementing rate limiting upstream.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Service Error Rate Spike
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: An elevated error rate originating from a {downstream} component is affecting {component}, causing it to return errors to its upstream callers.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Service Latency Spike
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Elevated latency from a {downstream} component is causing {component} to experience increased response times and potential timeouts for its upstream callers.

ruleIntents.name: API Gateway Resource Exhaustion
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} itself is experiencing resource exhaustion due to high traffic volume, leading to increased latency and errors for its upstream callers, and potentially impacting downstream service requests.

issues.cause: External Dependency
issues.title: Downstream Database Unavailability
issues.rootReason: Authentication requests cannot be fulfilled without a functioning database.
issues.impactSummary: Complete authentication failure for users.
issues.recommendation: Investigate database health, connectivity, and replication status.

issues.cause: External Dependency
issues.title: Downstream Database Latency
issues.rootReason: Slow database responses directly increase authentication request latency.
issues.impactSummary: Degraded performance for authentication, leading to slow user login times.
issues.recommendation: Optimize database queries, check resource utilization, investigate network latency.

issues.cause: External Dependency
issues.title: Downstream Cache Unavailability
issues.rootReason: Cache miss rate increases dramatically, shifting load to the database and potentially causing its overload.
issues.impactSummary: Increased load on the database, potential performance degradation or cascading failures if database capacity is exceeded.
issues.recommendation: Restore cache service, check cache cluster health, evaluate impact on database.

issues.cause: External Dependency
issues.title: Downstream Cache Latency
issues.rootReason: Slow cache responses increase the overall latency for requests that rely on cached data or cause direct cache misses resulting in database calls.
issues.impactSummary: Marginal increase in authentication latency, potential for increased database load.
issues.recommendation: Investigate cache performance, network latency to cache, and cache hit ratio.

issues.cause: System Behavior
issues.title: Autoscaling Lag During Load Spikes
issues.rootReason: The autoscaling mechanism cannot provision new instances quickly enough to meet a sudden surge in demand.
issues.impactSummary: Temporary performance degradation (high latency, increased errors) until new instances are ready.
issues.recommendation: Tune autoscaling policies (e.g., more aggressive scaling, shorter cooldowns), ensure adequate instance warm-up, pre-warm instances.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Database Unavailability Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {downstream} database is unavailable, preventing {component} from processing authentication requests, leading to increased error rates.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Database Latency Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Increased latency in the {downstream} database is causing {component} authentication requests to slow down significantly.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Cache Unavailability Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {downstream} cache is unavailable, causing {component} to direct all requests to the database, potentially overwhelming it and increasing latency.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Cache Latency Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Increased latency from the {downstream} cache is degrading performance for {component}, leading to slightly slower authentication responses.

ruleIntents.name: Autoscaling Lag During Traffic Spikes
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing increased latency and resource utilization due to autoscaling lag during a sudden traffic spike.

issues.cause: High latency or errors from downstream cache dependency.
issues.title: Authentication Service Latency Due to Slow Cache Response
issues.rootReason: Cache service performance degradation or unavailability.
issues.impactSummary: Users may experience slow login times or authentication failures. Increased load on the authentication service due to retries or longer request processing.
issues.recommendation: Investigate and optimize the downstream cache service. Consider increasing cache capacity or improving cache hit ratio. Implement circuit breakers for cache calls.

issues.cause: Sustained or sudden spike in request volume from the upstream API Gateway, exceeding the authentication service's current processing capacity.
issues.title: Authentication Service Overloaded by Upstream API Gateway Traffic
issues.rootReason: Uncontrolled traffic from API Gateway, insufficient autoscaling capacity/speed for authService, or misconfiguration of rate limits.
issues.impactSummary: Users may experience authentication failures (e.g., 503 errors) or significantly delayed responses. This can propagate to any service depending on authentication.
issues.recommendation: Implement rate limiting at the API Gateway or authentication service. Review autoscaling policies for the authentication service to ensure responsiveness to load spikes. Analyze API Gateway traffic patterns for anomalies.

issues.cause: Internal application errors, misconfigurations, or resource issues within the authentication service itself.
issues.title: Authentication Service Internal Errors Leading to User Authentication Failures
issues.rootReason: Code defects, database connectivity issues, out-of-memory errors, or critical service dependency failures within the authService.
issues.impactSummary: Users will be unable to log in or authenticate, leading to a complete service outage for functionalities requiring authentication. Upstream services (e.g., API Gateway) will report high error rates.
issues.recommendation: Review authentication service logs and metrics for error patterns. Debug application code, check configuration, and ensure internal dependencies are healthy.

ruleIntents.name: CacheLatencyAffectsAuthService
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing increased latency because its downstream dependency, {downstream} (cache), is responding slowly. This is directly impacting authentication request processing times.

ruleIntents.name: UpstreamOverloadCausesAuthServiceCapacityIssues
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing capacity exhaustion and performance degradation due to an excessive request volume originating from its upstream dependency, {upstream} (api_gateway). While {component} is configured for autoscaling, the sudden surge or sustained high load is exceeding its current scaling capabilities or rate limits.

ruleIntents.name: AuthServiceErrorsPropagateUpstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing a high rate of internal errors, leading to authentication failures for requests originating from {upstream} (api_gateway). This directly prevents user authentication and impacts all dependent services.

issues.cause: Excessive write load from upstream compute components.
issues.title: Write Contention on Database
issues.rootReason: Application design leading to high write volume or contention hotspots, or inadequate database scaling for write operations.
issues.impactSummary: Increased write latency, reduced write throughput, and potential timeouts for applications interacting with the database.
issues.recommendation: Optimize application write patterns, implement write sharding, scale database write capacity, or introduce write-behind caches.

issues.cause: High write throughput on the primary database exceeding the replication capabilities of downstream replica databases.
issues.title: Database Replication Lag
issues.rootReason: Insufficient replication bandwidth, CPU resources on replicas, or poor replication configuration relative to primary write load.
issues.impactSummary: Downstream consumers of replicated data experience stale information, leading to data inconsistency across the system.
issues.recommendation: Increase replication bandwidth, scale replica resources (CPU/IOPS), optimize replication stream, or adjust consistency models.

issues.cause: Sustained high write workload consuming all available CPU, memory, or IOPS resources of the database instance.
issues.title: Database Resource Exhaustion (Write-Heavy)
issues.rootReason: Under-provisioned database resources for the expected write load, or inefficient database queries/indexes for write operations.
issues.impactSummary: Severe performance degradation, unresponsiveness, and potential service outages for the database and dependent services.
issues.recommendation: Scale up database resources (CPU, RAM, IOPS), optimize database configuration, improve indexing strategy, or distribute write load.

issues.cause: Slow response times or unavailability of an upstream database that this component depends on for its operations.
issues.title: Upstream Database Dependency Slowdown
issues.rootReason: Performance issues, resource contention, or network problems affecting the upstream database.
issues.impactSummary: Operations on this database that rely on the upstream database will experience increased latency or failures, potentially cascading to dependent services.
issues.recommendation: Investigate and resolve performance bottlenecks in the upstream database, ensure network connectivity, or implement caching/circuit breakers.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream_Compute_Write_Contention
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {upstream} is generating a high volume of write requests, causing contention and increased latency for {component}.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream_Replication_Lag
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing high write throughput, leading to increased replication lag for {downstream}.

ruleIntents.name: Resource_Exhaustion_Write_Heavy
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing resource exhaustion (CPU/IOPS) due to sustained high write load, impacting its performance.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream_Database_Dependency_Slowdown
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Operations on {component} are being bottlenecked by slow responses from its upstream database dependency {upstream}.

issues.cause: The API Gateway component downstream from the load balancer is not responding to health checks or requests, or is returning connection errors.
issues.title: Downstream API Gateway Unresponsive
issues.rootReason: API Gateway instance failure, network partition, misconfiguration, or severe resource exhaustion at the API Gateway.
issues.impactSummary: Clients will experience service unavailability or errors when requesting resources routed through this load balancer, as no healthy downstream targets are available.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health, logs, and resource utilization of the downstream API Gateway instances. Verify network connectivity and security group rules between the load balancer and the API Gateway.

issues.cause: The API Gateway component downstream from the load balancer is responding slowly to requests, leading to increased overall request processing time.
issues.title: High Latency from Downstream API Gateway
issues.rootReason: API Gateway resource contention (CPU/memory), slow database queries, inefficient application logic, or latency from its own upstream dependencies.
issues.impactSummary: Clients will experience significantly increased request latency when accessing resources routed through this load balancer, potentially leading to timeouts or poor user experience.
issues.recommendation: Investigate performance metrics and logs of the downstream API Gateway instances. Look for resource bottlenecks, long-running processes, or slow internal dependencies affecting the API Gateway.

issues.cause: The load balancer has exhausted its available connections, concurrent request capacity, or other vital resources, preventing it from processing new client requests.
issues.title: Load Balancer Connection Limit Reached
issues.rootReason: Sudden surge in client traffic, insufficient load balancer capacity provisioning, long-lived client connections, or denial-of-service attack.
issues.impactSummary: New client requests will be rejected, dropped, or timed out at the load balancer level, leading to service unavailability for affected clients.
issues.recommendation: Scale up load balancer capacity (e.g., add more instances, increase maximum connections). Review connection timeout settings and consider implementing client-side rate limiting or request throttling if the traffic surge is sustained.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream API Gateway Unavailability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {downstream} API Gateway instances are failing health checks or rejecting connections from {component}, leading to service unavailability observed by the load balancer.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream API Gateway Latency Spike
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Requests from {component} to {downstream} are experiencing high latency, indicating performance issues within the API Gateway service impacting overall request duration.

ruleIntents.name: Local Load Balancer Connection Exhaustion
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing connection limit exhaustion, likely due to an overwhelming number of client requests or misconfigured connection pooling settings.

issues.cause: A downstream dependency (e.g., database, cache, or other service) is experiencing high latency, causing the app server to wait for responses.
issues.title: Downstream Service Latency Degrading App Server Performance
issues.rootReason: Slow processing or resource contention within one or more downstream services connected to the app server, amplified by the app server's synchronous request path.
issues.impactSummary: Increased response times and potential timeout errors for the app server, leading to a degraded user experience or reduced throughput for upstream services.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the performance metrics of {downstream} services. Optimize queries, increase capacity, or implement asynchronous patterns where applicable. Consider adjusting app server timeouts or implementing client-side retries with exponential backoff.

issues.cause: A downstream dependency (e.g., database, cache, or other service) is unavailable or returning errors, preventing the app server from completing requests.
issues.title: Downstream Service Failure Causing App Server Errors
issues.rootReason: Failure or critical error state in a required downstream service, preventing successful communication from the app server, potentially leading to cascading failures.
issues.impactSummary: App server is unable to process requests that rely on the failing downstream service, leading to service unavailability or errors for users/upstream services.
issues.recommendation: Examine the health and error logs of the {downstream} services. Ensure high availability for critical dependencies. Leverage the app server's circuit breaker to isolate failures and implement robust error handling and fallbacks.

issues.cause: The upstream component (e.g., load balancer) is sending a sustained volume of requests exceeding the app server's current capacity or configured rate limits.
issues.title: App Server Overloaded by Upstream Requests
issues.rootReason: High incoming traffic volume, misconfigured load balancing, or insufficient scaling capacity of the app server to handle the current load.
issues.impactSummary: App server experiences high resource utilization, increased latency, and potential request drops or errors, impacting its ability to serve traffic effectively.
issues.recommendation: Investigate upstream traffic patterns. Scale out the app server horizontally (if 'autoscale' is not sufficient or misconfigured). Implement stricter rate limiting upstream or within the app server. Review load balancer configuration for even distribution.

assumptions: The app server primarily acts as a synchronous request handler, waiting for downstream responses ('is_sync_request_path').
Downstream services are critical dependencies for the app server's functionality, especially given 'fanout_high'.
The app server's 'autoscale' trait may mitigate overload, but sustained, rapid spikes can still lead to transient issues or resource exhaustion before scaling completes.

The 'has_circuit_breaker' trait is assumed to be active for critical downstream calls, but its activation is a symptom of a problem and still impacts app server functionality (e.g., degraded mode or errors).
The 'has_rate_limiting' trait helps the app server protect itself but does not resolve the root cause of upstream overload.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Downstream Latency Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing elevated latency for requests to {downstream}, which is causing slow responses for {component} itself due to its synchronous request path.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Downstream Failure Impact (Cascading)
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Requests from {component} to {downstream} are failing, leading to errors in {component}'s processing and potentially causing cascading failures in the upstream.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Upstream Overload to App Server
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing high request volume from {upstream}, leading to resource saturation and performance degradation despite its rate limiting and autoscaling capabilities.

issues.cause: The downstream database component is unreachable or experiencing a severe outage.
issues.title: Downstream Database Unreachable
issues.rootReason: Loss of connectivity or complete failure of a critical downstream dependency.
issues.impactSummary: Service degradation or complete outage for requests requiring database access, leading to errors for upstream components.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and connectivity of the dependent database component. Restore database availability.

issues.cause: The upstream load balancer is sending more traffic than the application server can handle, potentially due to misconfiguration or a sudden spike in client requests.
issues.title: Upstream Load Balancer Sending Excessive Traffic
issues.rootReason: Insufficient capacity or mismanaged traffic distribution from an upstream load balancer.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, errors, and potential resource exhaustion on the application server, affecting overall service availability.
issues.recommendation: Investigate load balancer configuration, traffic patterns, and application server scaling. Implement rate limiting or scale out application server instances.

issues.cause: The downstream cache component is experiencing high latency, leading to slower response times for the application server.
issues.title: Downstream Cache Responding Slowly
issues.rootReason: Performance bottlenecks or resource contention within the cache infrastructure.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end request latency for user-facing operations that rely on cached data.
issues.recommendation: Investigate cache performance metrics, resource utilization, and potential hot-spots or contention. Optimize cache queries or scale cache capacity.

issues.cause: The component's internal circuit breaker has tripped to prevent cascading failures, typically due to a high rate of errors from a downstream dependency.
issues.title: Circuit Breaker Activated Due to Downstream Failures
issues.rootReason: Protective mechanism activating in response to sustained downstream dependency issues.
issues.impactSummary: Requests to the failed downstream dependency will be fast-failed, leading to errors or degraded functionality for the affected features.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the underlying cause of downstream errors that led to the circuit breaker trip. Once resolved, manually or automatically reset the circuit breaker.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Database Unavailability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing failures due to an issue with its {downstream} database dependency.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Upstream Traffic Spike
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing high load and potential degradation due to excessive traffic from its {upstream} load balancer.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Cache Latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing increased latency due to slow responses from its {downstream} cache dependency.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Circuit Breaker Trip
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The circuit breaker in {component} has tripped, preventing calls to a failing {downstream} dependency.

issues.cause: Data replication from the downstream database is lagging or failing.
issues.title: Replica Data Stale
issues.rootReason: Underlying database performance issues, network latency, or replication process errors on {component} itself.
issues.impactSummary: {component} may serve outdated information, leading to inconsistencies for consuming services.
issues.recommendation: Investigate downstream database health, replication logs, and network connectivity between {component} and its downstream database.

issues.cause: {component} has failed or is unresponsive.
issues.title: Replica Unavailability
issues.rootReason: Internal process crash, resource exhaustion, or host machine failure.
issues.impactSummary: {component} is unable to serve read requests or participate in failover, reducing system redundancy and potentially impacting read capacity.
issues.recommendation: Examine {component} logs, resource utilization, and host system health. Initiate failover procedures if necessary.

assumptions: The replicaNode's primary function is to maintain a redundant copy of data from a single downstream database.
The replicaNode is expected to serve read traffic, which may be affected by staleness or unavailability.
Failover mechanisms, while implied, are not explicitly modeled within this specialization.

ruleIntents.name: DownstreamDatabaseReplicationLagImpactsReplica
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing data staleness due to replication lag originating from its dependent downstream database ({downstream}).

ruleIntents.name: ReplicaFailureImpactsSelfAvailability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is unavailable, leading to a direct loss of its services and potential impact on read availability for systems relying on it.

issues.cause: The upstream database component is experiencing high latency or is unavailable, causing delays in data retrieval or storage for the primary node.
issues.title: Upstream Database Latency Affecting Primary Node
issues.rootReason: External dependency performance degradation or failure.
issues.impactSummary: Increased response times for requests handled by the primary node, potentially leading to cascading failures downstream.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance of the upstream database component. Check database logs, resource utilization, and connection pool status.

issues.cause: The downstream compute component is overloaded or slow to process requests, leading to backpressure and queueing within the primary node.
issues.title: Downstream Compute Backpressure on Primary Node
issues.rootReason: Downstream dependency resource exhaustion or processing bottleneck.
issues.impactSummary: Increased internal queues and resource consumption on the primary node, potentially leading to request timeouts or resource exhaustion.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and capacity of the downstream compute component. Check compute resource utilization, queue lengths, and processing rates. Consider scaling out or optimizing downstream services.

ruleIntents.name: DetectUpstreamDatabaseLatency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing degraded performance due to high latency from its {upstream} database dependency. This is impacting operations requested by {downstream} components.

ruleIntents.name: DetectDownstreamComputeBackpressure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing backpressure from its {downstream} compute dependency, causing internal queues to build up and affecting upstream processing. Requests from {upstream} may experience delays.

issues.cause: Downstream database performance degradation
issues.title: Application server experiencing increased latency due to downstream database
issues.rootReason: Database queries are slow or database is overloaded, impacting dependent services.
issues.impactSummary: Increased application response times, potential timeouts for users, and degraded user experience.
issues.recommendation: Investigate database performance, optimize queries, scale database resources, or implement read replicas/caching.

issues.cause: Upstream load balancer health check failure or misconfiguration
issues.title: Application server not receiving traffic due to upstream load balancer issues
issues.rootReason: Load balancer is unable to route traffic to the app server instances, possibly due to instance unhealthiness or LB configuration errors.
issues.impactSummary: Reduced or zero incoming traffic to the application server, resulting in service unavailability from the user's perspective.
issues.recommendation: Check load balancer health, target group configuration, backend instance health, and network connectivity.

issues.cause: Autoscaling mechanism failure, resource limits reached, or scaling policies misconfigured
issues.title: Application server unable to autoscale to meet demand
issues.rootReason: The autoscaler failed to add or remove instances as needed, leading to either resource exhaustion or over-provisioning.
issues.impactSummary: Application may become overloaded under increased traffic, leading to performance degradation, increased latency, or unavailability. Conversely, may lead to increased costs due to over-provisioning.
issues.recommendation: Review autoscaling group configuration, capacity limits, monitoring metrics, and underlying infrastructure for resource availability.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate downstream database latency to app server
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing increased latency because its critical downstream database {downstream} is slow, leading to blocked requests.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate upstream load balancer availability issues to app server
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is not receiving expected traffic volume because its upstream load balancer {upstream} is reporting unhealthy targets or misrouting traffic, making {component} unavailable externally.

issues.cause: The tool registry relies on an upstream AI model for its core functionality, which is currently unavailable or unresponsive.
issues.title: Upstream AI Model Unavailability
issues.rootReason: Dependency failure in a critical upstream AI model.
issues.impactSummary: The tool registry will be unable to process requests that require AI model inference, leading to a complete service disruption for AI-dependent operations.
issues.recommendation: Ensure the upstream AI model component is operational and accessible. Check its logs and health status.

issues.cause: The tool registry is connected to an upstream 'sketchy' component, which is exhibiting high latency or an elevated error rate, leading to degraded performance for the tool registry.
issues.title: Upstream Sketchy Dependency Causing Degradation
issues.rootReason: Dependency degradation in a non-critical or auxiliary upstream component.
issues.impactSummary: Operations relying on the 'sketchy' upstream component will experience increased latency and potentially higher error rates, leading to a degraded user experience.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the 'sketchy' upstream component for performance bottlenecks or reliability issues. Consider alternative data sources or rate limiting.

ruleIntents.name: Detect AI Model Dependency Failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing functional disruption because its critical upstream AI model, {source}, is unavailable or returning high error rates.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Sketchy Upstream Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing degraded performance due to issues with its upstream 'sketchy' dependency, {source}, which is showing high latency or error rates.

issues.cause: The upstream API Gateway is experiencing performance degradation, potentially due to high load, resource contention, or internal issues, leading to slower responses or increased error rates for the reverse proxy.
issues.title: Increased Latency or Errors from Upstream API Gateway
issues.rootReason: Resource constraints or application-specific failures in the upstream API Gateway.
issues.impactSummary: Directly impacts the ability of the reverse proxy to serve requests, leading to degraded user experience or service outages if severe, especially in a synchronous request path.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance metrics of the upstream API Gateway components. Check for resource saturation (CPU, memory, network I/O) or specific application errors within the API Gateway service. Ensure proper load balancing and scaling for the API Gateway.

issues.cause: The downstream Load Balancer is experiencing performance issues, either due to internal problems or issues with its own backend services, resulting in delayed responses or errors when the reverse proxy attempts to forward requests.
issues.title: Increased Latency or Errors from Downstream Load Balancer
issues.rootReason: Load balancer misconfiguration, saturation, or issues with its target groups/backend services.
issues.impactSummary: Prevents the reverse proxy from effectively distributing and forwarding requests, leading to service degradation or unavailability for end-users, critical for synchronous operations.
issues.recommendation: Examine the health and configuration of the downstream Load Balancer. Verify its backend health checks and ensure target services are responsive. Check for resource exhaustion on the load balancer itself.

issues.cause: The reverse proxy itself is experiencing internal performance issues, such as high CPU utilization, memory pressure, network saturation on its own interfaces, or misconfiguration, independent of its upstream or downstream dependencies.
issues.title: Reverse Proxy Internal Performance Degradation
issues.rootReason: Resource exhaustion, configuration errors, or software bugs within the reverse proxy service.
issues.impactSummary: Directly impacts all traffic passing through the reverse proxy, leading to increased latency, error rates, or complete unavailability for clients.
issues.recommendation: Review the reverse proxy's resource metrics (CPU, memory, network I/O, open connections). Check its configuration for any recent changes or known performance bottlenecks. Scale out or optimize the reverse proxy instance.

assumptions: The reverse proxy is part of a synchronous request path, meaning latency and errors are immediately visible to the end-user or calling service and directly impact user experience.

ruleIntents.name: Observe Upstream API Gateway Performance Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {upstream} API Gateway's performance degradation is causing {component} (reverse proxy) to experience increased latency and potential errors when forwarding requests.

ruleIntents.name: Observe Downstream Load Balancer Performance Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Issues with the {downstream} Load Balancer are causing {component} (reverse proxy) to accumulate requests and potentially return errors due to inability to forward traffic.

ruleIntents.name: Monitor Reverse Proxy Core Metrics for Self-Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} (reverse proxy) is experiencing internal performance issues, leading to increased latency and errors for both upstream and downstream interactions.

issues.cause: The graph database is failing to process queries efficiently, leading to high latency or errors for dependent upstream applications.
issues.title: Graph Database Causing Upstream Application Performance Degradation
issues.rootReason: Insufficient database resources, unoptimized queries, high transaction volume, or internal database contention.
issues.impactSummary: Upstream applications experience slow response times or errors, potentially leading to user-facing service disruptions.
issues.recommendation: Investigate graph database logs and metrics for bottlenecks. Optimize slow queries, scale database resources (CPU, memory, disk I/O), or implement read replicas/caching.

issues.cause: The graph database component is running critically low on essential resources such as CPU, memory, or disk I/O.
issues.title: Graph Database Resource Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: Sudden increase in load, inefficient queries, insufficient provisioned capacity, or memory leaks.
issues.impactSummary: The graph database becomes unresponsive or extremely slow, leading to widespread performance degradation and potential unavailability for all dependent services.
issues.recommendation: Monitor resource utilization and identify peak usage patterns. Scale up or scale out the graph database instance, optimize resource-intensive operations, or review configuration limits.

issues.cause: A critical downstream database system, on which the graph database relies for operations like replication or persistence, is experiencing an outage or severe performance degradation.
issues.title: Graph Database Impacted by Downstream Dependency Failure
issues.rootReason: Downstream database unavailability, high latency, data corruption, or network issues preventing communication with the downstream system.
issues.impactSummary: The graph database may suffer from data consistency issues, loss of durability, or become unable to process write operations, severely impacting its reliability and data integrity.
issues.recommendation: Address the underlying issues in the downstream database system. Verify network connectivity, data integrity, and operational status of the downstream dependency.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Consumer Performance Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Performance degradation observed at {upstream} due to high latency or error rates when interacting with {component}.

ruleIntents.name: GraphDB Resource Exhaustion
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing resource exhaustion (e.g., CPU, memory, disk I/O), leading to service degradation or unavailability for its consumers.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Database Dependency Failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is impacted by issues with its downstream database dependency ({downstream}), affecting data consistency, availability, or transaction speed.

issues.cause: The upstream load balancer is not directing requests to this application server instance, potentially due to health check failures, misconfiguration, or an issue with the load balancer itself.
issues.title: Application Server Not Receiving Traffic from Upstream Load Balancer
issues.rootReason: Application server health check failure or load balancer configuration issue prevents traffic from reaching the component.
issues.impactSummary: This application server instance is effectively isolated from the request path, reducing overall capacity and potentially causing service degradation if other instances are also affected. No user requests will be served by this instance.
issues.recommendation: Verify application server health and its health check endpoints. Inspect load balancer configuration and logs for issues in routing traffic or health probe results. Check network connectivity between load balancer and application server.

issues.cause: The application server is overloaded or experiencing a resource bottleneck (CPU, memory, I/O, network) preventing it from processing requests effectively.
issues.title: Application Server Experiencing Resource Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: High demand or inefficient resource utilization leads to critical resource exhaustion on the application server.
issues.impactSummary: The application server will respond slowly or fail to respond to requests, directly impacting user experience and contributing to increased latency and error rates for clients routed to this instance. This can cascade to the load balancer marking the instance unhealthy.
issues.recommendation: Investigate application server metrics for CPU, memory, disk I/O, and network utilization. Scale out instances, optimize application code, or adjust resource limits. Check for underlying issues like memory leaks or inefficient database queries.

assumptions: The 'load_balancer' upstream role bucket implies a direct request-response pattern where the appServer serves as a backend.
The 'is_sync_request_path' trait indicates that performance and error rates of this appServer directly affect the upstream component's experience (latency, errors).

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Load Balancer Health Check Response
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {upstream} stops sending traffic to {component} because {component} is failing its health checks or reports unhealthy status.

ruleIntents.name: Application Server Latency Impact on Synchronous Request Path
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Increased processing latency within {component} directly causes {upstream} to experience higher request latencies, impacting the overall synchronous request path.

ruleIntents.name: Application Server Error Rate Impact on Synchronous Request Path
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Increased error rates within {component} directly cause {upstream} to observe higher error rates for requests it forwards, affecting the synchronous request path.

issues.cause: The dependent 'notification' service is not responding or is unhealthy, preventing {component} from performing its functions related to sending notifications.
issues.title: Upstream notification service is unavailable or unhealthy
issues.rootReason: Loss of connectivity or health check failure to the upstream 'notification' service.
issues.impactSummary: {component} may be unable to send notifications, impacting user experience where notifications are critical.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and connectivity of the 'notification' service. Ensure network paths are clear and the 'notification' service is running and responsive.

issues.cause: The dependent 'user' service is not responding or is unhealthy, preventing {component} from performing its functions related to user profile management or authentication.
issues.title: Upstream user service is unavailable or unhealthy
issues.rootReason: Loss of connectivity or health check failure to the upstream 'user' service.
issues.impactSummary: {component} may be unable to retrieve or manage user data, leading to failed requests or degraded user experiences.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and connectivity of the 'user' service. Ensure network paths are clear and the 'user' service is running and responsive.

issues.cause: The upstream 'notification' service is responding slowly, causing delays in operations within {component} that rely on notifications.
issues.title: High latency from upstream notification service
issues.rootReason: Overload, resource contention, or performance issues within the 'notification' service.
issues.impactSummary: {component} experiences increased response times and potential timeouts for operations involving notifications.
issues.recommendation: Examine the performance metrics of the 'notification' service, including CPU, memory, and database response times. Scale the 'notification' service if necessary.

issues.cause: The upstream 'user' service is responding slowly, causing delays in operations within {component} that rely on user data.
issues.title: High latency from upstream user service
issues.rootReason: Overload, resource contention, or performance issues within the 'user' service.
issues.impactSummary: {component} experiences increased response times and potential timeouts for operations involving user data.
issues.recommendation: Examine the performance metrics of the 'user' service, including CPU, memory, and database response times. Scale the 'user' service if necessary.

ruleIntents.name: Detect 'notification' service unavailability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The upstream 'notification' service, which {component} depends on, is experiencing an availability issue, directly impacting {component}'s ability to function correctly.

ruleIntents.name: Detect 'user' service unavailability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The upstream 'user' service, which {component} depends on, is experiencing an availability issue, directly impacting {component}'s ability to function correctly.

ruleIntents.name: Detect 'notification' service latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High latency from the upstream 'notification' service is causing performance degradation in {component} operations.

ruleIntents.name: Detect 'user' service latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High latency from the upstream 'user' service is causing performance degradation in {component} operations.

issues.cause: Upstream compute services responsible for generating notification requests are degraded or unavailable.
issues.title: Upstream Compute Service Degradation Affecting Notification Delivery
issues.rootReason: Loss of application logic producing notification events.
issues.impactSummary: Reduced or halted generation of notifications by the system, leading to users not receiving expected communications.
issues.recommendation: Investigate health and performance of upstream compute services. Verify their output queues or APIs for backlog or errors.

issues.cause: External services or gateways responsible for delivering notifications to users (e.g., email service, push notification gateway) are failing or unreachable.
issues.title: Notification Delivery Channel Failure
issues.rootReason: Third-party service outage or network connectivity issues to downstream providers.
issues.impactSummary: Users are not receiving notifications, despite the notification service attempting to send them. Messages may be queued or lost.
issues.recommendation: Check status of external notification delivery providers. Verify network connectivity and credentials for API calls to these services.

issues.cause: The notification service itself is experiencing high load, leading to CPU, memory, or connection exhaustion.
issues.title: Notification Service Resource Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: Spike in notification volume, misconfiguration, or memory leak.
issues.impactSummary: Notification processing slows down or stops, leading to delayed or failed delivery of all notifications.
issues.recommendation: Scale out the notification service instances, optimize resource usage, or investigate sudden traffic spikes.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Compute Degradation to Notification Service
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {upstream} service degradation is preventing {component} from receiving requests to generate notifications.

ruleIntents.name: Notification Service Downstream User Delivery Failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is unable to deliver notifications to {downstream} due to external channel failure.

ruleIntents.name: Notification Service Resource Exhaustion
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing resource exhaustion, leading to inability to process notifications.

issues.cause: The upstream 'compute' component is experiencing performance degradation or failures, leading to reduced throughput or increased latency for the custom service.
issues.title: Custom Service Degradation Due to Upstream Compute Issues
issues.rootReason: Insufficient resources, software defects, or infrastructure issues within the upstream 'compute' dependency.
issues.impactSummary: The custom service experiences reduced capacity and increased response times, potentially leading to service unavailability for downstream users.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance metrics of the upstream 'compute' component. Scale 'compute' resources, fix identified bugs, or address infrastructure problems. Consider implementing retry mechanisms and circuit breakers in the custom service.

issues.cause: The custom service itself is experiencing internal issues such as resource exhaustion, high error rates, or processing bottlenecks.
issues.title: Custom Service Internal Degradation
issues.rootReason: Resource limits reached, software defects, configuration errors, or unexpected traffic spikes overwhelming the service.
issues.impactSummary: Downstream 'user' components experience increased latency, higher error rates, or service unavailability when attempting to access the custom service.
issues.recommendation: Review custom service logs and metrics for anomalies. Scale service instances, optimize code, adjust configurations, or apply rate limiting to manage load. If applicable, trigger failover to a healthy target.

issues.cause: The designated failover target for the custom service is either unavailable, unhealthy, or experiencing issues preventing a successful failover.
issues.title: Custom Service Failover Target Unavailable or Failing
issues.rootReason: Failover target component failure, misconfiguration of failover mechanisms, or network connectivity issues to the failover target.
issues.impactSummary: Automatic recovery from custom service degradation or failure is inhibited, leading to prolonged service disruption and increased downtime for downstream 'user' components.
issues.recommendation: Verify the health and configuration of the failover target. Ensure network connectivity. Investigate failover logic and automation. Consider manual intervention if failover automation is stalled.

ruleIntents.name: Impact from Upstream Compute Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {upstream} component is experiencing issues, leading to degradation of the {component}.

ruleIntents.name: Custom Service Internal Issues Impacting Downstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing internal issues, which is impacting the {downstream} component.

ruleIntents.name: Failover Target Issues Prevent Recovery
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The failover target for {component} is unavailable or failing, preventing successful recovery.

issues.cause: The App Server cannot successfully complete requests due to errors or high latency from its downstream Database service. As a synchronous request path component, this directly blocks upstream requests.
issues.title: App Server Impacted by Downstream Database Issues
issues.rootReason: Failure or performance degradation of the associated database instance, potentially due to resource contention, slow queries, or network problems. The 'fanout_high' trait amplifies the chance of encountering these issues across multiple database interactions.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency and error rates for the app server, leading to degraded user experience for features relying on database operations, and potential availability impact on the synchronous request path.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance metrics of the downstream Database service. Optimize database queries or scale database resources if necessary. Implement robust retry mechanisms with backoff and circuit breakers in the app server to gracefully handle database outages.

issues.cause: The App Server is experiencing difficulties sending notifications or completing notification-related tasks due to issues with its downstream Notification service.
issues.title: App Server Impacted by Downstream Notification Service Issues
issues.rootReason: Unavailability, high latency, or error conditions within the notification service, potentially due to message queue backlogs, service overload, or integration problems. The 'fanout_high' trait increases exposure to potential issues across multiple notification calls.
issues.impactSummary: Notification failures or delays, potentially impacting user communication or system events. While often not critical to the primary synchronous request path, it can still lead to user frustration or missed business events.
issues.recommendation: Examine the health and performance metrics of the downstream Notification service. Check for backlogs in message queues or service logs for errors. Consider implementing asynchronous notification patterns, dead-letter queues, or graceful degradation for non-critical notifications.

issues.cause: The App Server cannot successfully complete requests requiring user profile or authentication information due to issues with its downstream User Service. As a synchronous request path component, this directly blocks upstream requests.
issues.title: App Server Impacted by Downstream User Service Issues
issues.rootReason: Unavailability, high latency, or error conditions within the user service, potentially due to database issues, authentication system failures, or service overload. The 'fanout_high' trait means more chances to hit a problematic user service instance.
issues.impactSummary: Degraded or unavailable functionality related to user authentication, profile management, or authorization, severely impacting user experience and core application features on the synchronous request path.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance metrics of the downstream User Service. Check its dependencies and internal state. Implement proper error handling, caching, and circuit breakers for user service calls to protect the app server.

issues.cause: The App Server's resources (CPU, memory, network connections) are being fully utilized, leading to performance degradation or unresponsiveness. Despite 'autoscale', sudden spikes or limits can be reached before scaling occurs or if scaling limits are hit.
issues.title: App Server Experiencing Resource Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: Sudden increase in incoming request volume from {upstream}, inefficient application code, memory leaks, or insufficient provisioned capacity or autoscaling misconfiguration. The 'fanout_high' trait means each incoming request might consume more resources for downstream calls.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, elevated error rates (e.g., HTTP 5xx errors), and potential service unavailability for incoming requests, directly affecting user experience on the synchronous request path.
issues.recommendation: Examine app server resource metrics (CPU, memory, active connections, request queue depth). Profile application code for bottlenecks. Verify autoscaling policies are effective and scaling limits are appropriate. Consider implementing rate limiting at the {upstream} API Gateway or in the app server itself. Improve resource efficiency of downstream calls.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Dependency Degradation for Synchronous Path
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing degraded performance or errors due to issues with its downstream {downstream} service, which is a critical part of its synchronous request path. The 'fanout_high' trait means a single request to {component} can be impacted by multiple downstream issues, increasing the probability of overall service degradation.

ruleIntents.name: AppServer Resource Saturation Leading to Performance Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing high resource utilization, potentially leading to increased latency or errors for its callers, often due to an influx of requests from {upstream} or internal processing issues. Despite 'autoscale', a sudden surge or inefficient processing might exceed current capacity, especially with 'fanout_high' increasing per-request resource consumption.

issues.cause: The connected upstream load balancer is not responding or is unhealthy, preventing traffic delivery to the ingress.
issues.title: Upstream Load Balancer Unresponsive
issues.rootReason: Failure or misconfiguration of the upstream load balancer component, or network connectivity issues between the ingress and the load balancer.
issues.impactSummary: The ingress will not receive traffic from the upstream load balancer, leading to service unavailability for incoming requests.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and configuration of the load balancer. Verify network connectivity from the ingress to the load balancer. Check load balancer logs for errors.

issues.cause: The ingress component is receiving a traffic volume that exceeds its processing capacity, leading to resource exhaustion.
issues.title: Ingress Component Overload
issues.rootReason: Insufficient ingress capacity, misconfigured traffic shaping, or an unexpected surge in incoming requests.
issues.impactSummary: Increased request latency, elevated error rates, and potential unavailability for incoming requests due to dropped connections or timeouts.
issues.recommendation: Scale out ingress instances horizontally, increase resource limits for existing instances, implement rate limiting or circuit breakers, or optimize ingress configuration for higher throughput.

ruleIntents.name: UpstreamLoadBalancerFailureImpact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {upstream} component, acting as a load balancer for the {component} ingress, is failing or unresponsive, directly blocking incoming traffic.

ruleIntents.name: IngressOverloadSelfImpact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} ingress is experiencing resource saturation due to high incoming traffic volume, leading to degraded performance and potential request drops.

issues.cause: Incoming traffic volume exceeds the processing capacity of the load balancer or its configured maximum connections.
issues.title: Network Load Balancer Capacity Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: Insufficient scaling of the load balancer itself, or an unexpected surge in upstream traffic.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency for requests, dropped connections, and potential service unavailability for end-users.
issues.recommendation: Scale out the load balancer instances, implement stricter rate limiting upstream, or reduce traffic demand.

issues.cause: One or more registered downstream targets are failing health checks or are reported as unhealthy by the load balancer.
issues.title: Unhealthy Downstream Targets Detected
issues.rootReason: Application failures on target instances, resource exhaustion on targets, or network connectivity issues between NLB and targets.
issues.impactSummary: Reduced aggregate capacity, increased load on healthy targets leading to potential cascade failure, and degraded performance.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health of downstream instances, verify health check configurations, or replace/remediate unhealthy targets.

issues.cause: The rate of incoming traffic from the upstream network is unexpectedly low or limited, preventing the load balancer from receiving its expected traffic volume.
issues.title: Upstream Network Throughput Limitation
issues.rootReason: Congestion or failure in upstream network components, DDoS mitigation policies, or misconfiguration at the network edge.
issues.impactSummary: Underutilization of load balancer and downstream resources, perceived service degradation due to unavailable traffic.
issues.recommendation: Review upstream network health and configuration, investigate potential DDoS attacks or traffic shaping.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Traffic Reduction Affects NLB Ingress
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: A significant reduction in throughput from {upstream} components leads to {component} receiving less traffic than expected, indicating an upstream bottleneck or issue preventing traffic from reaching the load balancer.

ruleIntents.name: NLB High Utilization Causing Upstream Request Failures
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: When {component} exceeds its operational capacity, it struggles to process incoming requests, resulting in increased connection rejections, timeouts, and elevated error rates observed by {upstream} components.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Unhealthy Targets Reduce NLB Effective Capacity
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} detects unhealthy {downstream} targets, removing them from the active pool and thereby reducing the total available capacity. This can lead to increased load on remaining healthy targets and potential overload.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Latency Propagates Through NLB
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Increased processing time or network latency within {downstream} components directly contributes to elevated end-to-end latency for requests flowing through {component}, even if {component} itself is not overloaded.

issues.cause: One or more registered backend targets are failing health checks from the load balancer.
issues.title: Load Balancer Backend Health Check Failure
issues.rootReason: Backend service unavailability, misconfiguration of backend health endpoints, or network connectivity issues between the load balancer and backend instances.
issues.impactSummary: Reduced capacity or complete unavailability of services served by the load balancer, leading to client connection failures or timeouts.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health status of backend services, verify network ACLs and security groups, and review load balancer health check configuration parameters such as port, protocol, and thresholds.

issues.cause: The load balancer is exceeding its configured or provisioned capacity for processing connections or throughput.
issues.title: Network Load Balancer Capacity Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: Insufficiently provisioned load balancer capacity, sudden and sustained traffic spikes, or a resource leak within the load balancer itself (e.g., high CPU, memory, or connection table limits).
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, connection errors, and potential service unavailability for clients attempting to connect through the load balancer.
issues.recommendation: Scale up or scale out the load balancer, optimize backend application performance to reduce connection duration, or implement rate limiting upstream if applicable to manage traffic volume.

issues.cause: Network issues (e.g., congestion, routing problems, firewall blocks, or ISP outages) in the upstream path are preventing client traffic from reaching the load balancer.
issues.title: Upstream Network Connectivity Issue to Load Balancer
issues.rootReason: Network infrastructure failure, misconfigured routing, excessive traffic volume on the upstream network segment, or security policy blocking traffic.
issues.impactSummary: Clients are unable to establish connections to services behind the load balancer, resulting in partial or complete service outage.
issues.recommendation: Examine upstream network device metrics (routers, firewalls, network interfaces), verify the network connectivity path from clients to the load balancer, and check for any recent network configuration changes.

ruleIntents.name: Backend Health Check Failure Detection
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is detecting health check failures from its {downstream} targets, indicating potential issues with the backend services or their network connectivity.

ruleIntents.name: Load Balancer Resource Utilization Exceeds Thresholds
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing high resource utilization (e.g., CPU, memory, active connection count), indicating it's operating near or exceeding its capacity.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Network Path Disruption
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: An issue in the {upstream} network segment is preventing traffic from reaching {component}, leading to service unavailability.

issues.cause: The NAT Gateway is experiencing an internal fault, resource exhaustion, or misconfiguration, preventing it from forwarding outbound traffic.
issues.title: NAT Gateway Outbound Connectivity Failure
issues.rootReason: NAT Gateway operational failure
issues.impactSummary: All instances in associated private subnets will lose outbound internet connectivity and access to other external services.
issues.recommendation: Inspect NAT Gateway logs and metrics for errors, resource limits, or configuration issues. Verify routing tables.

issues.cause: The upstream DNS discovery service is unavailable or unreachable, preventing the NAT Gateway from resolving external hostnames.
issues.title: Upstream DNS Resolution Failure for NAT Gateway
issues.rootReason: Upstream dependency unavailability
issues.impactSummary: The NAT Gateway will be unable to resolve domain names, leading to outbound connection failures for all services relying on it for external access.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and connectivity of the DNS discovery service. Ensure network paths are clear.

issues.cause: A downstream load balancer that the NAT Gateway is routing traffic to is unreachable or unhealthy.
issues.title: Downstream Load Balancer Unreachable from NAT Gateway
issues.rootReason: Downstream dependency unavailability
issues.impactSummary: Traffic intended for services behind the unreachable load balancer will fail, affecting client connections.
issues.recommendation: Check the health and configuration of the affected load balancer and ensure network connectivity from the NAT Gateway.

ruleIntents.name: OUTBOUND_TRAFFIC_INTERRUPTION
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing an outage, resulting in a complete interruption of outbound network traffic for dependent services.

ruleIntents.name: UPSTREAM_DNS_UNREACHABLE
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {upstream} DNS discovery service is unreachable or failing, preventing {component} from resolving external hostnames and impacting outbound connections.

ruleIntents.name: DOWNSTREAM_LB_UNREACHABLE
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {downstream} load balancer is unreachable from {component}, causing traffic intended for services behind it to fail.

issues.cause: The configured downstream 'load_balancer' component is unresponsive or consistently returning errors, preventing the API Gateway from forwarding requests.
issues.title: Downstream Load Balancer Unavailable
issues.rootReason: Failure of the load balancer itself, its dependent services, or network connectivity issues between the API Gateway and the load balancer.
issues.impactSummary: All requests routed through the API Gateway will fail to reach their intended services, leading to a complete service outage for affected paths.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health, resource utilization, and logs of the 'load_balancer' component. Verify network connectivity from the API Gateway to the load balancer.

issues.cause: The API Gateway is receiving an extremely high volume of requests from upstream callers, exceeding its configured rate limits or processing capacity.
issues.title: Excessive Inbound Traffic from Upstream Callers
issues.rootReason: Spike in legitimate user traffic, misconfigured upstream service, or a potential distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack.
issues.impactSummary: The API Gateway will start rejecting or throttling requests, leading to increased error rates and latency for upstream callers and potentially impacting downstream service health.
issues.recommendation: Review and adjust rate limiting policies, implement dynamic scaling for the API Gateway, or consider external DDoS protection measures. Communicate with upstream service owners.

ruleIntents.name: Circuit Breaker Engaged
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} circuit breaker has tripped, indicating a severe issue or unresponsiveness from its {downstream} load_balancer. This protects the {downstream} from further overload but will cause direct failures for {upstream} requests.

ruleIntents.name: Rate Limit Exceeded
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} has exceeded its configured rate limits, causing throttling of requests originating from {upstream}. This action protects {component} and its {downstream} from being overwhelmed.

ruleIntents.name: Excessive Retries to Downstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is performing an unusually high number of retries against its {downstream} load_balancer. This suggests intermittent failures or slow responses from {downstream}, which can increase load on {downstream} and latency for {upstream} requests.

issues.cause: The API Gateway has detected that requests from an upstream service are exceeding the configured rate limit.
issues.title: API Gateway Rate Limit Exceeded
issues.rootReason: Upstream service overloading the API Gateway, or misconfigured rate limiting policy.
issues.impactSummary: Upstream requests are being rejected, leading to service degradation for consumers. Downstream services are protected from overload.
issues.recommendation: Investigate upstream service behavior to reduce request volume or adjust API Gateway rate limit configuration if appropriate.

issues.cause: The API Gateway has exhausted all configured retries when attempting to forward requests to its downstream dependency.
issues.title: API Gateway Retries Exhausted
issues.rootReason: Persistent errors, high latency, or unresponsiveness from the downstream service.
issues.impactSummary: Requests destined for the downstream service are failing at the API Gateway after multiple retry attempts. This impacts upstream consumers.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance of the downstream service for potential issues.

ruleIntents.name: apiGatewayRateLimitEnforcement
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is enforcing rate limits, rejecting requests from {upstream}. This is protecting {downstream} from overload. This may indicate an issue with {upstream}'s request rate.

ruleIntents.name: apiGatewayRetryExhaustion
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is exhausting its configured retries when attempting to reach {downstream} due to persistent errors or unresponsiveness. This suggests a problem with {downstream} and impacts requests from {upstream}.
