issues.cause: Excessive write operations from upstream components or inefficient query patterns leading to resource contention.
issues.title: Database Write Latency
issues.rootReason: Inadequate provisioned storage IOPS, CPU/memory bottleneck for write operations, or unoptimized data schema/queries for high write volumes. Sustained high write pressure from upstream 'compute' services.
issues.impactSummary: Increases application response times for write operations, potentially leading to timeouts and degraded user experience. Can cause backpressure on upstream services, reducing overall system throughput.
issues.recommendation: Optimize write queries, scale database resources (CPU, memory, storage IOPS), implement write sharding or replication, or review data indexing strategies. Implement transaction batching if applicable. Distribute write load across multiple instances if possible.

issues.cause: High write volume on the primary database, network latency between primary and replica, or slow processing on the replica database.
issues.title: Database Replication Lag
issues.rootReason: Sustained high write throughput on the primary exceeds the 'database' replica's ability to keep up, network bandwidth bottlenecks, or insufficient compute/storage resources on the replica. This is particularly relevant for write_heavy databases.
issues.impactSummary: Downstream services reading from 'database' replicas may receive stale data, impacting data consistency. Can lead to data loss or integrity issues during failover scenarios, and may prevent successful read scaling.
issues.recommendation: Scale up replica resources, optimize network connectivity, implement read replicas for read scaling to offload the primary, or consider a different replication strategy better suited for high write loads (e.g., eventually consistent models if appropriate).

ruleIntents.name: Detect Database Write Latency due to Upstream Compute Load
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} database is experiencing high write latency due to a significant increase in write requests from {upstream} compute services, identified by the {source} metric 'local.write_latency_ms' exceeding its threshold. This directly impacts the ability of {upstream} to process requests efficiently.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Replication Lag Affecting Downstream Replicas due to Primary Writes
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} primary database is experiencing replication lag with its {downstream} replica(s), identified by the {source} metric 'local.replication_lag_seconds' exceeding its threshold. This is likely due to the write-heavy nature and sustained high write activity on the primary, preventing {downstream} replicas from staying current.

issues.cause: The DNS component is unable to successfully resolve the IP address(es) for its configured downstream load balancer. This can be caused by incorrect DNS records, network connectivity problems between the DNS server and the load balancer's authoritative DNS, or issues with the load balancer's registration in DNS.
issues.title: DNS Resolution Failure for Downstream Load Balancer
issues.rootReason: Incorrect DNS configuration, network partition affecting DNS resolution, or load balancer registration failure.
issues.impactSummary: Any service or client attempting to connect to the load balancer via this DNS resolution will fail, leading to service unavailability or degraded performance for affected applications.
issues.recommendation: Verify the A/AAAA records for the downstream load balancer in the DNS zone. Check network reachability and firewall rules between the DNS server and the authoritative DNS server for the load balancer's domain. Ensure the load balancer is correctly advertising its IP to DNS.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Load Balancer Resolution Failure Detection
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing failures resolving its downstream dependency, {downstream}, which is a load balancer. This prevents clients from connecting to the load balancer.

issues.cause: One or more backend services registered with the network load balancer are failing health checks or are otherwise unavailable.
issues.title: Downstream Backend Service Unhealthy
issues.rootReason: Application failure, instance crash, network partition between load balancer and backend, or misconfiguration of health checks.
issues.impactSummary: Reduced capacity and potential service degradation for clients due to inability to route traffic to healthy backend instances.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and logs of the affected backend services. Verify network connectivity, application status, and health check configurations. Scale out backends if capacity is the issue.

issues.cause: Clients attempting to connect to the network load balancer are unable to resolve its DNS name.
issues.title: Upstream Client DNS Resolution Failure for NLB
issues.rootReason: Incorrect or missing DNS records for the network load balancer, issues with the DNS server authoritative for the NLB's domain, or network connectivity issues preventing DNS queries from reaching the server.
issues.impactSummary: Upstream clients cannot establish connections to the service, leading to a complete outage for new requests.
issues.recommendation: Verify the DNS configuration for the network load balancer. Check the health and reachability of the DNS servers used by clients. Ensure A/AAAA records are correctly registered and propagated.

issues.cause: The network load balancer itself is experiencing an unrecoverable internal error or software fault.
issues.title: Network Load Balancer Internal System Failure
issues.rootReason: Underlying infrastructure platform issue, software bug within the load balancer's control plane or data plane, or critical resource exhaustion within the load balancer's operating environment.
issues.impactSummary: Complete disruption of traffic routing through the network load balancer, leading to an outage for all services behind it and unreachability for all clients.
issues.recommendation: Contact the cloud provider or platform support. If possible, initiate failover to a redundant load balancer or attempt to recreate the component if it's stateless. Monitor platform status pages for wider incidents.

ruleIntents.name: DownstreamBackendHealthCheckFailure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing degradation because its downstream backend service(s) are failing health checks or are otherwise unavailable, preventing effective traffic distribution.

ruleIntents.name: UpstreamClientCannotDiscoverNLB
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is unreachable by its {upstream} clients due to issues with DNS resolution of its endpoint, preventing new connections from being established.

ruleIntents.name: LocalNLBInternalSystemFailure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing a critical internal system failure, leading to a complete outage for both its {upstream} clients and its ability to route traffic to {downstream} services.

issues.cause: The downstream load balancer, which the API Gateway routes traffic to, is unavailable or reporting severe errors.
issues.title: API Gateway Downstream Service Unavailable
issues.rootReason: Dependency failure, likely a failure of the load balancer or the services it routes to.
issues.impactSummary: API Gateway cannot forward requests, leading to increased error rates and service unavailability for clients.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and availability of the downstream load balancer '{downstream}' and the services it manages.

issues.cause: The downstream load balancer or its backend services are responding slowly, increasing overall request latency.
issues.title: API Gateway Experiencing High Latency from Downstream
issues.rootReason: Performance degradation of a downstream dependency.
issues.impactSummary: Clients experience slow API responses, potentially leading to timeouts and degraded user experience.
issues.recommendation: Analyze the performance metrics of the downstream load balancer '{downstream}' and its connected services to identify bottlenecks.

issues.cause: One or more replicas of the API Gateway are unhealthy, stopped, or experiencing critical errors.
issues.title: API Gateway Replica Unhealthy or Failed
issues.rootReason: Self-component instance failure or resource exhaustion.
issues.impactSummary: Reduced capacity for the API Gateway, potentially leading to increased load on healthy replicas and overall performance degradation or availability issues.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the specific unhealthy replica of the API Gateway '{component}' for underlying issues such as resource limits, crashes, or configuration problems.

issues.cause: The API Gateway has automatically or manually initiated a failover to its standby or redundant target.
issues.title: API Gateway Failover Mechanism Activated
issues.rootReason: Primary component failure, detected unhealthiness, or planned maintenance event requiring state transition.
issues.impactSummary: Potential temporary disruption in traffic during failover, followed by routing to the failover target. Requires verification of traffic flow and system stability.
issues.recommendation: Verify the status of the primary API Gateway instance and the failover target. Investigate the root cause that triggered the failover for '{component}'.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Load Balancer Unavailable
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The API Gateway {component} cannot reach its downstream load balancer {downstream}, leading to unavailability for clients.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Load Balancer High Latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The API Gateway {component} is experiencing high latency due to slow responses from its downstream load balancer {downstream}.

ruleIntents.name: API Gateway Replica Health Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: One or more replicas of the API Gateway {component} are unhealthy, reducing its overall capacity.

ruleIntents.name: API Gateway Failover Triggered
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The API Gateway {component} has initiated a failover to its target, indicating a potential issue with the primary instance or a planned maintenance.

issues.cause: The underlying object store dependency is experiencing elevated latencies for read/write operations.
issues.title: High Latency from Downstream Object Store
issues.rootReason: Degradation in object store performance due to resource contention, network issues, or internal service problems.
issues.impactSummary: Increased query and write times for the data lake, leading to degraded performance for dependent services.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance metrics of the downstream object store. Optimize data access patterns from the data lake, or consider scaling the object store.

issues.cause: The underlying object store dependency is unreachable or reporting an outage.
issues.title: Downstream Object Store Unavailable
issues.rootReason: Outage of the object store service, network partitioning, or severe infrastructure failure.
issues.impactSummary: Complete loss of data access and storage capabilities for the data lake, halting all operations.
issues.recommendation: Verify the status and health of the downstream object store. Initiate recovery procedures for the object store, or switch to a failover region if available.

issues.cause: The underlying object store dependency has reached its maximum throughput capacity, leading to throttled requests.
issues.title: Downstream Object Store Throughput Exhausted
issues.rootReason: Sudden spike in data lake operations, insufficient provisioned throughput for the object store, or upstream system misbehavior.
issues.impactSummary: Reduced data ingestion and retrieval rates for the data lake, causing backlogs and delays for dependent systems.
issues.recommendation: Review data lake access patterns and optimize for fewer or larger I/O operations. Increase the provisioned throughput or scale the downstream object store.

assumptions: The data lake component primarily uses its configured downstream 'object_store' for all persistent storage operations.
The performance and availability of the data lake are directly dependent on the performance and availability of the downstream 'object_store'.

ruleIntents.name: Detect High Object Store Latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High read/write latency reported by {downstream} object store is impacting {component} data access times.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Object Store Unavailability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {downstream} object store is reporting unavailability, preventing {component} from performing storage operations.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Object Store Throughput Exhaustion
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {downstream} object store is experiencing throughput exhaustion, leading to throttled operations for {component}.

issues.cause: Sustained high write load, inefficient query patterns from upstream compute, or underlying storage I/O limitations.
issues.title: Database experiences high write latency or throughput bottlenecks
issues.rootReason: Overwhelmed write capacity.
issues.impactSummary: Degraded performance for all operations dependent on write completion, potential data consistency issues.
issues.recommendation: Optimize write queries, introduce write-ahead logging (WAL) optimizations, scale underlying storage I/O, or consider sharding/partitioning.

issues.cause: Scaling policy thresholds are too high, scaling takes too long, or maximum scale limits are reached.
issues.title: Database autoscale fails to keep up with rapid write load changes
issues.rootReason: Insufficient reactive scaling capacity.
issues.impactSummary: Brief periods of performance degradation or outages during rapid load spikes, potential for resource thrashing if thresholds are too sensitive.
issues.recommendation: Adjust scaling policy, review underlying infrastructure provisioning, or consider pre-warming strategies.

issues.cause: High write volume on the primary database, network latency between primary and replicas, or insufficient I/O capacity on replicas.
issues.title: Replication lag detected for downstream database replicas
issues.rootReason: Inability of replicas to keep pace with primary writes.
issues.impactSummary: Stale data reads from replicas, potential for data inconsistency if read operations are directed to replicas immediately after writes on primary.
issues.recommendation: Optimize primary write performance, improve network throughput, provision higher I/O capacity for replicas, or consider logical replication.

ruleIntents.name: Detect high write load impacting upstream compute
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing high write latency due to heavy write operations, potentially originating from {source}. This is causing performance degradation for {upstream} components reliant on timely database writes.

ruleIntents.name: Autoscale operation impacting performance
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is currently undergoing an autoscale event, or failed to scale quickly enough, resulting in temporary performance degradation. This affects {upstream} components by increasing query latencies.

ruleIntents.name: High write load causing replication lag on downstream databases
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing heavy write load, which is causing significant replication lag for its {downstream} database replicas. This means {downstream} components may be reading stale data.

issues.cause: The upstream 'load_balancer' component is failing or degraded, preventing the reverse proxy from receiving traffic.
issues.title: Upstream Load Balancer Unavailable
issues.rootReason: Inability to connect to or receive traffic from the designated load balancer responsible for routing requests to this reverse proxy.
issues.impactSummary: The reverse proxy will not receive new requests, leading to complete or partial unavailability of services relying on this proxy.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and configuration of the upstream load balancer component(s). Ensure network connectivity and correct routing policies.

issues.cause: One or more downstream 'compute' components are failing or degraded, impacting the reverse proxy's ability to forward requests.
issues.title: Downstream Compute Unavailable
issues.rootReason: Inability to establish connections or successfully forward requests to the intended downstream compute instances.
issues.impactSummary: Requests forwarded by the reverse proxy will fail or experience high latency, resulting in service degradation or unavailability for end-users.
issues.recommendation: Examine the health and operational status of the downstream compute component(s). Verify network access and service endpoints.

issues.cause: The reverse proxy is operating beyond its designed capacity, leading to resource exhaustion or request queueing.
issues.title: Reverse Proxy Overloaded
issues.rootReason: Sustained high request volume or insufficient resource allocation (CPU, memory, network I/O) for the current traffic load.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, elevated error rates, and potential connection drops for requests flowing through this reverse proxy, affecting downstream services.
issues.recommendation: Scale out the reverse proxy instances or increase resource allocations (CPU/memory) to handle the traffic. Investigate potential traffic spikes or denial-of-service attempts.

issues.cause: The reverse proxy is experiencing errors when attempting to connect to its upstream load balancer.
issues.title: Upstream Connection Failures
issues.rootReason: Network connectivity issues, firewall misconfigurations, incorrect upstream endpoint settings, or certificate validation failures.
issues.impactSummary: Requests from the upstream load balancer cannot reach the reverse proxy, leading to service disruption or complete unavailability.
issues.recommendation: Verify network routes, firewall rules, and security group configurations between the reverse proxy and its upstream load balancer. Check load balancer's listen ports and certificates.

issues.cause: The reverse proxy is encountering errors when establishing connections to downstream compute components.
issues.title: Downstream Connection Failures
issues.rootReason: Network connectivity interruptions, incorrect downstream service endpoints, or misconfigured security policies on compute instances.
issues.impactSummary: The reverse proxy is unable to forward requests to its target services, resulting in end-user request failures and service unavailability.
issues.recommendation: Check network routes, security groups, and firewall rules between the reverse proxy and its downstream compute services. Confirm downstream service availability and correct port configurations.

assumptions: The reverse proxy is configured to route traffic from an upstream load balancer to downstream compute services.
The reverse proxy is critical in the synchronous request path, meaning its failure directly impacts service availability.
Failures in upstream or downstream components can manifest as connectivity issues or increased error rates on the reverse proxy.

ruleIntents.name: UpstreamLoadBalancerFailurePropagation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: An availability issue detected on an {upstream} component (role: load_balancer) is preventing {component} from receiving requests, leading to service degradation.

ruleIntents.name: DownstreamComputeFailurePropagation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: An availability issue detected on a {downstream} component (role: compute) is causing {component} to return errors for requests destined for that downstream service.

ruleIntents.name: ReverseProxyOverloadImpactToDownstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: An overload condition on {component} is leading to increased latency and errors for requests being forwarded to {downstream} components, impacting their observed performance.

issues.cause: The upstream cache service, crucial for ingress operations, is experiencing unavailability, high error rates, or severe performance degradation.
issues.title: Upstream Cache Service Unavailable or Degraded
issues.rootReason: Common causes include network partition, cache service crash, resource exhaustion on cache instances, or critical misconfiguration of the cache service.
issues.impactSummary: Ingress functionality is severely impaired, leading to increased latency, errors, or complete service unavailability for requests that depend on cached data. This directly impacts user experience.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health, logs, and resource metrics of the upstream cache service instances. Verify network connectivity and access permissions from the ingress to the cache.

assumptions: The ingress component relies on the upstream 'cache' service for critical request processing or data retrieval to function correctly.
Failures or severe degradation in the upstream cache directly translate to degraded service or unavailability for requests handled by this ingress, impacting end-users.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Cache Unavailability Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {upstream} is experiencing critical issues, causing {component} to be unable to retrieve cached data, leading to failures and poor user experience.

issues.cause: Increased cache misses due to data invalidation, eviction, or requests for data not present in cache.
issues.title: Cache Miss Rate Spike
issues.rootReason: Inability of the cache to serve requests efficiently, leading to increased load on downstream systems and elevated latency.
issues.impactSummary: Elevated latency for read operations, increased load on downstream databases or services, potential performance degradation.
issues.recommendation: Investigate cache eviction policies, data access patterns, and potential upstream application changes causing new data requests. Consider increasing cache capacity or optimizing cache population strategies.

issues.cause: Cache memory or storage limits have been reached, preventing new data from being cached or forcing aggressive eviction of existing data.
issues.title: Cache Capacity Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: Insufficient resources allocated to the cache, or an unexpected surge in data volume or unique keys being stored.
issues.impactSummary: Increased cache miss rate, higher latency, and elevated load on backend systems due to forced evictions.
issues.recommendation: Increase cache size or provision additional cache instances. Review cache entry expiration and eviction policies. Identify and mitigate potential memory leaks or inefficient data storage.

issues.cause: Cache entries are not being invalidated or updated promptly after changes in the source of truth, leading to users receiving outdated information.
issues.title: Stale Data in Cache
issues.rootReason: Ineffective cache invalidation strategy, delayed updates from source systems, or misconfigured Time-To-Live (TTL) values.
issues.impactSummary: Data inconsistencies, incorrect application behavior, and potential user dissatisfaction.
issues.recommendation: Implement a more robust cache invalidation mechanism (e.g., write-through, write-around, or publish/subscribe for invalidations). Adjust TTLs or consider event-driven cache updates. Ensure consistency requirements are met.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Cache Miss Rate Spike
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The cache {component} is experiencing a spike in miss rate, likely due to a change in data access patterns from upstream {upstream} services or an inability to serve requests efficiently. This is causing increased load and latency on downstream systems like {downstream} as they are directly queried more often.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Cache Capacity Exhaustion
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The cache {component} is experiencing capacity exhaustion. This directly leads to more aggressive eviction policies or failed write operations, resulting in a spike in cache misses and increased load on downstream systems like {downstream}.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Stale Data in Cache
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The cache {component} is serving stale data to upstream {upstream} services. This could be due to issues with the cache invalidation mechanism or delayed updates from the true source of data, potentially impacting the accuracy of applications relying on {component}.

issues.cause: The load balancer's high fanout (fanout_high) trait means a single incoming request generates multiple downstream requests. If downstream services (compute) are slow or experience increased latency, this effect is amplified across the fanout, and the synchronous request path (is_sync_request_path) means the upstream must wait for all responses.
issues.title: High Fanout and Synchronous Path Amplifying Downstream Latency
issues.rootReason: Cumulative latency from multiple downstream dependencies exacerbated by fanout and synchronous request patterns.
issues.impactSummary: Significant increase in end-to-end request latency, potential timeouts, and degraded user experience, impacting the upstream (api_gateway).
issues.recommendation: Optimize downstream service performance, consider reducing fanout if possible, implement aggressive timeouts, or explore asynchronous communication patterns where feasible. Ensure downstream autoscaling (autoscale) is responsive enough.

issues.cause: The load balancer's 'has_rate_limiting' trait is actively dropping or delaying requests because the incoming traffic from upstream (api_gateway) exceeds configured limits.
issues.title: Load Balancer Rate Limiting Activating / Dropping Requests
issues.rootReason: Excessive incoming traffic volume from upstream, or misconfigured/insufficient rate limiting capacity.
issues.impactSummary: Requests from upstream (api_gateway) are being dropped or delayed, leading to elevated error rates (e.g., 429 Too Many Requests) and reduced service availability for affected clients.
issues.recommendation: Review upstream traffic patterns and rate limit configurations. Consider increasing rate limit thresholds if capacity allows, implementing client-side retries with backoff, or applying traffic shaping upstream. Verify 'autoscale' is effectively scaling the load balancer itself if it's hitting its own capacity limits before rate limits.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Latency Amplification via Fanout
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing high latency because its `fanout_high` trait amplifies slow responses from its downstream services (e.g., {downstream}). Due to `is_sync_request_path`, this latency directly impacts {upstream} services.

ruleIntents.name: Rate Limit Exceeded
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is rejecting or delaying requests from {upstream} due to its `has_rate_limiting` trait being triggered by excessive incoming traffic.

issues.cause: The API Gateway's circuit breaker opened due to repeated failures from a downstream service.
issues.title: Network API Gateway Circuit Breaker Tripped
issues.rootReason: Repeated failures from a downstream dependency, causing the gateway to protect the system by preventing further requests to the failing service.
issues.impactSummary: Requests to affected downstream services will be rejected by the API Gateway, resulting in increased error rates for clients and reduced availability of those services.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance of the downstream services connected to the API Gateway. Address the root cause of the downstream failures. Consider adjusting circuit breaker thresholds if necessary.

issues.cause: The API Gateway's configured rate limits for incoming requests were exceeded.
issues.title: Network API Gateway Rate Limit Exceeded
issues.rootReason: High volume of incoming requests surpassing the defined thresholds, or misconfigured rate limits.
issues.impactSummary: Requests exceeding the rate limit will be rejected by the API Gateway, leading to increased error rates for clients.
issues.recommendation: Analyze request patterns and adjust rate limit configurations if the current limits are too restrictive for expected traffic. Implement client-side retry strategies with backoff. Investigate potential traffic spikes or DDoS attempts.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Circuit Breaker Open
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} detected repeated failures from its {downstream} dependencies and tripped its circuit breaker, preventing further requests from reaching them.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Rate Limit Exceeded
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} rejected requests due to exceeding configured rate limits, preventing them from reaching its {downstream} dependencies.

issues.cause: The upstream load balancer is failing or severely degraded, preventing requests from reaching the worker component.
issues.title: Upstream Load Balancer Failure
issues.rootReason: External dependency failure or misconfiguration.
issues.impactSummary: Significant reduction or complete loss of inbound traffic to the worker, leading to idle resources or starvation.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and configuration of the upstream load balancer. Check its logs, metrics, and routing rules.

issues.cause: The downstream cache service is unavailable or experiencing severe performance degradation.
issues.title: Downstream Cache Failure
issues.rootReason: Cache service outage, network partition, or resource exhaustion.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, error rates, and potentially higher load on the database due to cache misses. Could lead to cascading failures.
issues.recommendation: Examine the health and capacity of the downstream cache. Implement retry mechanisms, circuit breakers, or fallback strategies. Consider increasing cache capacity or optimizing queries.

issues.cause: The downstream database service is unavailable, experiencing high latency, or returning errors.
issues.title: Downstream Database Failure
issues.rootReason: Database server crash, deadlock, resource contention, or network issues.
issues.impactSummary: Critical impact on request processing, leading to high error rates and timeouts. This is typically a primary data persistence component, making its failure catastrophic.
issues.recommendation: Investigate database logs, metrics (CPU, memory, I/O, connections), and query performance. Ensure proper connection pooling, retries, and circuit breakers are in place. Consider read replicas or sharding.

assumptions: Standard retry and timeout mechanisms are in place for downstream dependencies.
Upstream load balancer is responsible for distributing traffic to this worker component.
Cache is used for accelerating data access, and database is for primary data persistence.
Failures in critical downstream dependencies will directly manifest as increased latency or errors in the worker component.

issues.cause: One or more upstream services are unavailable or unhealthy, preventing the load balancer from routing traffic.
issues.title: Load Balancer Upstream Unavailability
issues.rootReason: Failure of dependent upstream {upstream} components, making them unresponsive or failing health checks.
issues.impactSummary: Traffic cannot be routed to healthy upstream services, leading to client request failures or timeouts for {downstream} users.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and availability of upstream compute components. Scale up, repair, or restart failed instances.

issues.cause: The {component} is experiencing a higher volume of requests or connections than it can handle.
issues.title: Load Balancer Capacity Exceeded
issues.rootReason: Insufficient resources for the load balancer to handle current demand from {downstream} clients, potentially exacerbated by fanout_high trait.
issues.impactSummary: Client requests from {downstream} users may be dropped, rejected, or experience high latency due to the load balancer's inability to process them.
issues.recommendation: Scale out or scale up the load balancer instances, or adjust its configuration parameters (e.g., connection limits, request queues) to handle more traffic.

issues.cause: Upstream services are responding slowly, causing increased end-to-end latency through the load balancer.
issues.title: Load Balancer Upstream Latency
issues.rootReason: Performance degradation of dependent upstream {upstream} components, even if they are available.
issues.impactSummary: Client requests from {downstream} users experience increased latency, potentially leading to timeouts or a poor user experience.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the performance of upstream compute components. Identify bottlenecks, optimize their response times, or scale them to meet demand.

issues.cause: The authService is unable to process authentication requests due to internal errors or misconfiguration, leading to failed authentication attempts for upstream components.
issues.title: Upstream Authentication Requests Failing
issues.rootReason: Internal service error or dependency failure preventing successful authentication processing.
issues.impactSummary: All upstream components requiring authentication will be unable to function, resulting in service unavailability or degraded experience for end-users.
issues.recommendation: Investigate authService logs for errors. Check connectivity to any backend identity stores. Verify configuration for authentication protocols.

issues.cause: The authService is experiencing high processing times for authentication requests, potentially due to resource saturation, inefficient query execution, or slow responses from downstream dependencies.
issues.title: High Latency in Authentication Responses
issues.rootReason: Resource contention, inefficient processing, or slow downstream dependency response.
issues.impactSummary: Upstream components will experience delays when authenticating, leading to slow user login times, timeouts, or a generally sluggish application experience.
issues.recommendation: Monitor authService resource utilization (CPU, memory). Optimize authentication logic or queries. Scale out the authService or its downstream dependencies if load is the primary factor.

issues.cause: The downstream load balancer is overwhelmed by traffic or connection requests from the authService, exceeding its capacity to distribute load effectively.
issues.title: Downstream Load Balancer Saturated
issues.rootReason: Excessive outbound traffic from authService or under-provisioned load balancer capacity.
issues.impactSummary: Connections to downstream services from authService may be dropped or delayed, affecting the availability or performance of authentication flows that depend on those services.
issues.recommendation: Increase capacity of the downstream load balancer. Review authService outbound connection patterns. Implement rate limiting on authService if traffic is excessive.

ruleIntents.name: Auth failure propagates upstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {source} component is experiencing authentication failures because the {component} authService is unable to process requests, causing all dependent {upstream} components to fail their authentication checks.

ruleIntents.name: Auth latency propagates upstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {source} component is experiencing high request latency because the {component} authService is slow to respond, leading to degraded performance for dependent {upstream} components.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream LB saturation affects auth service
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {downstream} load balancer is saturated, causing delays and failures for {component} authService when it attempts to connect to its own downstream services. This may impact authentication flows requiring external data.

issues.cause: Excessive write operations or insufficient I/O capacity.
issues.title: High Database Write Latency
issues.rootReason: Inadequate database provisioning for sustained write loads, or inefficient query patterns causing I/O contention.
issues.impactSummary: Degraded performance for upstream services dependent on write operations, potentially leading to timeouts or data inconsistency.
issues.recommendation: Optimize write queries, increase I/O provisioned capacity, or scale out write capacity via sharding/replication.

issues.cause: Too many concurrent connections from upstream services, exceeding database limits.
issues.title: Database Connection Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: Misconfiguration of connection limits, connection leaks in upstream applications, or an unexpected surge in demand.
issues.impactSummary: Upstream services will fail to establish new connections to the database, leading to service unavailability or errors.
issues.recommendation: Increase database connection limits, implement connection pooling in upstream services, or optimize application connection management.

issues.cause: High write volume on the primary database, network latency, or insufficient I/O capacity on replicas.
issues.title: Database Replication Lag
issues.rootReason: Replicas cannot keep up with the write rate of the primary, leading to stale data on read replicas.
issues.impactSummary: Downstream services reading from replicas may receive stale data, affecting consistency and potentially application logic.
issues.recommendation: Optimize replica hardware, improve network connectivity, or consider sharding/distributed database solutions to reduce primary write load.

ruleIntents.name: WriteLatencyImpact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} database is experiencing high write latency, causing {source} to observe slower data persistence.

ruleIntents.name: ConnectionExhaustionImpact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} database has exhausted its available connections, preventing {source} from establishing new database sessions.

ruleIntents.name: ReplicationLagImpact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} database is experiencing replication lag, causing {downstream} to read stale data from its replicas.

issues.cause: One or more downstream services (compute or other load balancers) are unhealthy, unresponsive, or experiencing high latency/errors, causing the load balancer to remove them from rotation or route traffic to failing services.
issues.title: Downstream Service Failure or Degradation
issues.rootReason: Underlying issues within a target 'compute' instance or a connected 'load_balancer' pool, such as resource exhaustion, application errors, or network partitioning.
issues.impactSummary: Requests routed through the load balancer may experience increased latency, higher error rates, or be dropped entirely when attempting to reach degraded downstream services. This can lead to a cascading failure to upstream services like 'api_gateway'.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance of downstream 'compute' and 'load_balancer' components. Check logs, metrics, and health checks for the affected targets. Adjust load balancing algorithms or health check thresholds if necessary.

issues.cause: The load balancer is receiving a volume of requests from its upstream 'api_gateway' that exceeds its capacity to process or distribute, leading to resource saturation.
issues.title: Upstream Overload Causing Load Balancer Saturation
issues.rootReason: Spike in incoming traffic, misconfigured rate limits on upstream components, insufficient load balancer capacity, or inefficient load balancing algorithms under peak load.
issues.impactSummary: The load balancer may drop requests, exhibit high latency, or become unresponsive, directly impacting the 'api_gateway' and subsequent user requests. This can propagate back to the 'api_gateway' as increased error rates.
issues.recommendation: Examine traffic patterns from 'api_gateway'. Scale up the load balancer's capacity, implement throttling or rate limiting on the 'api_gateway', or refine the load balancing strategy to better handle high loads. Check for DDoS attacks.

issues.cause: An internal software or hardware fault within the load balancer component itself, leading to a loss of functionality.
issues.title: Load Balancer Internal System Failure
issues.rootReason: Software bug, configuration error, resource exhaustion (CPU, memory, network), or infrastructure failure affecting the load balancer instance(s).
issues.impactSummary: All traffic attempting to pass through the affected load balancer will fail or be severely degraded, directly impacting both upstream 'api_gateway' and downstream 'compute'/'load_balancer' services.
issues.recommendation: Review system logs and metrics for the load balancer. Restart or replace the failed instance(s). Verify configuration and ensure adequate resource allocation. Consider high-availability configurations.

ruleIntents.name: downstreamServiceDegradation
ruleIntents.impactShape: This load balancer will experience degradation or failure if its downstream dependencies degrade or fail. Given 'is_sync_request_path', this directly impacts user experience.
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing degradation due to a dependency issue with its downstream services, specifically {downstream}. This is preventing {component} from effectively distributing traffic and returning responses to {upstream}.

ruleIntents.name: upstreamOverload
ruleIntents.impactShape: The load balancer becomes overloaded due to excessive traffic from its upstream dependencies, leading to its own resource saturation and potential failure.
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing overload and potential saturation due to excessive request volume from its upstream service, {upstream}.

ruleIntents.name: loadBalancerFailure
ruleIntents.impactShape: The load balancer itself fails, causing complete unavailability or severe degradation for all traffic it manages.
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} has encountered an internal failure, rendering it unable to process or route traffic effectively. This immediately impacts {upstream} and traffic intended for {downstream}.

issues.cause: The subnet has lost its network connectivity, preventing traffic flow.
issues.title: Subnet Experiences Connectivity Loss
issues.rootReason: Underlying infrastructure failure, misconfiguration, or network partitioning.
issues.impactSummary: All downstream components, including API Gateways, will lose access to services routed through this subnet. Applications and APIs will become unreachable.
issues.recommendation: Verify network interfaces, routing tables, and underlying network infrastructure for the subnet. Check for security group or NACL misconfigurations.

issues.cause: The subnet has run out of available IP addresses, preventing new instances or services from being deployed or existing ones from scaling.
issues.title: Subnet IP Address Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: Insufficient CIDR block size, misconfigured auto-scaling, or uncontrolled resource provisioning.
issues.impactSummary: New deployments, scaling events, or even recovery operations for dependent components like API Gateways that require IP addresses within this subnet will fail. This can lead to service degradation or outage for dependent services.
issues.recommendation: Expand the subnet's CIDR block if possible, review resource provisioning, and ensure proper cleanup of terminated instances. Consider adding new subnets.

ruleIntents.name: subnet_downstream_api_gateway_connectivity_impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing a loss of network connectivity, preventing {downstream} (an API Gateway) from routing traffic. This will manifest as unavailability for services reliant on {downstream}.

ruleIntents.name: subnet_downstream_api_gateway_deployment_impact_ip_exhaustion
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Deployment or scaling operations for {downstream} (an API Gateway) are failing because {component} has run out of available IP addresses. This prevents new instances of {downstream} from coming online.

issues.cause: A downstream load balancer is reporting unhealthy status or high error rates, preventing proper request routing.
issues.title: Downstream Load Balancer Unhealthy
issues.rootReason: Insufficient capacity in the load balancing layer, misconfiguration of load balancer rules, or failure of backend targets managed by the load balancer.
issues.impactSummary: Requests routed through the affected load balancer will experience increased latency or failures, leading to degraded API Gateway performance for those specific routes.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and configuration of the downstream load balancer components. Verify backend target group health. Scale up or reconfigure as necessary.

issues.cause: A downstream traffic service (e.g., microservice, application) is reporting unhealthy status, high error rates, or high latency.
issues.title: Downstream Traffic Service Unhealthy
issues.rootReason: Application bug, resource exhaustion (CPU, memory, database connections) within the downstream service, or failure of an internal dependency of the downstream service.
issues.impactSummary: Requests targeting the unhealthy downstream service will fail or experience significant delays, directly impacting the API Gateway's ability to serve those requests to end-users.
issues.recommendation: Examine the logs and metrics of the specific downstream traffic service to identify the root cause of its unhealthiness. Consider scaling the service, optimizing its performance, or investigating its internal dependencies.

ruleIntents.name: downstream_load_balancer_unhealthy
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing degraded performance due to an unhealthy downstream load balancer {downstream}. Requests are either failing or experiencing high latency.

ruleIntents.name: downstream_traffic_service_unhealthy
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is encountering errors due to an unhealthy downstream traffic service {downstream}. This may manifest as higher error rates or timeouts.

issues.cause: The load balancer downstream of the API Gateway is experiencing high latency.
issues.title: Downstream Load Balancer Latency Impacting API Gateway
issues.rootReason: Performance bottleneck or resource exhaustion within the downstream load balancer or the services it routes traffic to.
issues.impactSummary: Requests processed by the API Gateway will experience increased response times, potentially leading to client timeouts and a degraded user experience.

issues.cause: The API Gateway cannot establish a connection with its downstream load balancer.
issues.title: Downstream Load Balancer Unreachable
issues.rootReason: Network connectivity issues, load balancer service failure, or incorrect network configuration.
issues.impactSummary: All requests routed through the affected load balancer will fail, leading to significant service disruption and unavailability for clients accessing the API Gateway.
issues.recommendation: Verify network paths, firewall rules, and the operational status of the downstream load balancer. Ensure correct IP addresses and port configurations.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Load Balancer Performance Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing increased latency and errors due to performance degradation in its {downstream} Load Balancer.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Load Balancer Unreachable
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is unable to reach its {downstream} Load Balancer, causing requests to fail.

issues.cause: All downstream services registered with the load balancer are reporting as unhealthy, or no targets are registered, preventing traffic routing.
issues.title: Load Balancer has no healthy downstream targets
issues.rootReason: Failure of backend services, misconfiguration of health checks, or unintended scaling down of service instances.
issues.impactSummary: Complete service outage or severe degradation for all traffic attempting to pass through this load balancer due to its inability to forward requests.
issues.recommendation: Verify the health and availability of registered downstream services. Ensure service instances are running, responsive to health checks, and correctly configured for the load balancer.

issues.cause: The incoming request rate from upstream components significantly exceeds the processing capacity of the load balancer.
issues.title: Load Balancer is experiencing excessive upstream traffic
issues.rootReason: An unexpected traffic surge, misconfiguration in upstream rate limiting, or insufficient scaling of the load balancer itself.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, request timeouts, and potential unavailability for clients due to dropped connections and resource exhaustion on the load balancer.
issues.recommendation: Implement or adjust rate limiting rules on upstream components or the load balancer. Scale out load balancer instances to handle the increased load, or investigate the source of the traffic surge.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate Downstream Unhealthiness to Upstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} reports a critical issue because its downstream services are unavailable or unhealthy. This directly impacts the {upstream} components, causing a service outage.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate Load Balancer Overload to Upstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is overloaded by excessive traffic from {upstream}, leading to degraded performance and increased errors for {upstream} components.

issues.cause: One or more downstream load balancer targets are unresponsive or returning errors.
issues.title: Downstream Load Balancer Unavailable
issues.rootReason: Loss of connectivity, overload, or failure of a dependent load balancing service.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, error rates, or complete unavailability of services routed through this load balancer due to downstream path issues.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and connectivity of downstream load balancer targets. Ensure proper configuration of routing rules and health checks.

issues.cause: The designated failover target load balancer is either unresponsive, returning errors, or has reached its capacity limit, preventing successful traffic re-routing.
issues.title: Failover Load Balancer Target Inoperable or Exhausted
issues.rootReason: Failure of the failover target, misconfiguration of failover rules, or insufficient capacity in the failover path.
issues.impactSummary: Traffic intended for failover will be dropped or experience service degradation, potentially leading to cascading failures or complete service unavailability when primary targets are down.
issues.recommendation: Verify the health and capacity of the failover load balancer target. Adjust failover policies or provision additional capacity if necessary.

ruleIntents.name: downstream_load_balancer_failure_propagation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is unable to route traffic due to a failure in its downstream load balancer target, {downstream}. This impacts service availability for all requests routed through {component}.

ruleIntents.name: failover_target_inoperable_detection
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component}'s failover mechanism has detected that its failover target, {failover}, is inoperable or exhausted. This means {component} cannot successfully re-route traffic when primary targets fail, severely impacting {component}'s resilience.

issues.cause: One or more downstream services (e.g., load balancer, queue) are unresponsive or in an unhealthy state, preventing the API Gateway from forwarding requests.
issues.title: Downstream Service Unresponsive
issues.rootReason: Failure in a critical downstream dependency, leading to a complete halt in traffic processing capability.
issues.impactSummary: The API Gateway is unable to process incoming API requests, resulting in elevated error rates (e.g., 5xx status codes) for clients and potential service disruption.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and operational status of all downstream 'load_balancer' and 'queue' components. Check network connectivity, service discovery, and resource availability for these dependencies.

issues.cause: Increased response times or processing delays from one or more downstream services (e.g., load balancer, queue) are impacting the API Gateway's performance.
issues.title: Downstream Service Latency
issues.rootReason: Congestion, resource saturation, or performance bottlenecks within downstream dependencies, leading to delayed request processing.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end request latency for API clients, potentially leading to client timeouts, degraded user experience, and a buildup of requests within the API Gateway.
issues.recommendation: Monitor performance metrics of downstream 'load_balancer' and 'queue' components for signs of congestion, resource exhaustion, or increased processing times. Implement client-side timeouts and consider scaling downstream resources.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Service Unavailability Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing critical unavailability because its critical {downstream} services are unresponsive, preventing any API requests from being forwarded.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Service Latency Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing increased latency and potential request queuing due to slow response times from its {downstream} dependencies.

issues.cause: The connected database component is experiencing high latency, errors, or is unavailable.
issues.title: Upstream Database Dependency Impaired
issues.rootReason: Database resource exhaustion, network issues, or internal database failures within the upstream database.
issues.impactSummary: Application server transactions will fail or experience significant delays, leading to poor user experience and potential service unavailability.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance of the upstream database components. Ensure proper scaling, indexing, and connection pooling. Implement fault tolerance patterns like circuit breakers or retries with exponential backoff.

issues.cause: Upstream compute components (e.g., other appServers, microservices) are overloaded or under-provisioned, leading to a bottleneck in request processing before reaching this app server.
issues.title: Insufficient Upstream Compute Capacity
issues.rootReason: Upstream service cannot handle current request volume, leading to queuing, throttling, or rejection of requests destined for this appServer.
issues.impactSummary: The app server may appear underutilized or perform poorly due to a lack of incoming healthy requests, leading to perceived service degradation.
issues.recommendation: Scale up or optimize the performance of the upstream compute components. Implement rate limiting or backpressure mechanisms on the upstream components to manage traffic flow.

ruleIntents.name: DetectUpstreamDatabaseImpairment
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing high error rates and latency because its upstream database dependency, {upstream}, is impaired.

ruleIntents.name: DetectUpstreamComputeBottleneck
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing a reduced volume of healthy requests because its upstream compute dependency, {upstream}, is experiencing a capacity bottleneck.

issues.cause: The application server cannot connect to or receive responses from its upstream cache service.
issues.title: Upstream Cache Unresponsive or Unavailable
issues.rootReason: The upstream cache component is unhealthy, overloaded, or experiencing network connectivity issues.
issues.impactSummary: Increased request latency and/or error rates for operations that rely on cached data, potentially leading to service degradation or outage.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance metrics of the upstream cache service (e.g., latency, error rates, resource utilization). Check network connectivity between the app server and cache.

issues.cause: The application server is unable to send requests to or receive timely responses from its downstream compute service.
issues.title: Downstream Compute Unresponsive or Unavailable
issues.rootReason: The downstream compute component is unhealthy, overloaded, or experiencing network connectivity issues.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency and error rates for operations that delegate tasks to downstream compute. This can cause partial or full service degradation.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance metrics of the downstream compute service. Check network connectivity and resource utilization on compute instances.

issues.cause: The application server cannot send notifications or receive confirmations from its downstream notification service.
issues.title: Downstream Notification Unresponsive or Unavailable
issues.rootReason: The downstream notification component is unhealthy, overloaded, or experiencing network connectivity issues.
issues.impactSummary: Notifications fail to send or are significantly delayed. Core application functionality might continue, but user experience for notification-dependent features will be impacted.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance metrics of the downstream notification service. Check network connectivity and queue backlogs.

issues.cause: The autoscaling mechanism failed to correctly provision or de-provision instances based on demand, resulting in a mismatch between capacity and load.
issues.title: Autoscaling Event Failure
issues.rootReason: Misconfiguration of autoscaling policies, cloud provider resource limits reached, or issues with instance launch templates.
issues.impactSummary: Performance degradation (e.g., increased latency, high error rates) if under-provisioned, or increased operational costs if over-provisioned unnecessarily.
issues.recommendation: Review autoscaling group logs and configurations. Verify cloud provider service limits and ensure sufficient resources are available for scaling events. Validate instance launch templates.

ruleIntents.name: impact_from_upstream_cache_degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing increased latency and errors because its upstream cache service ({upstream}) is degraded.

ruleIntents.name: propagate_to_downstream_compute
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing increased latency and errors, which is propagating to its downstream compute service ({downstream}).

ruleIntents.name: propagate_to_downstream_notification
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing increased latency and errors for notification-related operations, which is propagating to its downstream notification service ({downstream}).

ruleIntents.name: capacity_impact_from_autoscale_failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing capacity limitations due to an autoscaling failure, leading to potential performance issues under load.

issues.cause: Excessive concurrent connections from upstream {upstream} services, exceeding {component}'s configured connection limits or processing capacity.
issues.title: Connection Exhaustion Due to High Fan-in
issues.rootReason: Inadequate connection capacity or resource allocation for {component} to handle the concurrent client connections from high fan-in {upstream} workloads.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, connection refused errors for {upstream} services, leading to degraded application performance and potential service unavailability.
issues.recommendation: Implement connection pooling on {upstream} services, scale {component} horizontally or vertically, or introduce connection limiters/load balancers to manage fan-in.

issues.cause: High volume of read requests from {upstream} services, exceeding {component}'s processing or I/O capacity for reads.
issues.title: Read Performance Degradation Under High Read Load
issues.rootReason: Insufficient read throughput capacity on {component}, memory pressure, or inefficient data access patterns under sustained high read QPS from {upstream}.
issues.impactSummary: Elevated read latency, potential for stale data if data consistency models are relaxed, and increased error rates for {upstream} applications reliant on {component}.
issues.recommendation: Introduce read replicas, optimize data serialization/deserialization, scale {component} horizontally or vertically, or implement client-side caching/rate limiting.

ruleIntents.name: High Fan-in Connection Overload Detection
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High fan-in from {upstream} to {component} is causing connection exhaustion, leading to increased latency and potential connection refusal errors.

ruleIntents.name: Read-Heavy Workload Performance Degradation Detection
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High read traffic from {upstream} is causing {component} to experience performance degradation, leading to elevated read latency and potential data staleness.

issues.cause: Increased latency from a downstream cache dependency.
issues.title: Downstream Cache Latency Impacting Custom Service
issues.rootReason: The cache component is experiencing performance degradation or high load, leading to slower response times for requests made by this custom service.
issues.impactSummary: Elevated response times for this custom service, potentially causing a ripple effect and degraded experience for upstream users.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance metrics of the downstream cache. Consider optimizing data access patterns, implementing client-side caching, or increasing the capacity of the cache.

issues.cause: The incoming request rate from upstream users has surpassed the operational capacity of this custom service.
issues.title: Upstream User Request Rate Exceeds Custom Service Capacity
issues.rootReason: A sudden surge in upstream user traffic or a reduction in the processing capacity of this custom service has led to an inability to handle the current load.
issues.impactSummary: Increased error rates, elevated latency, and potential request drops for this custom service, directly impacting upstream user experience.
issues.recommendation: Implement or adjust rate limiting mechanisms. Scale out instances of this custom service to accommodate increased load. Investigate the source of the upstream traffic spike.

issues.cause: Errors originating within the custom service itself, independent of external dependencies.
issues.title: Custom Service Experiencing Internal Errors
issues.rootReason: Software bugs, resource exhaustion (e.g., memory, CPU), or misconfiguration within the custom service are causing operational failures.
issues.impactSummary: Decreased availability and increased error rates for this custom service, directly affecting upstream users and potentially leading to incorrect states downstream.
issues.recommendation: Review recent code deployments, analyze logs and error traces, check resource utilization metrics, and consider rolling back to a previous stable version or deploying a fix.

ruleIntents.name: DetectCacheLatencyImpact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {downstream} cache is experiencing high latency, directly increasing the response time of {component} and potentially affecting {upstream} users.

ruleIntents.name: DetectUpstreamRateExceedance
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {upstream} user component is sending requests at a rate that exceeds the processing capacity of {component}, leading to resource saturation and potential errors.

ruleIntents.name: DetectServiceInternalError
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing internal errors, leading to degraded availability and increased error rates for {upstream} users.

issues.cause: A downstream service of type 'user' that {component} depends on is unavailable or unhealthy.
issues.title: Downstream user service unavailability impacts {component}
issues.rootReason: Dependency failure downstream.
issues.impactSummary: {component} experiences increased latency, error rates, or service interruptions due to its inability to reach critical downstream 'user' services.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and availability of the specific downstream 'user' service. Review its logs and metrics for errors, resource exhaustion, or misconfiguration.

issues.cause: The configured failover target for {component} is not reachable or is unhealthy, preventing successful failover.
issues.title: Failover target for {component} is unavailable
issues.rootReason: Failover mechanism failure or target unavailability.
issues.impactSummary: {component} cannot successfully fail over to its designated target, leading to prolonged outage or impaired service during primary system failures.
issues.recommendation: Verify the health and availability of the failover target. Ensure network connectivity and proper configuration for failover routing.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream User Service Unavailability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: A dependency on a downstream 'user' service for {component} is failing. The {downstream} component is showing signs of unavailability, causing {component} to experience errors.

ruleIntents.name: Failover Target Unavailability Detection
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The failover target for {component} is unavailable, preventing the system from switching to a backup. The {failover} component is currently unhealthy, which means {component} cannot rely on its redundancy mechanism.

issues.cause: The replica is experiencing issues keeping its state synchronized with its upstream source.
issues.title: Replication Health Degraded
issues.rootReason: Insufficient resources, network latency, or upstream source issues preventing timely replication.
issues.impactSummary: Data staleness or inconsistency may lead to incorrect application behavior or serve stale data to downstream consumers.
issues.recommendation: Investigate network connectivity to the upstream source, check resource utilization on the replica, and review logs for replication errors.

issues.cause: The upstream component, from which this replica sources data or state, is unreachable or performing poorly.
issues.title: Upstream Source Unavailable or Degraded
issues.rootReason: Network partition, upstream component failure, or excessive latency in data transfer from upstream.
issues.impactSummary: Replication will halt or slow significantly, leading to increased data staleness and potential data loss if the replica cannot re-sync.
issues.recommendation: Verify the health and connectivity of the upstream source component. Check network paths and latency.

issues.cause: The replica component is experiencing high load, resource exhaustion, or internal processing delays, leading to slow responses for downstream consumers.
issues.title: Replica Responding Slowly to Downstream Requests
issues.rootReason: Increased request volume, CPU/memory saturation, I/O bottlenecks, or inefficient query/processing logic.
issues.impactSummary: Downstream components relying on this replica will experience increased latency, potentially leading to cascading slowdowns or timeouts.
issues.recommendation: Review replica resource utilization, optimize query patterns, scale replica capacity, or investigate upstream sources of increased load.

issues.cause: The replica's ability to take over as primary is degraded due to internal inconsistencies or lack of synchronization.
issues.title: Failover Readiness Compromised
issues.rootReason: Replication lag, configuration errors, or resource constraints preventing the replica from maintaining a healthy and ready state for failover.
issues.impactSummary: In the event of a primary failure, this replica may not be able to seamlessly take over, leading to extended downtime or data loss.
issues.recommendation: Address underlying replication issues, verify failover configurations, and ensure adequate resources are allocated to the replica.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Replica Lag
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing replication lag, leading to potential data staleness from its {upstream} source.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Source Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} cannot replicate from its {upstream} source due to issues with the upstream component, potentially leading to data inconsistencies.

ruleIntents.name: Replica Performance Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is responding slowly, causing {downstream} components to experience increased latency.

ruleIntents.name: Failover Readiness Check
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component}'s failover readiness is compromised due to internal state issues, potentially leaving the system vulnerable if the primary fails.

issues.cause: The cache is experiencing resource exhaustion (e.g., connections, memory, CPU) due to a high number of concurrent requests from downstream components with a 'worker' role, reflecting its 'fanout_high' trait.
issues.title: Cache Capacity Exhaustion due to High Fanout
issues.rootReason: Insufficient cache resources or misconfigured connection limits to handle the aggregate demand from a large fanout of downstream 'worker' components, leading to an inability to serve all incoming requests.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, connection errors, and potential service unavailability for downstream 'worker' components due to the cache's inability to accept or process new requests.
issues.recommendation: Increase cache resources (CPU, memory, connection limits) or implement connection pooling/rate limiting on downstream 'worker' components. Consider sharding the cache to distribute load.

issues.cause: The cache is experiencing performance degradation (e.g., high latency, low throughput) under sustained high 'read_heavy' load from downstream 'worker' components, potentially due to CPU saturation, memory contention, or network bandwidth limits.
issues.title: Read Performance Degradation Under High Load
issues.rootReason: The cache's resources are insufficient to meet the demands of the 'read_heavy' workload from 'worker' components, leading to increased processing time per read request.
issues.impactSummary: Elevated read latencies for downstream 'worker' components, potentially causing cascading timeouts and application slowdowns as data retrieval becomes slower.
issues.recommendation: Optimize cache configuration for read operations, scale cache resources (CPU, network), or investigate query patterns from downstream 'worker' components for efficiency improvements. Implement read replicas if supported.

issues.cause: The worker's upstream cache dependency is unavailable or significantly degraded, leading to increased load and latency.
issues.title: Upstream Cache Dependency Failure
issues.rootReason: Failure or performance degradation of a critical upstream cache component.
issues.impactSummary: This worker will experience increased processing latency, potentially higher error rates, and increased resource consumption due to repeated computations or data fetches that would normally be served by the cache.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance of the upstream cache service. Consider increasing cache capacity, optimizing cache hit ratio, or implementing a fallback mechanism.

issues.cause: The asynchronous processing queue of the worker is growing excessively, indicating that the worker cannot process incoming tasks at the required rate.
issues.title: Asynchronous Processing Backlog
issues.rootReason: Insufficient processing capacity, slow downstream dependencies, or a sudden surge in upstream requests.
issues.impactSummary: Tasks processed by this worker will experience significantly delayed completion times, and the worker may eventually run out of memory or other resources if the backlog continues to grow.
issues.recommendation: Scale out the worker's processing capacity, optimize task processing logic, or investigate upstream sources for unusual request patterns. Consider rate limiting upstream or implementing backpressure.

issues.cause: The upstream worker dependency is congested or applying backpressure, limiting the rate at which it can provide work to this worker.
issues.title: Upstream Worker Backpressure or Congestion
issues.rootReason: Performance bottleneck, resource exhaustion, or intentional backpressure mechanisms in an upstream worker service.
issues.impactSummary: This worker will experience reduced incoming request rates and potentially underutilization, leading to decreased overall system throughput for tasks dependent on this worker.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the upstream worker service for performance bottlenecks or capacity issues. Consider scaling the upstream worker or improving its efficiency. Evaluate if the current worker is configured to handle intermittent upstream slowdowns gracefully.

ruleIntents.name: Asynchronous Path Backlog
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing a significant backlog in its asynchronous processing queue, leading to increased overall processing time and potential resource exhaustion.

issues.cause: The upstream cache component is experiencing high request latency, delaying responses to this worker.
issues.title: Upstream Cache Latency Impacting Worker
issues.rootReason: Slow processing or contention at the upstream cache, or network latency.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end latency for requests processed by this worker, potentially leading to timeouts or cascading failures for its downstream dependencies.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the upstream cache's performance metrics (CPU, memory, IO, queue depth) and logs. Consider scaling the cache or optimizing cache operations and queries.

issues.cause: This worker is sending excessive load or malformed requests to its downstream worker dependencies.
issues.title: Downstream Worker Performance Degradation Caused by This Worker
issues.rootReason: Increased request volume, inefficient processing logic, or data format incompatibility originating from this worker.
issues.impactSummary: Degraded performance or increased error rates for downstream workers, potentially causing them to become overloaded or fail, and affecting overall system throughput.
issues.recommendation: Review request patterns from this worker to downstream. Implement rate limiting, batching, or optimize request serialization. Check logs for errors reported by downstream components.

issues.cause: The worker component is consuming excessive CPU, memory, or other local resources.
issues.title: Worker Local Resource Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: Inefficient code, memory leaks, high incoming request volume, or insufficient allocated resources for the current workload.
issues.impactSummary: This worker will experience high latency, increased error rates, and potentially become unresponsive, directly affecting its upstream callers and its ability to process requests for downstream dependencies.
issues.recommendation: Profile the worker application to identify resource bottlenecks. Optimize code, increase allocated resources (CPU/memory), or implement auto-scaling policies to handle load spikes.

issues.cause: The configured failover target for this worker component cannot be reached or is reporting as unhealthy.
issues.title: Failover Target for Worker is Unavailable
issues.rootReason: Failover target is down, network connectivity issues, or misconfiguration of the failover path.
issues.impactSummary: Loss of resilience and increased risk of service interruption in case of primary worker failure, as automated failover may not occur, leading to extended downtime.
issues.recommendation: Verify the health and reachability of the failover target. Check network connectivity and failover configuration settings. Ensure the target itself is operational and correctly provisioned.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Cache Latency impacting Worker
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Upstream {source} (a cache) is experiencing high latency, causing {component} (a worker) to slow down its processing of requests.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Worker Performance Degradation Caused by This Worker

issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, elevated error rates (e.g., HTTP 5xx Service Unavailable), and potential service downtime for the application server.
issues.recommendation: Implement rate limiting, scale up application server instances, or optimize application code for higher throughput. Review autoscaling policies and thresholds.
