issues.cause: Excessive write operations or contention within the database, leading to slow processing of write requests.
issues.title: Database write latency impacting API Gateway performance
issues.rootReason: Insufficient database write capacity, suboptimal indexing, or unoptimized queries for write-heavy workloads.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency for API requests processed by downstream API Gateways, potentially leading to timeouts or poor user experience.
issues.recommendation: Optimize database schema and queries for write operations, scale database resources (e.g., CPU, IOPS), implement write caching, or consider sharding/partitioning for horizontal scaling.

issues.cause: The primary database is experiencing write loads exceeding its capacity, leading to delays in replicating data to secondary databases.
issues.title: Database write throughput saturation affecting downstream database replication
issues.rootReason: Inadequate write capacity for the primary database, leading to bottlenecks and replication queues building up.
issues.impactSummary: Potential for data staleness, inconsistency, or failures in downstream databases that rely on replication, affecting read performance or data integrity.
issues.recommendation: Increase write capacity of the primary database, optimize write-intensive processes, implement advanced replication strategies (e.g., logical replication), or distribute write load across multiple primaries if architecture supports it.

ruleIntents.name: High write latency impact on API Gateway
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High write latency on {component} is impacting the response times for {downstream} due to increased database operation times.

ruleIntents.name: Database write throughput saturation impacting downstream replication
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Write throughput saturation on {component} is causing replication lag or data inconsistency with downstream {downstream} databases.

issues.cause: The health check monitor component itself is experiencing an internal failure, resource exhaustion, or misconfiguration.
issues.title: Health Check Monitor Internal Failure
issues.rootReason: Internal component malfunction, resource constraint, or misconfiguration preventing proper operation of the monitor.
issues.impactSummary: Unable to accurately assess the health of monitored upstream components, potentially leading to incorrect service routing or delayed detection of upstream issues.
issues.recommendation: Investigate health check monitor logs for errors. Check resource utilization (CPU, memory) on the host where the monitor is running. Verify its configuration settings.

issues.cause: The health check monitor has detected that one or more of its upstream 'compute' components are unhealthy.
issues.title: Upstream Compute Component Unhealthy
issues.rootReason: The monitored upstream component is not responding to health checks, indicating a service outage, overload, network isolation, or application-level failure.
issues.impactSummary: Service degradation or outage for functionalities dependent on the unhealthy upstream compute component. Traffic may be misrouted or delayed.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the status of the affected upstream compute components. Check their logs, resource usage, network connectivity, and application health endpoints.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Health Check Monitor Internal Failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing internal failures, preventing it from accurately monitoring upstream services.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Compute Health Check Failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} has detected that an {upstream} component it monitors is unhealthy. This indicates a potential issue with the {upstream} service itself.

issues.cause: Slow response times from connected downstream 'compute' services.
issues.title: Application Server Experiencing High Latency Due to Downstream Dependency
issues.rootReason: Database query bottlenecks, slow external API calls, or resource contention in downstream services.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end request latency, potential timeouts, and degraded user experience.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the performance of downstream 'compute' components. Optimize queries, improve caching, or scale downstream resources.

issues.cause: One or more critical downstream 'compute' services are unavailable or failing requests.
issues.title: Application Server Impacted by Downstream Dependency Failure
issues.rootReason: Outage of a database, cache, or another critical microservice dependency.
issues.impactSummary: Service unavailability, failed requests, and errors propagated to upstream services and end-users.
issues.recommendation: Identify and restore the failing downstream 'compute' components. Implement retry mechanisms, circuit breakers, or graceful degradation.

issues.cause: The upstream 'load_balancer' is misconfigured, has unhealthy member checks failing, or is distributing traffic unevenly.
issues.title: Application Server Receiving Unbalanced or Unhealthy Traffic
issues.rootReason: Load balancer health check misconfiguration, internal load balancer issues, or network partition affecting specific app servers.
issues.impactSummary: Overloading specific instances, leading to performance degradation, increased error rates, or even instance failures.
issues.recommendation: Review load balancer configuration, health check settings, and ensure even distribution of traffic. Investigate network connectivity.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Latency to App Server
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing high latency due to slow responses from its downstream {downstream} dependency.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Failure to App Server
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is failing requests because its critical downstream {downstream} dependency is unavailable or failing.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Load Balancer Unhealthy Traffic
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing increased load or errors due to an upstream {upstream} sending unbalanced or unhealthy traffic.

issues.cause: The API Gateway is experiencing increased response times due to latency originating from its dependent compute services.
issues.title: Elevated Downstream Compute Latency Affecting API Gateway
issues.rootReason: Slow processing or contention within downstream compute components.
issues.impactSummary: Requests through the API Gateway will experience higher latency, potentially leading to client-side timeouts and degraded user experience for synchronous calls.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the performance bottlenecks within the downstream compute services. Consider scaling out compute, optimizing application logic, or adjusting API Gateway timeouts.

issues.cause: API Gateway is unable to route requests successfully as a significant portion of its configured downstream compute services are unavailable or returning errors.
issues.title: Downstream Compute Services Unavailable
issues.rootReason: Failure or unhealthiness of one or more downstream compute instances or services.
issues.impactSummary: API Gateway will return error responses (e.g., 5xx) for requests, leading to a direct loss of service for affected endpoints.
issues.recommendation: Inspect the health and operational status of the downstream compute services. Restore or replace failed instances, and ensure sufficient capacity and redundancy.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Compute Latency Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing elevated latency due to slow responses from its {downstream} compute services.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Compute Unavailability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is unable to process requests due to a significant portion of its {downstream} compute services being unavailable or unhealthy.

issues.cause: Incorrectly configured WAF rules lead to false positives, blocking valid requests destined for the downstream API Gateway.
issues.title: WAF Misconfiguration Blocking Legitimate Traffic
issues.rootReason: Application changes, rule updates, or environment shifts cause WAF rules to become overzealous, mistakenly identifying legitimate traffic as malicious.
issues.impactSummary: Legitimate user requests are denied, leading to service disruption for valid users and potential revenue loss for services relying on the API Gateway.
issues.recommendation: Review WAF logs for blocked legitimate traffic, refine existing rules, or add new allow rules based on application requirements. Consider a staged rollout for WAF rule changes.

issues.cause: Complex or inefficient WAF rules, high traffic volume, or resource constraints on the WAF instance are degrading performance.
issues.title: WAF Introducing Performance Overhead
issues.rootReason: Insufficient WAF capacity, unoptimized rule sets, or unexpected traffic spikes overwhelm the WAF processing capabilities, increasing latency.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency for all traffic passing through the WAF, potentially leading to timeouts, degraded user experience, and reduced overall throughput to the downstream API Gateway.
issues.recommendation: Optimize WAF rule sets for efficiency, ensure sufficient WAF instance resources (CPU, memory), consider scaling out WAF instances, or offload simpler rules to upstream components if possible.

issues.cause: WAF rules are not comprehensive enough, misconfigured, or an attacker has found a bypass for existing protections.
issues.title: WAF Failed to Block Malicious Traffic
issues.rootReason: Outdated rule sets, missing attack signatures, or zero-day exploits circumventing existing WAF protections allow malicious traffic to pass through.
issues.impactSummary: Malicious requests successfully reach the downstream API Gateway, potentially leading to service compromise, data breaches, or denial of service for the API Gateway and its backend services.
issues.recommendation: Update WAF rulesets regularly, review and refine custom rules based on threat intelligence, apply security patches, and analyze WAF bypass attempts. Consider integrating advanced threat intelligence feeds.

ruleIntents.name: Block Malicious Downstream Requests
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is configured to block malicious requests before they reach the {downstream} API Gateway. A failure in this intent means {source} malicious traffic reaches {downstream}.

ruleIntents.name: Allow Legitimate Downstream Requests
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is configured to allow legitimate requests to pass through to the {downstream} API Gateway. A failure in this intent means {source} legitimate traffic is blocked by {component}.

issues.cause: One or more critical external dependencies connected to the load balancer are experiencing failures or unresponsiveness, or are returning a high rate of errors.
issues.title: Downstream External Dependency Failure
issues.rootReason: Failure of an underlying service instance or infrastructure component that the load balancer relies on to distribute traffic.
issues.impactSummary: Increased error rates, degraded performance, or complete outage for clients attempting to reach services via this load balancer.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and availability of the affected external dependencies. Ensure proper health checks are configured and functioning on the load balancer and that these dependencies are adequately provisioned.

issues.cause: One or more critical external dependencies connected to the load balancer are experiencing elevated response times, leading to processing delays.
issues.title: Downstream External Dependency Latency Spike
issues.rootReason: Performance bottlenecks, resource contention, inefficient processing, or overload within an underlying service instance.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end latency for clients, potentially leading to timeouts, degraded user experience, or cascading failures in synchronous call paths.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the performance metrics of the affected external dependencies. Optimize their resource usage, scale up instances, or investigate code-level inefficiencies.

issues.cause: The load balancer itself is reporting internal health check failures, indicating a problem with its own operational status.
issues.title: Load Balancer Internal Health Check Failure
issues.rootReason: Internal component failure, resource exhaustion (CPU, memory, network), misconfiguration, or software bugs within the load balancer instance.
issues.impactSummary: Potential for requests to be misrouted, dropped, or for the load balancer to become entirely unavailable, leading to upstream client impact and service disruption.
issues.recommendation: Review load balancer configuration, resource utilization, and internal logs for specific error messages. Consider restarting the load balancer instance if it's stateless, or scaling out if it's under load.

assumptions: The load balancer is actively monitoring the health of its downstream external dependencies.
The load balancer is configured to automatically reroute traffic away from unhealthy downstream instances.
The component operates within a synchronous request-response model, allowing direct propagation of downstream latency and errors to upstream.
Upstream 'client' implies that direct user-facing impact is highly probable for issues propagated upstream.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate Downstream External Dependency Latency to Upstream Client
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Elevated latency detected in {downstream} external dependency causes {component} to experience increased processing times, directly impacting the response times observed by {upstream} clients.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate Widespread Downstream External Dependency Failure to Upstream Clients
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Widespread unresponsiveness or critical failures detected in {downstream} external dependencies lead to {component} returning errors or timeouts to {upstream} clients.

ruleIntents.name: Traffic Rerouting from Unhealthy Downstream External Dependency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Upon detecting an unhealthy state in a {downstream} external dependency instance, {component} proactively removes it from the traffic rotation, safeguarding {upstream} client experience from direct service failures by sending traffic to healthy instances instead.

issues.cause: The connected AI model service is experiencing performance degradation, errors, or is completely unavailable, impacting the LLM Gateway's ability to process requests.
issues.title: Downstream AI Model Degradation or Failure
issues.rootReason: Unhealthy or overloaded AI model instance(s), network issues, or internal model errors within the AI service.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, error rates, and potential unavailability for requests routed through the LLM Gateway, directly affecting {upstream} services.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance metrics of the downstream AI model service. Scale up or restart unhealthy instances. Check network connectivity and model logs.

issues.cause: Excessive request volume or complexity from upstream services (such as AI applications or load balancers) is overwhelming the LLM Gateway's resources.
issues.title: LLM Gateway Overloaded by Upstream Requests
issues.rootReason: Sudden spike in user demand, misconfigured upstream load balancer routing, or insufficient capacity of the LLM Gateway itself.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, timeouts, and error rates for requests reaching the LLM Gateway, potentially causing cascading failures to {upstream} services.
issues.recommendation: Implement rate limiting at the upstream service or gateway level. Scale out LLM Gateway instances. Optimize gateway processing logic if possible.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Downstream AI Model Unavailability or Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {downstream} AI model is reporting high error rates or latency, directly impacting the {component} LLM Gateway's ability to serve requests originating from {upstream}.

ruleIntents.name: Detect LLM Gateway Resource Exhaustion from Upstream Load
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} LLM Gateway is experiencing high resource utilization (e.g., CPU, memory, concurrent requests), likely due to an increased load from {upstream} services, leading to degraded performance for all incoming requests.

issues.cause: Loss of network connectivity to the application server or failure of an upstream network component.
issues.title: Application Server Unreachable
issues.rootReason: Inability of upstream components (e.g., load balancer, API gateway) to route traffic to the application server.
issues.impactSummary: The application server will not receive incoming requests, leading to service degradation or outage for end-users.
issues.recommendation: Investigate network path, health of upstream load balancers/proxies, and component network interfaces. Ensure proper routing and firewall rules.

issues.cause: The downstream cache service is unavailable or experiencing errors.
issues.title: Application Server Cache Dependency Failure
issues.rootReason: Loss of caching capabilities, leading to increased load on the database and potentially degraded performance or failure of the application server.
issues.impactSummary: Application server performance may degrade, response times may increase, and the database may become overloaded due to missing cache hits.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and connectivity of the cache service. Consider adding retry mechanisms or circuit breakers to the application.

issues.cause: The downstream database service is unavailable, unresponsive, or experiencing critical errors.
issues.title: Application Server Database Dependency Failure
issues.rootReason: Inability to perform data persistence or retrieval operations essential for application functionality.
issues.impactSummary: The application server will fail to serve requests requiring database access, leading to critical application errors or a complete outage.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health, connectivity, and performance of the database service. Ensure proper connection pooling and error handling in the application.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Network Failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} cannot receive requests because its upstream {source} ({upstream}) is experiencing a failure or connectivity issue.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Cache Failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing degraded performance because its downstream dependency {source} ({downstream}) (cache) is unavailable or failing.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Database Failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} has failed because its critical downstream dependency {source} ({downstream}) (database) is unavailable or failing.

issues.cause: The DNS server is not responding to queries or is completely offline.
issues.title: DNS Server Unreachable or Down
issues.rootReason: Network connectivity issues, server process crash, or hardware failure preventing the DNS server from operating.
issues.impactSummary: Clients depending on this DNS server will experience name resolution failures, leading to service disruption for applications that require DNS. However, if a failover target is configured, the impact may be mitigated by redirection.
issues.recommendation: Verify network connectivity to the DNS server. Check server health, process status, and logs. Consider failover to a redundant DNS server if configured and available.

issues.cause: The DNS server is operational but cannot resolve DNS queries, potentially due to configuration errors, corrupt zones, or internal resource exhaustion.
issues.title: DNS Server Failing to Resolve Names
issues.rootReason: Misconfiguration of DNS zones, resource limits reached on the server, or internal software defects.
issues.impactSummary: Clients may experience intermittent or consistent name resolution failures, impacting application functionality that relies on DNS resolution.
issues.recommendation: Review DNS server configuration, check zone files for errors, monitor resource utilization (CPU, memory, disk I/O), and inspect server logs for specific resolution errors.

issues.cause: The DNS server is responding slowly to queries, possibly due to high load, network congestion, or underlying infrastructure performance issues.
issues.title: High Latency in DNS Server Responses
issues.rootReason: Overload of DNS server resources (CPU, network), slow upstream DNS resolvers, or general network congestion between clients and the server.
issues.impactSummary: Applications and users will experience delays in name resolution, which can cascade into application performance degradation and timeouts.
issues.recommendation: Monitor DNS server load and resource usage. Optimize DNS queries or consider adding more DNS server capacity. Investigate network path for congestion.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream dns_discovery clients impacted by DNS server unreachability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: When {component} is unreachable, downstream components performing dns_discovery will fail to resolve names. While this component is unavailable, client requests may be redirected to a configured failover target, potentially leading to degraded resolution performance during the transition.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream dns_discovery clients impacted by DNS resolution failures
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: If {component} is unable to resolve names, downstream components performing dns_discovery will experience resolution errors, leading to degraded service.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream dns_discovery clients impacted by DNS server latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Increased response latency from {component} will cause delays for downstream components performing dns_discovery, potentially leading to timeouts and degraded application performance.

issues.cause: One or more downstream 'compute' services are experiencing high latency or errors.
issues.title: Downstream Service Degradation Impacting Load Balancer
issues.rootReason: Unhealthy or overloaded backend 'compute' services.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency and error rates for requests passing through the load balancer, potentially leading to upstream timeouts.
issues.recommendation: Investigate downstream 'compute' services for resource exhaustion, misconfiguration, or deployment issues. Scale up or repair unhealthy instances.

issues.cause: Incoming request rate from upstream 'api_gateway' services has exceeded the configured rate limit of the load balancer.
issues.title: Load Balancer Rate Limiting Activated
issues.rootReason: Excessive traffic volume from upstream 'api_gateway' or misconfigured rate limit threshold on {component}.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are being throttled and rejected by the load balancer, leading to an increased error rate for upstream clients.
issues.recommendation: Review traffic patterns from upstream 'api_gateway' services. Adjust the rate limit configuration on the {component} if appropriate, or coordinate with upstream teams to reduce traffic.

issues.cause: Frequent failures or timeouts observed when connecting to downstream 'compute' services have caused the load balancer's circuit breaker to trip and open.
issues.title: Load Balancer Circuit Breaker Open
issues.rootReason: Persistent unhealthiness or unresponsiveness of backend services, causing repeated failures.
issues.impactSummary: Traffic to affected downstream services is completely blocked, leading to immediate failures for requests routed to those services.
issues.recommendation: Investigate and resolve issues with the unhealthy downstream 'compute' services. Once services recover, the circuit breaker will typically close automatically or can be manually reset.

ruleIntents.name: Detect downstream compute service degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} load balancer is experiencing degraded performance due to issues in its {downstream} compute services.

ruleIntents.name: Detect load balancer rate limiting activation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} load balancer has activated its rate limiting mechanism, affecting requests from {upstream} 'api_gateway'.

ruleIntents.name: Detect load balancer circuit breaker open state
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} load balancer's circuit breaker has opened due to repeated failures with {downstream} services, blocking traffic.

issues.cause: Excessive failures or high latency from downstream components caused the circuit breaker to open, protecting the downstream service but blocking traffic.
issues.title: Load Balancer Circuit Breaker Open
issues.rootReason: Sustained high error rates, timeouts, or unavailability from a dependency.
issues.impactSummary: Requests to affected downstream services will be immediately rejected or failed at the load balancer, resulting in increased error rates for upstream callers.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance of downstream components. Consider scaling downstream services or adjusting circuit breaker thresholds if appropriate.

issues.cause: Incoming request rate from upstream components exceeded configured limits, leading to rejected requests.
issues.title: Load Balancer Rate Limit Exceeded
issues.rootReason: Unexpected traffic surge, misconfigured rate limits, or denial-of-service attempt.
issues.impactSummary: Requests exceeding the configured rate limit will be rejected, leading to increased error rates for upstream callers and potential service unavailability for some clients.
issues.recommendation: Analyze traffic patterns from upstream components. Adjust rate limit configurations or scale backend services to handle increased load, or implement client-side backoff/retry mechanisms.

issues.cause: Health checks indicate that one or more downstream 'compute' instances are unhealthy or unreachable, preventing the load balancer from forwarding requests effectively.
issues.title: Load Balancer Downstream Service Unhealthy
issues.rootReason: Backend service instance failure, network partition, resource exhaustion, or misconfigured health checks.
issues.impactSummary: Requests will fail to reach healthy downstream 'compute' instances, resulting in increased error rates and potential service unavailability for upstream components.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and operational status of 'compute' instances. Ensure proper networking and health check configurations. Scale backend services if capacity is insufficient.

ruleIntents.name: CircuitBreakerOpenImpactsUpstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The circuit breaker in {component} opened due to failures from its downstream services, leading to requests from {upstream} being rejected.

ruleIntents.name: RateLimitExceededImpactsUpstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The load balancer {component} is rejecting requests from {upstream} due to hitting its configured rate limits.

ruleIntents.name: DownstreamUnhealthyImpactsUpstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is unable to forward requests to {downstream} due to unhealthiness, causing failures for {upstream} requests.

ruleIntents.name: DownstreamFailureTriggersCircuitBreaker
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Sustained high error rates or latency from {downstream} services caused the circuit breaker in {component} to trip.

issues.cause: The CDN's origin server is unreachable or unresponsive, preventing content fetching/refreshing.
issues.title: CDN Origin Server Unreachable
issues.rootReason: Loss of connectivity or failure of the upstream origin server(s).
issues.impactSummary: Clients may experience stale or unavailable content as the CDN cannot refresh its cache or fetch new data from the origin.
issues.recommendation: Investigate connectivity to the CDN's origin servers. Verify origin server health and availability. Check network routes and firewall rules.

issues.cause: The CDN is experiencing an unusually high volume of requests, exceeding its capacity.
issues.title: CDN Request Volume Exceeded
issues.rootReason: Sudden surge in legitimate user traffic, distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack, or misconfigured client-side caching resulting in cache misses.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, elevated error rates, or complete unavailability for clients attempting to access content through the CDN.
issues.recommendation: Scale CDN capacity, investigate traffic sources for anomalies (e.g., DDoS), optimize content delivery for cache hits, or implement rate limiting.

ruleIntents.name: Origin Server Unreachable
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing degraded performance because it cannot reach its origin server to fetch content for its {downstream} consumers.

ruleIntents.name: CDN Overload
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is unable to handle the current volume of requests from its {downstream} consumers, leading to service degradation.

issues.cause: Misconfigured WAF rules, resource exhaustion, or network congestion affecting WAF processing time.
issues.title: WAF introducing high latency
issues.rootReason: WAF processing overhead impacting request response times.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end request latency, potentially leading to timeouts at downstream services, especially the load balancer.
issues.recommendation: Optimize WAF rule sets, scale WAF resources, or inspect network path for congestion. Monitor WAF-specific metrics like request processing time and queue depth.

issues.cause: Overly aggressive WAF rules or incorrect configuration leading to valid requests being identified as malicious.
issues.title: WAF blocking legitimate traffic (false positives)
issues.rootReason: Incorrect WAF rule tuning.
issues.impactSummary: Service unavailability for legitimate users, perceived as an outage or degraded experience by clients. Can cause downstream load balancer to report healthy but inaccessible targets.
issues.recommendation: Review WAF logs for blocked legitimate traffic, refine rule sets, or implement an allow-listing strategy for known safe patterns. Consider a 'learning mode' for new rules.

issues.cause: WAF component crash, resource exhaustion, or critical error causing it to fail open (bypassing security) or fail closed (blocking all traffic).
issues.title: WAF component failure impacting traffic flow
issues.rootReason: Critical software or hardware failure within the WAF.
issues.impactSummary: Complete loss of service (fail closed) or exposure to security threats (fail open), directly impacting downstream load balancer's ability to serve traffic.
issues.recommendation: Investigate WAF system logs, check resource utilization (CPU, memory, network), and ensure high-availability configurations are properly active. Implement health checks at the load balancer level.

ruleIntents.name: WAF Latency Impact on Downstream Health Checks
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is introducing significant latency, causing {downstream} to register increased response times and potentially mark {component} or its subsequent services as unhealthy.

ruleIntents.name: WAF False Positives Causing Downstream Errors
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is generating false positives, blocking legitimate requests and causing {downstream} to report an increased error rate or unavailable backend services.

ruleIntents.name: WAF Failure Mode Impacting Downstream Availability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} has experienced a critical failure (e.g., failed closed), leading to a significant drop in traffic reaching {downstream}, which reports degraded availability.

issues.cause: High traffic load from upstream components exceeding fabric capacity.
issues.title: Memory Fabric Congestion
issues.rootReason: Insufficient fabric bandwidth, bursty upstream demand, or misconfigured QoS.
issues.impactSummary: Increased memory access latency, degraded AI model performance, potential for job timeouts or stalls.
issues.recommendation: Scale memory fabric capacity, optimize data access patterns from upstream, implement QoS, or offload some AI workloads.

issues.cause: Internal fabric contention, resource contention, or issues with interconnected memory modules.
issues.title: Memory Fabric Latency Spike
issues.rootReason: Transient fabric misbehavior, sudden increase in internal cross-bar contention, or temporary unavailability of downstream memory banks.
issues.impactSummary: Reduced AI inference/training speed, potential for real-time AI system failures, increased resource consumption due to retries.
issues.recommendation: Investigate fabric internal state, check health of connected memory banks, analyze traffic patterns for sudden bursts.

issues.cause: Critical failure of memory fabric components, power loss, or complete software malfunction.
issues.title: Memory Fabric Unavailable
issues.rootReason: Hardware failure, severe software bug, or unrecoverable configuration error.
issues.impactSummary: Complete loss of memory access for connected components, leading to system-wide AI workload failure.
issues.recommendation: Check hardware status, review fabric logs, restart or replace the faulty memory fabric component.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Traffic Overload
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Congestion on {component} is likely caused by excessive data requests from {upstream} components, leading to a bottleneck in memory access.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Memory Latency Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Elevated memory access latency on {component} is being reported, potentially due to performance degradation or contention within its {downstream} memory banks.

ruleIntents.name: Fabric Failure Cascade
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} has become unavailable, directly impacting all {upstream} components relying on its memory access services and preventing {downstream} memory banks from being utilized.

issues.cause: The autoscaling mechanism for the {component} failed to provision additional resources when demand increased, leading to resource exhaustion.
issues.title: Autoscaling Failed
issues.rootReason: Misconfiguration of autoscaling policies, resource quota limits, or underlying infrastructure issues preventing scale-out operations.
issues.impactSummary: Performance degradation due to insufficient resources, potentially leading to service outages or unresponsive behavior.
issues.recommendation: Review autoscaling group configurations, monitor resource quotas, and check logs for autoscaling service errors. Manually scale out if necessary.

issues.cause: The automatic failover process for the {component} failed to successfully transfer operations to a redundant target when the primary component became unhealthy.
issues.title: Failover Mechanism Failure
issues.rootReason: Misconfiguration of failover policies, health check failures on the failover target, network partitioning, or issues with the failover orchestration service.
issues.impactSummary: Extended service outage for the primary component, as automatic recovery is hindered, requiring manual intervention.
issues.recommendation: Verify failover configurations, ensure health checks are correctly configured and monitored on primary and failover targets. Manually initiate failover if safe and appropriate.

issues.cause: The {component} is unable to perform its functions because an essential upstream service is unresponsive or experiencing an outage.
issues.title: Upstream Service Unresponsive
issues.rootReason: Outage or severe degradation of an upstream compute component, network connectivity issues to upstream, or misconfiguration of upstream service endpoints.
issues.impactSummary: Degraded performance or full outage of the {component} due to lack of required input or services from upstream dependencies.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and connectivity of the upstream service ({upstream}). Resolve issues at the source or configure retries/circuit breakers.

issues.cause: The {component} is experiencing issues processing requests because an essential downstream service it depends on is unresponsive or experiencing an outage.
issues.title: Downstream Service Unresponsive
issues.rootReason: Outage or severe degradation of a downstream compute component, network connectivity issues to downstream, or misconfiguration of downstream service endpoints.
issues.impactSummary: Requests handled by the {component} may fail, queue up, or experience high latency, leading to degraded performance or service unavailability.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and connectivity of the downstream service ({downstream}). Resolve issues at the source or implement retry logic and error handling.

ruleIntents.name: Detect autoscaling failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing degraded performance because its autoscaling mechanism failed to provision additional resources, leading to resource exhaustion.

ruleIntents.name: Detect failover mechanism failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing an outage because its failover mechanism failed to transfer operations to a healthy target.

ruleIntents.name: Detect upstream service unresponsiveness
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is impacted because an upstream service ({upstream}) is unresponsive or unhealthy, preventing {component} from receiving necessary inputs.

ruleIntents.name: Detect downstream service unresponsiveness
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is impacted because a downstream service ({downstream}) is unresponsive or unhealthy, preventing {component} from completing requests.
