issues.cause: Slowness or unavailability of dependent upstream compute components.
issues.title: Upstream Compute Component Degradation
issues.rootReason: Inadequate capacity, misconfiguration, or failure in upstream services preventing {component} from receiving requests or data efficiently.
issues.impactSummary: Reduced throughput and increased latency for {component} due to insufficient or delayed requests from upstream compute services. May lead to idle capacity or resource waste if {component} is over-provisioned for the reduced upstream load.
issues.recommendation: Investigate health and performance metrics of upstream compute services. Optimize upstream service configurations or scale up resources. Implement robust retry mechanisms and circuit breakers in {component} for upstream calls.

issues.cause: Slowness or unavailability of dependent downstream cache components.
issues.title: Downstream Cache Component Degradation
issues.rootReason: Cache service failures, network issues to cache, or cache capacity exhaustion, forcing {component} to perform more expensive operations like database lookups.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency for {component} operations that rely on cached data. Can lead to increased load on {component} and other downstream services (e.g., databases) if cache misses become frequent. May also lead to reduced availability if cache failures are critical.
issues.recommendation: Investigate health and performance metrics of downstream cache services. Optimize cache configurations or scale up resources. Implement robust fallback mechanisms, such as temporary in-memory caches or direct database access with appropriate rate limits, in {component} for cache failures.

issues.cause: Increased upstream demand combined with a failure in the autoscaling mechanism to provision additional {component} instances.
issues.title: Autoscaling Failure Under Increased Upstream Load
issues.rootReason: Misconfiguration of autoscaling policies, resource constraints (e.g., lack of available compute instances in the pool), or issues with the autoscaling service itself, preventing {component} from scaling out in response to rising load from upstream {upstream} components.
issues.impactSummary: Service degradation for {component} due to resource exhaustion (e.g., high CPU, memory, or connection saturation), leading to increased latency, errors, and potential outages. This impacts upstream {upstream} components that rely on {component}.
issues.recommendation: Review and adjust autoscaling policies to ensure they are responsive to expected load patterns. Verify available capacity in the underlying compute infrastructure. Investigate logs of the autoscaling service for errors. Consider implementing manual scaling as a temporary measure.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Compute Performance Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The performance of {component} is degraded due to issues originating from its upstream {upstream} compute dependencies. This leads to a reduced flow of requests or data to {component}.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Cache Performance Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The performance and resource utilization of {component} are impacted by issues in its downstream {downstream} cache dependencies. This typically results in increased latency and potentially higher load on {component} or other services.

ruleIntents.name: Autoscale Failure with Upstream Demand
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing resource exhaustion and potential unavailability due to a failure in its autoscaling mechanism to cope with increased demand from its upstream {upstream} dependencies.

issues.cause: appServer is reacting to compute instability.
issues.title: appServer topology pressure
issues.rootReason: The appServer|up:load_balancer|down:compute|traits:autoscale,has_failover_target,is_sync_request_path dependency profile is causing realistic spillover behavior.
issues.impactSummary: appServer is surfacing dependency-driven pressure from the current topology.
issues.recommendation: Review adjacent dependencies, retries, and failover behavior for appServer.

assumptions: Fallback topology overlay generated because AI output was unavailable.

ruleIntents.name: appServer_profile_overlay
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is reacting to dependency pressure from {source}.

reviewWarnings: Review this auto-generated overlay in the admin studio.

issues.cause: The upstream API Gateway responsible for routing requests to this application server is unavailable or unresponsive.
issues.title: Upstream API Gateway Unavailability Affects Application Server
issues.rootReason: Loss of connectivity or operational failure of the primary upstream API Gateway.
issues.impactSummary: Application server will be unable to receive requests, leading to service degradation or outage for end-users.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and connectivity of the upstream API Gateway. Ensure proper routing configuration and redundancy. Consider implementing circuit breakers or retries on the application server for upstream calls.

issues.cause: The upstream API Gateway is experiencing high latency, causing delays in requests reaching the application server.
issues.title: Increased Latency from Upstream API Gateway
issues.rootReason: Overload, resource contention, or misconfiguration in the upstream API Gateway.
issues.impactSummary: Users will experience slow application responses, potentially leading to timeouts or poor user experience.
issues.recommendation: Optimize the performance of the upstream API Gateway. Implement caching, rate limiting, or scale the gateway resources. Review network paths for bottlenecks.

issues.cause: The application server's requests to its downstream API Gateway are failing due to the gateway's unavailability or unresponsiveness.
issues.title: Downstream API Gateway Unavailability Affects Application Server Operations
issues.rootReason: Loss of connectivity or operational failure of the primary downstream API Gateway, critical for {component}'s internal operations or external service calls.
issues.impactSummary: Application server operations relying on the downstream API Gateway will fail, leading to feature degradation or complete service outage. Due to 'is_sync_request_path', this can directly block user requests.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and connectivity of the downstream API Gateway. Ensure proper routing configuration and redundancy. Implement robust error handling, retries, and circuit breakers in the application server for downstream calls.

issues.cause: Increased request volume or computational intensity from the upstream API Gateway is causing high resource utilization on the application server.
issues.title: Application Server Experiencing High Load from Upstream
issues.rootReason: Spike in user traffic, inefficient upstream request patterns, or insufficient capacity on the application server.
issues.impactSummary: If not scaled appropriately, the application server may become overloaded, leading to increased latency, errors, and potential outages.
issues.recommendation: Ensure horizontal scaling (autoscale) is properly configured and responsive. Optimize application performance to handle higher loads. Consider rate limiting at the upstream API Gateway.

ruleIntents.name: Impact of Upstream API Gateway Latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing increased request latency because its {upstream} API Gateway is slow to respond to incoming requests.

ruleIntents.name: Impact of Downstream API Gateway Failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is generating errors because its {downstream} API Gateway dependency is unavailable or failing to respond.

ruleIntents.name: Autoscale Reaction to Upstream Load
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is scaling its replicas in response to increased traffic volume originating from its {upstream} API Gateway.

issues.cause: High latency from a critical downstream service is causing the API Gateway to hold connections longer and accumulate processing time.
issues.title: Downstream Service Latency Impacting API Gateway Performance
issues.rootReason: Slow response times from one or more downstream 'compute' or 'load_balancer' components, exacerbated by the 'is_sync_request_path' trait of the API Gateway.
issues.impactSummary: Increased P50/P90/P99 latency for API Gateway requests, potential for timeout errors propagating upstream, and resource exhaustion on the gateway.
issues.recommendation: Investigate downstream service performance bottlenecks. Implement circuit breakers or timeouts for downstream calls. Consider asynchronous patterns if 'is_sync_request_path' allows.

issues.cause: A failure in one or more downstream services is amplified by the API Gateway's fanout pattern, leading to increased error rates and potential resource contention.
issues.title: Fanout Failure Amplification Due to Downstream Service Outage
issues.rootReason: High fanout (`fanout_high` trait) combined with failures in downstream 'compute' or 'load_balancer' components, leading to cascading failures or resource exhaustion within the API Gateway.
issues.impactSummary: Increased error rates (e.g., 5xx status codes) for requests to the API Gateway, potential for increased latency as retries occur, and gateway resource exhaustion.
issues.recommendation: Implement robust circuit breakers and fallback mechanisms for all downstream calls. Utilize bulkheads for different downstream service groups. Ensure proper error handling and retry policies with exponential backoff.

issues.cause: The combination of high fanout and synchronous request paths leads to rapid resource consumption (connections, threads, memory) when downstream services are slow or traffic is high.
issues.title: API Gateway Resource Exhaustion under Load
issues.rootReason: The `fanout_high` and `is_sync_request_path` traits cause {component} to hold open many connections and consume significant resources while waiting for responses from downstream 'compute' or 'load_balancer' components, leading to resource limits being hit.
issues.impactSummary: Gateway becomes unresponsive, new connections are rejected, and existing requests time out, leading to service outage for upstream callers.
issues.recommendation: Monitor resource utilization closely. Optimize downstream service performance. Implement connection pooling and timeouts aggressively. Consider auto-scaling the API Gateway based on resource metrics.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate Downstream Latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Increased latency from {downstream} services is propagating through {component}, causing {upstream} components to experience elevated response times for requests routed through this gateway.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate Downstream Failure Rate
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Failures in {downstream} services are amplified by {component}'s high fanout, leading to increased error rates observed by {upstream} components.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate Resource Exhaustion
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing resource exhaustion due to high fanout and synchronous request processing, potentially exacerbated by slow {downstream} services. This prevents it from serving new requests from {upstream}.

issues.cause: Excessive incoming requests or slow downstream processing overwhelming the API Gateway's capacity.
issues.title: API Gateway Overload
issues.rootReason: High traffic volume from upstream services, misconfigured rate limits, or degraded performance in a critical downstream service.
issues.impactSummary: Increased request latency, client timeouts, and potential for service unavailability or request drops.
issues.recommendation: Implement dynamic rate limiting, scale out API Gateway instances, investigate and optimize performance of critical downstream dependencies, or apply circuit breaking.

issues.cause: One or more critical downstream services are unavailable or returning errors, preventing the API Gateway from fulfilling requests.
issues.title: Downstream Service Dependency Failure
issues.rootReason: Deployment issues, resource exhaustion, or fatal errors in a dependent service.
issues.impactSummary: Client requests fail or return error responses, severely impacting functionality dependent on the failed service.
issues.recommendation: Investigate health and logs of affected downstream services. Implement retry mechanisms, circuit breakers, or graceful degradation for dependent services.

assumptions: API Gateway is configured for synchronous request processing, making it highly sensitive to downstream latency.
The API Gateway acts as a critical path component, processing requests for multiple upstream and downstream services.
The API Gateway has limited internal queuing capacity for burst traffic.
Rate limiting is either not configured or insufficient to handle extreme load conditions.

ruleIntents.name: DetectUpstreamRequestSpike
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: An excessive volume of requests from {upstream} is causing {component} to experience high latency and resource contention, leading to an API Gateway Overload.

ruleIntents.name: DetectDownstreamServiceSlowdown
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Slow response times from the {downstream} service are increasing the end-to-end latency and resource utilization for {component}, leading to an API Gateway Overload.

ruleIntents.name: DetectDownstreamServiceErrors
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High error rates from the {downstream} service are being propagated by {component}, leading to a Downstream Service Dependency Failure.

issues.cause: Slow response times from connected downstream services (e.g., compute, load_balancer).
issues.title: Downstream Service Latency Impact
issues.rootReason: Resource exhaustion, database contention, external service dependency, or code inefficiency in downstream services.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end latency, potential for connection exhaustion and cascading failures in the {component} and its upstream clients due to synchronous path and high fanout.
issues.recommendation: Investigate downstream service health and performance metrics, consider circuit breaking or rate limiting from {component} to downstream, and scale downstream services.

issues.cause: Complete failure or unresponsiveness of one or more downstream services.
issues.title: Downstream Service Unavailability
issues.rootReason: Service crash, deployment error, network partition, or resource starvation in downstream services.
issues.impactSummary: Direct impact on service availability for requests handled by {component}, leading to user-visible errors and degraded experience, potentially amplified across multiple requests due to high fanout.
issues.recommendation: Implement robust retry mechanisms with backoff, circuit breaking, and health checks within {component}. Ensure downstream services have high availability strategies (e.g., redundancy, auto-scaling).

issues.cause: Excessive incoming request volume from upstream components (e.g., load balancer).
issues.title: Upstream Traffic Overload
issues.rootReason: Unexpected user traffic surge, misconfigured upstream service, or denial-of-service attack.
issues.impactSummary: Degradation or complete loss of service for {component} due to resource exhaustion, potentially cascading to downstream services if they are also overwhelmed by increased requests from {component} that has high fanout.
issues.recommendation: Implement rate limiting, request queueing, and auto-scaling for {component}. Collaborate with upstream teams to manage traffic patterns.

ruleIntents.name: downstream_service_latency
ruleIntents.impactShape: Increased latency, resource exhaustion (CPU, memory, connections) for {component}; increased upstream latency and timeouts.
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: A latency spike in {downstream} service, exacerbated by {component}'s high fanout and synchronous request path, causes {component} to experience increased response times and resource saturation, impacting its {upstream} clients.

ruleIntents.name: downstream_service_failure
ruleIntents.impactShape: High error rate for {component}, directly impacting upstream services. Amplified impact due to high fanout.
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Failure or unavailability of {downstream} service, especially with {component}'s high fanout, causes {component} to return errors to its {upstream} clients, leading to reduced overall system availability.

ruleIntents.name: upstream_overload
ruleIntents.impactShape: Resource exhaustion (CPU, memory, connections) and increased latency for {component}, potentially leading to self-failure and cascading errors to upstream.
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: An unexpected surge in traffic from {upstream} causes {component} to become overwhelmed and unable to process requests efficiently, leading to resource exhaustion and degraded performance or complete unavailability.

issues.cause: One or more downstream services (e.g., api_gateway, dns_discovery) are unresponsive or unhealthy, as detected by the load balancer's health checks.
issues.title: Downstream Service Unavailability
issues.rootReason: Health check failures, service crashes, network partitions, or capacity exhaustion in dependent services.
issues.impactSummary: Requests forwarded by the load balancer to the affected downstream services will fail or timeout, leading to degraded user experience or complete service outage for paths traversing these services.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and operational status of downstream services, scale up resources if necessary, or configure automatic failover strategies within the load balancer.

issues.cause: The network load balancer itself is receiving an exceptionally high volume of requests, exceeding its configured or provisioned capacity for connection handling or request processing.
issues.title: Load Balancer Overload
issues.rootReason: Sudden traffic surge, insufficient scaling of the load balancer instances, misconfigured rate limits, or a 'thundering herd' problem from upstream retries.
issues.impactSummary: The load balancer will start dropping requests, increasing request latency, or becoming unresponsive, leading to cascading failures for all upstream services depending on it.
issues.recommendation: Increase the capacity of the load balancer by scaling out instances, implement more aggressive upstream rate limiting, or optimize request processing within the load balancer configuration.

issues.cause: The dns_discovery service, which is critical for resolving hostnames to IP addresses for backend services or for the load balancer's own operational configuration, is unavailable or returning errors.
issues.title: DNS Resolution Failure
issues.rootReason: DNS server issues, network isolation of DNS infrastructure, misconfigured DNS resolvers, or stale DNS caches preventing fresh resolution.
issues.impactSummary: The load balancer will be unable to discover or connect to its downstream services by hostname, leading to connection failures and service unavailability, even if downstream services are healthy.
issues.recommendation: Restore the DNS discovery service's operational status, verify network connectivity to DNS resolvers, and ensure redundant DNS resolvers are configured to prevent single points of failure.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate Downstream Unavailability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing failures due to unavailable downstream services, impacting {upstream} by failing to forward requests.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate Load Balancer Overload
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is overloaded, causing request failures and high latency for {upstream} due to internal capacity limits.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate DNS Resolution Failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} cannot resolve downstream service addresses due to failures in {source} (DNS discovery), resulting in connection failures to {downstream}.

issues.cause: The DNS component failed to resolve a domain name request, preventing service discovery.
issues.title: DNS Resolution Failure
issues.rootReason: Possible causes include DNS server misconfiguration, network isolation of the DNS server, unavailability of upstream DNS resolvers, or corrupted DNS records.
issues.impactSummary: Upstream components (clients or load balancers) will be unable to obtain IP addresses for their intended downstream targets, leading to connection failures and service unavailability for affected traffic flows.
issues.recommendation: Verify DNS server configuration (e.g., /etc/resolv.conf, DNS zone files). Check network connectivity to configured DNS servers. Inspect DNS server logs for errors and queries. Consider implementing redundant DNS services.

ruleIntents.name: Detect DNS resolution errors impacting upstream clients
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} DNS component failed to resolve a critical domain name for {upstream}. This directly prevents {upstream} from establishing connections to its intended downstream services.

issues.cause: High concurrent write operations overwhelming I/O, CPU, or lock mechanisms, leading to slower transaction commits and queuing.
issues.title: Database experiencing write contention and slowdown
issues.rootReason: Inadequate resource provisioning for write operations, suboptimal indexing for common write patterns, lack of write-optimized storage or configuration.
issues.impactSummary: Increased write latency, reduced throughput, potential timeouts for upstream compute services, and delays in data availability for downstream consumers.
issues.recommendation: Optimize write queries, introduce connection pooling, scale database vertically or horizontally (e.g., sharding), implement write-ahead logging (WAL) improvements, use a dedicated write replica set if architecture allows.

issues.cause: Concurrent heavy write operations consume significant I/O, memory, and CPU resources, causing read queries to contend for resources and experience increased latency. Lock contention between reads and writes can also occur.
issues.title: Read performance degradation due to intense write activity
issues.rootReason: Shared resource contention (disk, CPU, memory) between read and write operations; insufficient indexing for common read patterns under high write load; database locking mechanisms.
issues.impactSummary: Increased read latency, slower query execution for upstream services, potentially stale data if reads are directed to lagging replicas.
issues.recommendation: Implement read replicas, optimize read queries, ensure proper indexing, consider separating read/write concerns (CQRS pattern), scale resources.

ruleIntents.name: Write Contention Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} database is experiencing severe write contention, causing increased transaction latency for {upstream} compute services and delaying data synchronization with {downstream} load balancers.

ruleIntents.name: Read Impact from Write Load
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} database is experiencing degraded read performance due to heavy write activity, impacting {upstream} compute services that rely on timely data retrieval.

issues.cause: One or more downstream services connected to the load balancer are reporting unhealthy status, failing health checks, or exhibiting high error rates.
issues.title: Load Balancer detects unhealthy downstream service
issues.rootReason: Failure in a {downstream} service, or network connectivity issues between {component} and {downstream}.
issues.impactSummary: Increased error rates and potentially reduced availability for traffic routed through the affected downstream services, leading to user-facing impact.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance of the affected downstream services. Verify network connectivity and load balancer health check configurations.

issues.cause: Excessive latency observed from upstream services, causing backpressure on the load balancer and potential queueing or timeouts.
issues.title: Increased latency from upstream services impacting load balancer
issues.rootReason: High latency or contention within an {upstream} service, or network congestion between {source} and {component}.
issues.impactSummary: User-facing requests experience increased latency and potential timeouts as the load balancer waits for responses from slow upstream dependencies, impacting end-to-end request performance.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the performance of upstream services and network path. Consider adjusting load balancer timeouts or circuit breaking thresholds.

ruleIntents.name: Route traffic away from unhealthy downstream services
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: When a {downstream} service becomes unhealthy, {component} will stop routing traffic to it, impacting the availability of the {downstream} and potentially shifting load to other healthy instances.

ruleIntents.name: Manage upstream request latency and queueing
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: When {upstream} services send requests with high latency to {component}, {component} may experience increased queueing or timeouts, propagating performance degradation to its {source}.

issues.cause: The downstream database component is exhibiting high latency or errors, directly impacting the application server's ability to process requests that depend on database operations.
issues.title: Application Server Experiencing Elevated Latency due to Downstream Database Performance Degradation
issues.rootReason: Database contention, resource exhaustion, network issues, or query inefficiencies in the database.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-user request latency, potential timeout errors, and reduced throughput for the application server. Circuit breakers may trip, leading to increased error rates for specific database-dependent operations.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the connected database instance for performance bottlenecks, resource utilization, or error logs. Optimize database queries or scale database resources. Review application server database connection pooling and timeout settings.

issues.cause: The downstream cache component is exhibiting high latency or errors, causing the application server to either slow down when accessing cached data or fall back to more expensive data sources like a database.
issues.title: Application Server Experiencing Elevated Latency due to Downstream Cache Performance Degradation
issues.rootReason: Cache resource exhaustion, network issues, or high cache eviction rates leading to increased backend load.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-user request latency, increased load on downstream databases, and potential timeout errors for the application server. Circuit breakers may trip for cache access, causing fallback behavior or increased error rates.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the connected cache instance for performance bottlenecks, resource utilization, or error logs. Optimize cache usage patterns in the application or scale cache resources. If the app server falls back to database, investigate database load.

issues.cause: The upstream load balancer is failing to route traffic to the application server, potentially due to misconfiguration, health check failures, or load balancer issues.
issues.title: Application Server Not Receiving Expected Traffic from Upstream Load Balancer
issues.rootReason: Load balancer configuration errors, health check misconfigurations on the application server, or issues within the load balancer itself.
issues.impactSummary: Application server becomes idle, leading to reduced overall service capacity and potential unavailability if other instances are also affected.
issues.recommendation: Verify load balancer health checks and routing rules for the application server. Inspect application server's readiness/liveness probes. Check load balancer logs for errors.

issues.cause: The application server is consuming excessive resources (CPU, memory, connections) due to repeated retries or long-running requests against a slow or erroring downstream dependency, leading to its own performance degradation or unavailability.
issues.title: Application Server Resource Exhaustion Induced by Downstream Dependency Latency or Errors
issues.rootReason: Downstream service performance degradation (e.g., database, cache), insufficient application server capacity to handle prolonged retry storm, or misconfigured retry/timeout policies.
issues.impactSummary: Degraded performance and potential unavailability of the application server itself, even for requests not directly dependent on the failing downstream. Can trigger autoscaling, which might not resolve the underlying dependency issue.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the root cause of the downstream dependency issues. Review application server resource utilization and scaling policies. Adjust retry budgets, timeouts, and circuit breaker configurations to prevent resource exhaustion.

ruleIntents.name: downstream_database_latency_propagation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing elevated response times due to high latency from its downstream database {downstream}. This significantly impacts requests requiring database access.

ruleIntents.name: downstream_cache_latency_propagation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing elevated response times due to high latency or errors from its downstream cache {downstream}. This might also increase load on the database if cache misses become more frequent.

ruleIntents.name: upstream_load_balancer_traffic_drop
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is not receiving expected traffic from its upstream load balancer {upstream}, indicating a potential issue with routing or health checks at the load balancer level.

ruleIntents.name: downstream_induced_resource_exhaustion
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing high resource utilization (e.g., CPU, memory, connections) due to excessive retries or prolonged blocked requests waiting for a slow or erroring downstream dependency like {downstream}.

issues.cause: Sustained high request volume from upstream, or internal resource exhaustion.
issues.title: High Incoming Load Exceeds App Server Capacity
issues.rootReason: Insufficient scaling, inefficient processing, or misconfigured rate limits.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, dropped requests, service degradation due to inability to process incoming requests effectively.
issues.recommendation: Investigate upstream traffic patterns, optimize application performance, or adjust autoscaling policies/rate limits.

issues.cause: Slow response times from connected downstream services (e.g., database, cache).
issues.title: Downstream Dependency Latency
issues.rootReason: Downstream bottleneck, network latency, or inefficient queries/data access within the downstream service.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end request latency for upstream callers and potential timeouts on the app server.
issues.recommendation: Optimize downstream service performance, check network connectivity, or implement better caching/data access patterns.

issues.cause: Excessive errors or timeouts from a downstream dependency causing the circuit breaker to open.
issues.title: Circuit Breaker Tripped Due to Downstream Failure
issues.rootReason: Downstream service failure, degradation, or network issues preventing successful communication from the app server.
issues.impactSummary: Requests to the affected downstream service will fail fast, leading to errors (e.g., 500s) on the app server for functionality relying on that dependency.
issues.recommendation: Investigate and resolve downstream dependency issues. The circuit breaker is working as designed to prevent cascading failures.

issues.cause: Frequent transient failures or high latency from downstream services leading to an excessive number of retries by the app server.
issues.title: Downstream Retry Storm
issues.rootReason: Downstream instability, misconfigured retry policies, or thundering herd problem where retries exacerbate downstream issues.
issues.impactSummary: Increased load on downstream services, exacerbating their problems; increased latency and resource consumption on the app server.
issues.recommendation: Investigate downstream service health, review and tune retry policies (e.g., exponential backoff, jitter, max retries), or implement client-side rate limiting to downstream.

ruleIntents.name: Incoming Load Handling
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High incoming request rate from {upstream} is causing {component} to experience resource exhaustion or trigger rate limits.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Latency Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Slow responses from downstream service {downstream} are increasing the request latency for {component}.

ruleIntents.name: Circuit Breaker Activation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The circuit breaker in {component} has tripped due to excessive failures or timeouts when calling {downstream}, preventing further requests to that dependency.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Retry Pressure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Frequent failures from {downstream} are causing {component} to issue numerous retries, potentially overwhelming {downstream} further or consuming local resources.

issues.cause: appServer is reacting to cache instability.
issues.title: appServer topology pressure
issues.rootReason: The appServer|up:load_balancer|down:cache,database|traits:autoscale,has_rate_limiting,has_retries,is_sync_request_path dependency profile is causing realistic spillover behavior.
issues.impactSummary: appServer is surfacing dependency-driven pressure from the current topology.
issues.recommendation: Review adjacent dependencies, retries, and failover behavior for appServer.

assumptions: Fallback topology overlay generated because AI output was unavailable.

ruleIntents.name: appServer_profile_overlay
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is reacting to dependency pressure from {source}.

reviewWarnings: Review this auto-generated overlay in the admin studio.

issues.cause: The application server is receiving more requests than it can process, often due to an unexpected surge in traffic or misconfigured upstream components.
issues.title: Application Server Overloaded
issues.rootReason: Excessive upstream request volume or insufficient resource allocation for the current traffic load.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, elevated error rates, and potential unavailability of the application server, affecting end-users and dependent services.
issues.recommendation: Investigate upstream traffic patterns, scale out the application server instances, or implement rate limiting at the load balancer. Optimize application code for better resource efficiency.

issues.cause: A critical downstream service, such as a database or cache, is experiencing issues (e.g., high latency, errors, unavailability), preventing the application server from completing its requests.
issues.title: Downstream Service Dependency Failure
issues.rootReason: Failure or performance degradation in a required downstream service component.
issues.impactSummary: Application server requests fail or time out, leading to service disruption for users and other upstream services relying on this application server.
issues.recommendation: Identify and resolve the root cause of the issue in the specific downstream service. Implement circuit breakers and retries on the application server to gracefully handle dependency failures.

issues.cause: The application server is consuming excessive CPU, memory, or other system resources, leading to degraded performance or unresponsiveness, often due to inefficient code or memory leaks.
issues.title: Application Server Resource Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: Internal application logic or configuration issues causing resource spikes or leaks.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency and error rates on the application server, leading to poor user experience and potential propagation of issues to upstream components.
issues.recommendation: Analyze application logs and metrics for performance bottlenecks or memory leaks. Optimize application code, adjust resource limits, or scale out if the issue is sustained load that exceeds current capacity.

assumptions: The application server interacts synchronously with its downstream dependencies (cache, database).
The upstream load balancer distributes traffic evenly across application server instances.
Resource metrics (CPU, memory, request queue depth) are available for the application server.

ruleIntents.name: Detect AppServer Overload from Upstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing overload, likely due to excessive traffic from its {upstream} sources, leading to performance degradation.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Downstream Dependency Failure Impacting AppServer
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is suffering performance degradation due to issues with its {downstream} dependencies, such as slow responses or errors from the database or cache.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Internal AppServer Resource Exhaustion
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing internal resource exhaustion (e.g., high CPU, memory, or thread pool usage), leading to degraded performance for all incoming requests.

issues.cause: Excessive inbound request traffic from upstream components or clients.
issues.title: API Gateway Rate Limit Exceeded
issues.rootReason: The configured rate limits on the API Gateway have been surpassed, leading to request rejections.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are rejected at the point of ingress, leading to increased error rates for clients and potentially degraded user experience.
issues.recommendation: Review and adjust rate limiting policies, scale upstream services if they are causing a traffic amplification, or implement backpressure mechanisms.

issues.cause: Repeated failures when attempting to proxy requests to an internal backend service or process.
issues.title: API Gateway Circuit Breaker Tripped
issues.rootReason: The circuit breaker has opened due to a high rate of errors or latency from an internal dependency, preventing further requests from reaching the failing dependency.
issues.impactSummary: All requests routed through the tripped circuit are immediately rejected, leading to unavailability for specific API endpoints or services.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance of internal backend services. Implement auto-recovery or health checks to reset the circuit breaker once the backend is healthy.

issues.cause: Overload, resource exhaustion (CPU, memory), or slow responses from internal backend services.
issues.title: API Gateway Experiencing High Latency
issues.rootReason: The API Gateway is processing requests slower than expected, potentially due to resource contention or internal service performance issues.
issues.impactSummary: Increased response times for all API requests, degrading user experience and potentially causing timeouts for upstream services.
issues.recommendation: Monitor resource utilization, optimize gateway processing logic, scale the gateway instances, or investigate internal service performance.

ruleIntents.name: Rate Limit Breach Detection
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} API Gateway is rejecting requests due to exceeding its configured rate limits, impacting {upstream} components.

ruleIntents.name: Circuit Breaker Open Detection
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} API Gateway has opened its circuit breaker due to internal service failures, causing requests from {upstream} to be rejected.

ruleIntents.name: Latency Spike Detection
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} API Gateway is experiencing high latency, causing slow responses for {upstream} components.

issues.cause: The load balancer is unable to find healthy downstream API Gateway instances to route traffic to.
issues.title: Downstream Service Pool Unhealthy
issues.rootReason: High error rates, resource exhaustion, or failure of backend API Gateway instances.
issues.impactSummary: Incoming requests cannot be served, leading to increased latency or errors for upstream consumers.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and capacity of the downstream API Gateway services. Scale up or resolve issues in the API Gateway pool.

issues.cause: The load balancer is experiencing a significant reduction in incoming traffic due to congestion or failure in the upstream network path.
issues.title: Upstream Network Congestion
issues.rootReason: Network infrastructure issues, high traffic volume exceeding network capacity, or misconfiguration in upstream routing.
issues.impactSummary: The load balancer is under-utilized, and services relying on it for traffic ingress are not receiving requests.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and capacity of the upstream network path (e.g., network switches, routers, firewalls, DNS). Alert network operations.

issues.cause: The load balancer itself is experiencing a critical internal failure, preventing it from processing requests.
issues.title: Load Balancer Internal Failure
issues.rootReason: Software bugs, resource exhaustion (CPU, memory), critical configuration errors, or underlying host infrastructure failure.
issues.impactSummary: All traffic attempting to pass through this load balancer instance will fail, leading to widespread impact on downstream services and upstream consumers.
issues.recommendation: Investigate load balancer logs and metrics for errors, resource spikes, or crashes. Consider restarting or replacing affected instances.

ruleIntents.name: Failover due to unhealthy downstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing a critical issue because its downstream API Gateway service pool is unhealthy. This means {component} cannot route traffic effectively to {downstream}, causing errors for {upstream} services.

ruleIntents.name: Reduced upstream traffic due to network issues
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing reduced traffic due to congestion or failure in the upstream {upstream} network path. This is preventing incoming requests from reaching {component} and subsequently {downstream} services.

ruleIntents.name: Load Balancer self-health failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} has failed internally. It is unable to process any incoming traffic from {upstream} and therefore cannot forward requests to {downstream}. This effectively isolates {downstream} from its consumers.

issues.cause: The load balancer is receiving more traffic or connection requests than its configured capacity or available resources can handle.
issues.title: Load Balancer Overloaded
issues.rootReason: Insufficient resource allocation for anticipated traffic, misconfiguration of connection limits, or an unexpected surge in demand.
issues.impactSummary: Service degradation, increased latency, connection drops, and potential unavailability for applications routed through this load balancer.
issues.recommendation: Increase the load balancer's capacity (e.g., scale out instances, increase resource limits), optimize load balancing algorithms, or implement rate limiting upstream. Review capacity planning.

issues.cause: The load balancer cannot establish or maintain connections to its upstream network resources.
issues.title: Upstream Network Unreachable
issues.rootReason: Network partitioning, firewall misconfiguration, routing issues, or the upstream network segment being offline.
issues.impactSummary: All traffic attempting to pass through the load balancer to the upstream network will fail, resulting in complete service unavailability.
issues.recommendation: Verify network connectivity between the load balancer and its upstream network. Check firewall rules, routing tables, and network device status upstream. Confirm network interface health.

issues.cause: Increased latency or error rates observed when the load balancer communicates with its upstream network.
issues.title: Upstream Network Performance Degradation
issues.rootReason: Congestion within the upstream network, high load on network devices, or performance issues in services residing upstream.
issues.impactSummary: Degraded performance for all applications relying on the load balancer, resulting in slower response times and potential user dissatisfaction.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the upstream network for congestion, resource bottlenecks, or misconfigurations. Consider optimizing network paths or scaling upstream network infrastructure. Review QoS settings.

ruleIntents.name: Load Balancer Resource Saturation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing high resource utilization, potentially leading to performance degradation or unavailability.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Network Connectivity Failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is unable to connect to its {upstream} network dependencies, likely due to a network outage or connectivity issue at the source.

ruleIntents.name: High Upstream Network Latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Elevated latency is observed from {component} when communicating with its {upstream} network. This suggests a performance bottleneck or congestion upstream.

issues.cause: The networkRateLimiter has reached its configured traffic limit, dropping excess requests.
issues.title: Network Rate Limit Exceeded
issues.rootReason: Insufficient rate limit capacity for current demand, or unexpected traffic surge.
issues.impactSummary: Traffic directed to downstream components will experience increased latency, connection errors, or dropped requests.
issues.recommendation: Review and potentially increase the rate limit configuration. Investigate the source and patterns of incoming traffic. Scale upstream components if they are sending excessive traffic.

issues.cause: The networkRateLimiter component is experiencing internal resource exhaustion (e.g., CPU, memory) or critical software errors, preventing it from processing traffic effectively.
issues.title: Network Rate Limiter Unhealthy
issues.rootReason: Software bug, resource starvation, misconfiguration, or underlying infrastructure issue.
issues.impactSummary: Total or partial loss of network traffic processing, leading to an outage or severe degradation for connected downstream components.
issues.recommendation: Investigate component logs and metrics for specific error messages or resource bottlenecks. Consider restarting the component or scaling its allocated resources. Review recent configuration changes.

ruleIntents.name: Rate Limit Exceeded Impact on Downstream Load Balancer
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is dropping traffic due to exceeding its configured rate limit, which is causing {downstream} (load_balancer) to receive reduced throughput and experience an increase in errors from dropped connections.

ruleIntents.name: Rate Limiter Unhealthy Impact on Downstream Load Balancer
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is unhealthy and unable to process traffic, leading to a significant outage or severe degradation for {downstream} (load_balancer) as it receives no or corrupted traffic.

issues.cause: A large number of upstream 'compute' components are writing to the stream concurrently, exceeding its ingress capacity or connection limits.
issues.title: Stream Overload from High Fan-in Write Contention
issues.rootReason: The stream is designed for high fan-in ('fanin_high' trait), but its provisioned resources or configuration are insufficient to handle the aggregated concurrent write load, leading to contention or saturation.
issues.impactSummary: Increased write latency, potential for message drops, connection timeouts, and backpressure propagating to upstream services.
issues.recommendation: Increase stream partition count, scale out broker nodes, optimize producer batching, or implement backpressure mechanisms at upstream 'compute' components. Consider connection pooling limits.

issues.cause: Downstream 'worker' components are not processing messages from the stream quickly enough, causing messages to accumulate in the stream's queues or logs.
issues.title: Stream Message Accumulation and Consumer Lag
issues.rootReason: The stream is part of an 'is_async_path', meaning lag can build up without immediate blocking. The consumption rate by 'worker' components does not keep pace with the incoming message rate, indicating under-provisioning or inefficient processing.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end latency for asynchronous operations, elevated resource usage (disk/memory) on the stream, potential for message expiration or service degradation.
issues.recommendation: Scale out downstream 'worker' instances, optimize 'worker' processing logic, implement consumer group rebalancing, or investigate message serialization/deserialization overhead. Consider a Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) for poisoned messages.

issues.cause: The stream is experiencing a sustained high volume of writes, characteristic of its 'write_heavy' trait, pushing its underlying infrastructure capacity limits.
issues.title: Sustained High Write Load Exceeds Stream Capacity
issues.rootReason: The stream's provisioned resources (e.g., I/O throughput, CPU, network bandwidth for brokers or storage) are insufficient to handle the persistent high write demand.
issues.impactSummary: Elevated write latency, throttled requests, increased resource utilization leading to potential failures, and general performance degradation for all operations.
issues.recommendation: Scale stream resources (e.g., add more partitions, use faster storage, upgrade broker instances), implement rate limiting upstream, or re-evaluate data retention policies and batching strategies.

ruleIntents.name: Detect_Upstream_Fanin_Overload
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing high write contention from multiple {upstream} 'compute' components due to its 'fanin_high' trait, potentially leading to increased latency or message drops. This indicates potential overload.

ruleIntents.name: Detect_Downstream_Consumer_Lag
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is accumulating messages due to its 'is_async_path' trait and insufficient consumption by {downstream} 'worker' components, leading to increased end-to-end latency and potential resource exhaustion.

ruleIntents.name: Detect_Sustained_Write_Overload
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing a sustained high write load, typical for a 'write_heavy' component, pushing its capacity limits and risking performance degradation and availability.

issues.cause: Overloaded or unhealthy backend compute services connected downstream from the API Gateway.
issues.title: Downstream Compute Service Degradation Impacting API Gateway
issues.rootReason: Resource exhaustion, software bugs, or misconfiguration in downstream services are preventing them from processing requests efficiently.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency and error rates for requests processed by the API Gateway, potentially leading to partial or complete service unavailability for end-users.
issues.recommendation: Investigate logs and metrics of the downstream compute services for errors, high resource utilization, or process failures. Consider scaling out downstream compute instances, optimizing inefficient operations, or fixing application-level errors.

issues.cause: High latency or errors originating from the upstream load balancer responsible for directing traffic to the API Gateway.
issues.title: Upstream Load Balancer Introducing Latency
issues.rootReason: The upstream load balancer might be misconfigured, experiencing network issues with the API Gateway, or itself be overloaded.
issues.impactSummary: End-to-end request latency observed at the API Gateway increases, directly impacting user experience and potentially violating service level objectives.
issues.recommendation: Examine the health and configuration of the upstream load balancer. Verify network connectivity and bandwidth between the load balancer and the API Gateway. Review load balancer metrics for signs of saturation or error.

issues.cause: The incoming request rate to the API Gateway has exceeded its configured rate limiting thresholds.
issues.title: API Gateway Rate Limiting Engaged
issues.rootReason: This can be due to an unexpected surge in legitimate traffic, a malicious DDoS attack, a misconfigured rate limit, or a client application bug sending excessive requests.
issues.impactSummary: A portion of incoming requests are rejected with a 429 (Too Many Requests) status, leading to service disruption for affected clients while protecting backend services from overload.
issues.recommendation: Review and adjust API Gateway rate limiting configurations if the increased traffic is legitimate and expected. Investigate client behavior to identify potential misconfigurations or malicious activity. Consider implementing client-side circuit breakers or backpressure mechanisms.

ruleIntents.name: DownstreamComputeServiceDegradation
ruleIntents.impactShape: Increased errors and latency on API Gateway as a result of downstream issues.
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing increased error rates and latency due to degradation in its {downstream} compute services.

ruleIntents.name: UpstreamLoadBalancerLatency
ruleIntents.impactShape: Higher overall request latency observed by the API Gateway due to upstream bottlenecks.
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is observing higher overall request latency due to delays introduced by the {upstream} load balancer.

ruleIntents.name: APIGatewayRateLimitingActive
ruleIntents.impactShape: API Gateway rejecting requests due to rate limits being hit.
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is actively rate-limiting incoming requests, leading to some requests being rejected. This is often due to high demand or misconfigured limits.

issues.cause: High concurrent write operations, inefficient queries, or insufficient database resources (CPU, I/O, memory) lead to contention and performance degradation.
issues.title: Database Write Contention or Resource Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: The component is experiencing a high volume of write traffic from upstream {source} systems, exceeding its capacity or leading to resource bottlenecks.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency for write operations, potential for query timeouts, and degradation of overall application performance.
issues.recommendation: Review database query performance, optimize indexes, scale database resources (CPU, I/O), or implement write-sharding/replication strategies.

issues.cause: Uneven distribution of data or access patterns across database shards, leading to disproportionate load on a subset of shards.
issues.title: Database Shard Hot-Spotting
issues.rootReason: Specific shards within {component}'s sharded topology are receiving a significantly higher volume of read or write traffic compared to others, likely due to data access patterns from upstream {source}.
issues.impactSummary: Performance bottlenecks on hot shards, leading to increased latency and potential service disruptions for operations targeting those shards.
issues.recommendation: Rebalance data across shards, review sharding key strategy, or scale resources for the hot shard(s).

issues.cause: Too many concurrent connections from upstream clients exhausting the database's configured connection limits.
issues.title: Database Connection Saturation
issues.rootReason: The {component} database is overwhelmed by the number of active connections originating from upstream {upstream} components, causing new connection requests to fail.
issues.impactSummary: Upstream services fail to establish new database connections, leading to service unavailability or error rates.
issues.recommendation: Increase database connection limits, optimize connection pooling in upstream services, or implement a connection proxy.

ruleIntents.name: Detect High Write Load
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing high write contention, likely due to heavy write traffic from upstream {upstream} services. This is causing increased latency and potential service degradation for {upstream} operations.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Shard Imbalance
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing a shard hot-spot, where traffic from upstream {upstream} is unevenly distributed, overwhelming specific shards. This causes localized performance degradation impacting {upstream} requests.

ruleIntents.name: Detect High Fan-in Connection Load
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is saturated with connections from upstream {upstream} components, exceeding its capacity. This prevents {upstream} from establishing new database connections, leading to errors and service unavailability for {upstream} operations.

issues.cause: Configured rate limits on the API Gateway have been breached by incoming traffic.
issues.title: API Gateway Rate Limit Exceeded
issues.rootReason: Sudden influx of traffic or insufficient rate limit capacity for current load.
issues.impactSummary: Requests are being rejected at the API Gateway, leading to increased error rates for upstream clients.
issues.recommendation: Review and adjust API Gateway rate limit configurations, scale underlying resources if traffic is legitimate, or implement traffic shaping upstream.

issues.cause: High fanout from the API Gateway or excessive request volume is overwhelming downstream compute services.
issues.title: API Gateway Causing Downstream Overload
issues.rootReason: Insufficient downstream capacity, inefficient API Gateway fanout logic, or unmanaged upstream traffic surge.
issues.impactSummary: Downstream services become unresponsive or fail, leading to API Gateway errors and degraded service.
issues.recommendation: Optimize API Gateway fanout logic, scale downstream compute services, implement circuit breakers or dynamic throttling.

issues.cause: Increased response times from downstream compute services directly propagate to the API Gateway due to its synchronous request path.
issues.title: Downstream Latency Affecting API Gateway Response Times
issues.rootReason: Slow processing within downstream services, database bottlenecks, or network issues affecting downstream dependencies.
issues.impactSummary: API Gateway response times are degraded, potentially leading to client timeouts and poor user experience.
issues.recommendation: Optimize downstream service performance, explore asynchronous patterns for specific operations, increase API Gateway timeouts.

assumptions: The API Gateway enforces rate limiting policies.
The API Gateway performs fanout to multiple downstream services for some requests.
The primary request path through the API Gateway is synchronous, making it sensitive to downstream latency.

ruleIntents.name: Rate Limit Breach Detection
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is rejecting requests from {source} due to exceeding configured rate limits, leading to increased upstream error rates.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Load Propagation (High Fanout)
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High fanout processing within {component} is generating an amplified load on {downstream} services, potentially leading to {downstream} overload.

ruleIntents.name: Synchronous Downstream Latency Correlation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Elevated latency observed at {component} directly correlates with increased response times from {downstream} services, impacting {upstream} clients.

issues.cause: High write load on the primary, network latency between primary and replica, or replica resource contention preventing it from keeping up.
issues.title: Database Replication Lag
issues.rootReason: Inability of a replica database to keep up with the primary's write operations, leading to an out-of-sync state.
issues.impactSummary: Out-of-sync data between primary and replica, potential for stale data to be served by the replica, and increased recovery point objective (RPO) during failover.
issues.recommendation: Increase replica capacity, optimize network connectivity, review primary database write patterns for inefficiencies, or consider sharding.

issues.cause: The designated failover target database is unreachable, unhealthy, or improperly configured, preventing it from taking over primary duties.
issues.title: Database Failover Target Unavailable
issues.rootReason: Loss of high availability and disaster recovery capability due to a compromised or unhealthy failover peer, rendering the system vulnerable to downtime.
issues.impactSummary: During a primary database failure, automatic or manual failover to the designated target will fail, leading to extended downtime and potential data loss.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health, connectivity, and configuration of the failover target. Ensure replication is healthy and failover mechanisms are properly tested.

issues.cause: Sustained high write operations exceeding the database's configured capacity, leading to resource contention (CPU, I/O, memory), or inefficient write queries.
issues.title: Database Performance Degradation Due to High Write Load
issues.rootReason: The database's inability to process incoming write requests efficiently, resulting in resource saturation and backlogs.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency for write operations, which can also cascade and affect read operations, leading to overall system slowdown, and potential request timeouts.
issues.recommendation: Optimize write queries, scale database resources (CPU, memory, I/O), implement database sharding, or distribute write load across multiple instances.

ruleIntents.name: Primary detects downstream replica replication lag
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} primary database has identified its {downstream} replica database is experiencing significant replication lag. This impacts the freshness of data served by the replica and could lead to data loss during a failover event.

ruleIntents.name: Primary detects unavailable failover target
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} primary database has detected that its {failover} target is unavailable or unhealthy. This compromises the high availability and disaster recovery posture of {component}.

ruleIntents.name: Detect performance degradation from high write load
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} database is experiencing performance degradation due to a high volume of write operations. This may be causing increased latency for both write and read requests.

issues.cause: Excessive inbound traffic or network congestion from the upstream 'network' affecting API Gateway's ability to receive requests.
issues.title: Upstream Network Congestion or High Traffic Volume
issues.rootReason: Inadequate network capacity, misconfigured upstream network devices, or malicious traffic spikes (DDoS).
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, request timeouts, and potential service unavailability for clients.
issues.recommendation: Implement rate limiting, WAF rules, or scale upstream network capacity. Investigate network infrastructure for bottlenecks.

issues.cause: The downstream 'load_balancer' is returning errors or failing health checks, preventing the API Gateway from forwarding requests successfully.
issues.title: Downstream Load Balancer Unhealthy or Misconfigured
issues.rootReason: Load balancer misconfiguration, backend service failures behind the load balancer, or the load balancer itself being overloaded/unhealthy.
issues.impactSummary: Requests cannot be forwarded to backend services, leading to service unavailability.
issues.recommendation: Verify load balancer configuration, health checks, and the health of its backend targets. Scale or restart the load balancer if necessary.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Network Traffic Anomaly Detection
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} detected unusually high incoming traffic from the {upstream} network, leading to request saturation and degraded performance.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Load Balancer Error Rate Anomaly
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is receiving an unusually high error rate when attempting to forward requests to the {downstream} load balancer, indicating an issue with the load balancer or its targets.

issues.cause: A downstream service connected to the load balancer has become unresponsive, failed, or is otherwise unable to process requests, preventing the load balancer from forwarding traffic to it.
issues.title: Downstream Service Unresponsive or Failed
issues.rootReason: Operational failure, network isolation, or complete unresponsiveness of a backend service targeted by the load balancer's health checks.
issues.impactSummary: Traffic intended for the unavailable downstream service will be redirected to other healthy services, or fail if no alternatives exist. This can increase load on remaining healthy services or result in client-facing errors.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and operational status of the affected downstream services. Ensure proper health checks are configured and functioning correctly on the load balancer to accurately reflect service health.

ruleIntents.name: HandleDownstreamServiceUnavailability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The load balancer {component} detected that a downstream service {downstream} is unavailable, leading to traffic redirection.

issues.cause: The downstream queue is receiving messages faster than it can process them, leading to its capacity being exceeded.
issues.title: Downstream Queue Capacity Exceeded
issues.rootReason: Insufficient downstream processing capacity, sudden spike in upstream message production, or downstream service outage.
issues.impactSummary: Messages from {component} are rejected or buffered, leading to increased latency, message loss, or internal backpressure on the {component}.
issues.recommendation: Scale out downstream consumers, optimize downstream processing logic, implement retry mechanisms with backoff, or apply rate limiting at the {component}.

issues.cause: The {component} is unable to send messages to its downstream {downstream} queue due to issues like queue saturation, network connectivity problems, or downstream service unresponsiveness.
issues.title: Worker Output Blocked by Downstream Dependency
issues.rootReason: Downstream queue operational issues, network partitioning, or an inability of the {component} to handle failed enqueue operations gracefully.
issues.impactSummary: Tasks processed by {component} cannot be completed, leading to increased local resource utilization, timeouts, or eventual task failure within {component}.
issues.recommendation: Implement robust retry logic, dead-letter queues, circuit breakers, or increase downstream queue capacity/consumer throughput. Monitor network health between {component} and {downstream}.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Queue Saturation Hinders Worker Output
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} cannot enqueue messages to its downstream {downstream} queue due to saturation. This blocks the {component}'s ability to process and complete work, leading to internal backpressure or dropped tasks.

issues.cause: Excessive incoming request or task rate from upstream worker components.
issues.title: Upstream Traffic Overload
issues.rootReason: The {component} is receiving a higher volume of work than it can process, leading to resource exhaustion or queuing.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, potential queue build-up, and risk of task failures within {component} due to resource contention.
issues.recommendation: Scale out the {component}, optimize its processing efficiency, or implement rate limiting at the upstream source. Investigate the cause of the increased upstream load.

issues.cause: Resource contention or inefficient processing logic within the {component} itself.
issues.title: Internal Processing Bottleneck
issues.rootReason: Inadequate CPU, memory, or I/O resources, or inefficient execution paths are limiting the {component}'s ability to process tasks effectively.
issues.impactSummary: Degraded processing performance, increased task completion times, and potential for internal queue build-up, affecting overall system responsiveness.
issues.recommendation: Profile {component} for performance hotspots, optimize critical code paths, or scale up/out {component}'s resources to match demand.

issues.cause: Tasks are being enqueued faster than the {component} can process them, leading to a growing queue depth.
issues.title: Asynchronous Queue Backlog
issues.rootReason: A mismatch between the rate of incoming asynchronous tasks and the processing capacity of the {component}. This could be due to a surge in demand or a decrease in processing throughput.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end latency for asynchronous tasks, potential for task expiration or loss if the queue overflows, and increased resource strain on the queuing mechanism.
issues.recommendation: Increase the processing capacity of {component} (scale out), optimize individual task processing time, or investigate upstream sources for potential task rate reduction. Consider adjusting queue sizing or implementing dead-lettering strategies.

ruleIntents.name: Detect upstream overload on worker
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Heavy traffic from {source} upstream worker(s) is causing {component} to exceed its processing capacity, leading to potential delays or failures.

ruleIntents.name: Detect internal processing bottlenecks in worker
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing internal processing bottlenecks due to resource limitations or inefficient code, leading to increased latency.

ruleIntents.name: Detect asynchronous queue backlog
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The asynchronous processing queue for {component} is building up, indicating that incoming tasks are overwhelming processing capacity. This will lead to increased task completion times.

issues.cause: The worker component is unable to successfully enqueue tasks into its downstream queue, possibly due to the queue being full, slow, or unavailable.
issues.title: Downstream Queue Congestion or Failure
issues.rootReason: Insufficient capacity in the downstream queue, network issues preventing connection to the queue, or an unhealthy state of the queueing system.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, task backlog, and potential data loss or operational errors if the worker's internal buffers are exhausted or retry limits are reached.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and capacity of the downstream queue. Scale the queueing system, optimize message processing by consumers of the queue, or adjust worker retry logic.

issues.cause: The worker component is experiencing errors or high latency when calling or interacting with its downstream worker dependency.
issues.title: Downstream Worker Unhealthy or Slow
issues.rootReason: Resource exhaustion, misconfiguration, or an application-level defect in the downstream worker component.
issues.impactSummary: Increased processing time for tasks handled by {component}, potential for task failures or retries, and overall system throughput reduction.
issues.recommendation: Examine the downstream worker's logs, metrics, and configuration. Consider scaling the downstream worker or optimizing its workload.

issues.cause: The component is unable to connect to or utilize its configured failover target when the primary dependency fails, indicating a problem with the failover path itself.
issues.title: Failover Target Inaccessible or Misconfigured
issues.rootReason: Network connectivity issues to the failover target, incorrect configuration of the failover target, or the failover target itself being unhealthy.
issues.impactSummary: Loss of resiliency for {component}, leading to service interruption or full outage if the primary downstream dependency fails.
issues.recommendation: Verify network reachability to the failover target. Review the failover configuration and ensure the failover target is operational and has sufficient capacity.

issues.cause: The worker is processing tasks slower than they are arriving, leading to an increasing internal queue size, high resource utilization (CPU/memory), or thread pool exhaustion.
issues.title: Worker Internal Saturation
issues.rootReason: Under-provisioning of worker resources, inefficient processing logic, or sustained high incoming task volume exacerbated by slow downstream dependencies.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end latency for tasks, eventual task failures due to timeouts or resource exhaustion within {component}, and potential cascading failures.
issues.recommendation: Scale up the worker instances, optimize worker processing logic, or investigate downstream performance bottlenecks. Consider implementing backpressure mechanisms.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Queue Backpressure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} worker is experiencing increased latency and errors when interacting with its {downstream} queue, indicating backpressure or saturation in the queueing system.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Worker Latency/Error
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} worker is observing elevated latency or error rates when communicating with its {downstream} worker dependency.

ruleIntents.name: Failover Path Failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} worker is reporting failures when attempting to switch to its failover target, indicating the failover path is unavailable or non-functional.

ruleIntents.name: Worker Backlog Growth
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} worker is experiencing a rapidly increasing internal backlog of tasks, indicating it cannot process work at the incoming rate.

issues.recommendation: Review adjacent dependencies, retries, and failover behavior for worker.
