issues.cause: Downstream processing delays or upstream write surges.
issues.title: Queue Backlog Causing Increased Message Latency
issues.rootReason: Insufficient downstream capacity, sudden high upstream traffic, or misconfigured queue limits.
issues.impactSummary: Messages experience increased end-to-end latency, potential for message expiration or resource exhaustion on the queue, leading to degraded user experience or data loss.
issues.recommendation: Scale downstream service_mesh consumers, optimize downstream processing, or implement upstream rate limiting. Consider adjusting queue capacity or message retention policies.

issues.cause: Failure or degraded performance of the service_mesh component or its proxied consumers.
issues.title: Downstream Service Mesh Failure Affecting Queue Processing
issues.rootReason: Service mesh component failure, misconfiguration, or out-of-capacity. Could also be issues with the services routed by the mesh.
issues.impactSummary: Messages accumulate in the queue, leading to backlog, increased latency, and potential message expiration or loss if not addressed. Eventually impacts upstream if the queue fills and rejects writes.
issues.recommendation: Investigate and resolve issues within the service_mesh or the backend services it routes to. Ensure adequate capacity, health checks, and error handling mechanisms.

issues.cause: Upstream compute component generating messages at a rate higher than the queue or downstream can handle.
issues.title: Upstream Compute Overload Causing Excessive Queue Writes
issues.rootReason: Sudden surge in upstream traffic, inefficient upstream message generation, or insufficient queue/downstream processing capacity.
issues.impactSummary: Can lead to queue resource exhaustion, backpressure, message rejection at the queue, or cascading failures if the queue becomes a bottleneck.
issues.recommendation: Implement rate limiting on the compute component, scale queue resources, or scale downstream consumers. Analyze upstream traffic patterns to prevent future overload.

ruleIntents.name: HighQueueDepthBacklog
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} queue is experiencing a significant backlog due to increased writes from {upstream} or slow processing by {downstream}. This is leading to higher message latencies and potential resource exhaustion.

ruleIntents.name: DownstreamServiceMeshUnhealthy
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {downstream} service mesh component is experiencing issues, which is preventing {component} from delivering messages effectively. This may lead to message accumulation and eventual service degradation for {upstream} if the queue becomes full.

ruleIntents.name: UpstreamComputeWritePressure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {upstream} component is generating a high volume of messages, potentially overwhelming the {component} queue's capacity and processing capabilities. This could lead to message rejections or resource exhaustion on the queue.

issues.cause: Ineffective caching strategy, insufficient cache capacity, or high request churn for read-heavy workloads.
issues.title: Cache Miss Rate Exceeds Threshold
issues.rootReason: The cache is unable to serve a sufficient percentage of read requests directly, forcing a fallback to the origin data source, which increases upstream service latency and origin load.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency for upstream compute services and higher load on origin data sources, directly impacting user experience or dependent operations.
issues.recommendation: Review caching policies, increase cache capacity, optimize data access patterns for read-heavy use, or consider pre-warming the cache.

issues.cause: Resource contention within the cache component (CPU, memory), network issues, or underlying storage/hardware performance bottlenecks.
issues.title: Cache Read Latency Exceeds Threshold
issues.rootReason: The time taken to retrieve data from the cache is consistently above acceptable limits, causing direct performance degradation for dependent upstream services.
issues.impactSummary: Direct increase in response times for all upstream services depending on this cache, leading to cascading performance issues.
issues.recommendation: Scale cache resources (CPU, memory, network bandwidth), optimize cache configuration, investigate underlying infrastructure performance, or distribute read load.

issues.cause: Cache capacity insufficient for the active working set, or an overly aggressive eviction policy for a read-heavy access pattern.
issues.title: Cache Eviction Rate Exceeds Threshold
issues.rootReason: Frequently accessed data is being prematurely removed from the cache due to lack of space, leading to potential re-fetching from origin and increased cache misses.
issues.impactSummary: Can lead to an increase in cache misses, higher latency, and subsequent load on origin data sources, impacting overall system performance and cost.
issues.recommendation: Increase cache capacity, review eviction policies (e.g., LRU, LFU), or consider distributing data across multiple caches to manage the working set.

issues.cause: The downstream queue (e.g., for asynchronous writes or events) is unable to process messages at the rate they are being sent by the cache.
issues.title: Downstream Queue Exhibiting Backpressure
issues.rootReason: The buffer or processing capacity of the downstream queue is being overwhelmed, leading to increased queue depth and potential blocking or slowdowns for the cache's write operations.
issues.impactSummary: Can lead to increased write latency for the cache, potential data loss if buffers overflow, or cascading failures affecting the cache's internal operations.
issues.recommendation: Scale the downstream queue processing capacity, optimize downstream consumers, or implement rate limiting on the cache's writes to the queue.

ruleIntents.name: cache_miss_rate_check
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High cache miss rates in {component} are directly increasing the load and response times for its {upstream} dependencies, such as compute services.

ruleIntents.name: cache_read_latency_check
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Elevated read latency in {component} is causing significant delays for its {upstream} callers, impacting their overall performance.

ruleIntents.name: cache_eviction_rate_check
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High eviction rates in {component} indicate capacity pressure, potentially leading to increased cache misses and subsequent load on origin data sources from {upstream}.

ruleIntents.name: downstream_queue_backpressure_check
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Backpressure from {downstream} is impacting {component}'s ability to process or persist data, leading to increased write latency and potential resource exhaustion within the cache.

issues.cause: The DNS server is unreachable or unresponsive, preventing hostname resolution.
issues.title: DNS Service Unavailability
issues.rootReason: Failure of the DNS server host, network partition, or misconfiguration preventing access to the DNS service.
issues.impactSummary: Upstream compute services will be unable to resolve hostnames, leading to connection failures for all dependent services and application unavailability.
issues.recommendation: Verify the operational status of the DNS server, inspect network connectivity paths to the DNS server, and ensure DNS client configurations on upstream components are correct.

issues.cause: The DNS server is responding slowly to queries, delaying hostname resolution.
issues.title: High DNS Resolution Latency
issues.rootReason: Overload on the DNS server, network congestion affecting DNS query response times, or inefficient DNS server configuration/caching.
issues.impactSummary: Upstream compute services will experience delays in establishing connections to other services, leading to overall application slowdowns and potential timeouts.
issues.recommendation: Investigate DNS server resource utilization (CPU, memory, I/O), analyze network latency between clients and the DNS server, and optimize DNS server caching mechanisms. Consider scaling DNS server capacity.

ruleIntents.name: Detect DNS Unavailability Impact on Upstream Callers
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} DNS server is unavailable, preventing {source} from resolving hostnames, which impacts its ability to connect to other services.

ruleIntents.name: Detect High DNS Latency Impact on Upstream Callers
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} DNS server is experiencing high latency, causing delays in hostname resolution for {source}, which impacts its connection establishment times.

issues.cause: The downstream database service is responding slowly to queries from the application server, or is experiencing increased contention.
issues.title: Application Server experiencing increased latency due to slow database responses
issues.rootReason: Database server overload, inefficient queries, insufficient database resources, or network latency between the application server and database.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-user request latency, potential timeouts and errors, and resource contention on the application server as it waits for database responses.
issues.recommendation: Investigate database performance metrics, query optimization, and network health. Consider scaling database resources, implementing read replicas, or optimizing application data access patterns.

issues.cause: The downstream cache service is unresponsive, inaccessible, or completely unavailable to the application server.
issues.title: Application Server facing errors or increased load due to unavailable cache service
issues.rootReason: Cache service crash, network partition, misconfiguration preventing connection, or resource exhaustion on the cache service.
issues.impactSummary: Increased load on backend databases, higher request latency, and potential application errors if caching is a critical path or the application cannot gracefully handle cache misses.
issues.recommendation: Verify cache service status, network connectivity, and configuration. Implement failover mechanisms, ensure cache redundancy, or review cache eviction policies.

issues.cause: The volume of requests from upstream sources (e.g., users) exceeds the processing capacity of the application server, even with autoscaling capabilities.
issues.title: Application Server experiencing saturation from high upstream request volume
issues.rootReason: Sudden traffic surge beyond scaling limits, slow cold start for new instances, autoscaling misconfiguration, or inefficient resource utilization within the application server.
issues.impactSummary: Elevated request latency, increased error rates (e.g., 503 Service Unavailable), and potential denial of service for end-users accessing the application.
issues.recommendation: Review autoscaling configuration and policies, increase scaling limits, optimize application performance for higher concurrency, or implement rate limiting and circuit breakers at the ingress.

ruleIntents.name: Detect database latency affecting application server
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing increased request latency because its downstream database, {downstream}, is responding slowly, causing {component} to wait for query completion.

ruleIntents.name: Detect cache unavailability affecting application server
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is failing to connect to its downstream cache, {downstream}, which is causing errors or forcing {component} to rely on slower backend data sources.

ruleIntents.name: Detect upstream request saturation on application server
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is becoming saturated due to a high volume of requests originating from {upstream}, leading to degraded performance and potential request failures.

issues.cause: One or more critical downstream services (database, object store, queue) are unavailable or returning critical errors, preventing {component} from processing requests.
issues.title: Downstream Dependency Unavailability
issues.rootReason: External dependency failure, network partition, or service crash affecting {downstream}.
issues.impactSummary: {component} will experience significant request failures and potential outages until the downstream dependency is restored. Services relying on {component} will also be affected.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and availability of all downstream services, particularly database, object store, and queue. Confirm network connectivity and service status.

issues.cause: Critical downstream services (database, object store, queue) are responding slowly, causing requests processed by {component} to exceed acceptable latency thresholds.
issues.title: Downstream Dependency Latency
issues.rootReason: Resource contention, query inefficiencies, or overloaded state in the {downstream} dependency.
issues.impactSummary: {component} will exhibit slow response times, leading to a degraded user experience. This can cascade to upstream services and potentially cause timeouts.
issues.recommendation: Examine the performance metrics of the database, object store, and queue services. Optimize queries, increase capacity, or reduce load on the affected {downstream} service.

issues.cause: The high fanout characteristic of {component} combined with increased request volume is exceeding the capacity of its {downstream} dependencies (database, object store, queue), leading to resource contention and performance issues downstream.
issues.title: App Server Overloading Downstream
issues.rootReason: Insufficient capacity in {downstream}, inefficient request patterns from {component}, or unexpected traffic spike.
issues.impactSummary: {downstream} services will experience severe performance degradation or availability issues, which will eventually feedback to {component} as increased latency and errors. This can lead to a cascading failure.
issues.recommendation: Implement client-side rate limiting, circuit breakers, or backpressure mechanisms in {component}. Review and optimize downstream resource allocation and query patterns. Consider increasing {downstream} capacity or sharding.

issues.cause: The {component} is attempting to autoscale in response to increased load, but a downstream dependency (database, object store, queue) is hitting its capacity limits, becoming a bottleneck and preventing effective scaling, leading to overall system performance degradation.
issues.title: Autoscaling Hindered by Downstream Bottleneck
issues.rootReason: Inadequate {downstream} capacity, unoptimized {downstream} operations, or unexpected high demand from {component} during scaling events.
issues.impactSummary: Even with autoscaling enabled for {component}, the system will experience performance degradation and potential availability issues as demand increases, because {downstream} cannot keep up. This defeats the purpose of horizontal scaling for {component}.
issues.recommendation: Identify and address the bottleneck in the {downstream} dependency. This may involve scaling {downstream} independently, optimizing its performance, or implementing stricter rate limits/throttling from {component} during scaling events.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Service Unavailable
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing availability issues due to an outage or critical failure in its {downstream} dependency.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Service Latency Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing performance degradation due to high latency from its {downstream} dependency, impacting request processing times.

ruleIntents.name: High Fanout Overloading Downstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component}'s high fanout and request volume are overloading the {downstream} dependency, leading to performance degradation or resource exhaustion in {downstream}.

ruleIntents.name: Autoscale Constrained by Downstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: While {component} attempts to autoscale to meet demand, the {downstream} dependency is becoming a bottleneck, preventing effective scaling and leading to performance issues.

issues.cause: Excessive latency from a critical downstream dependency (e.g., database, cache, object store, queue).
issues.title: Downstream Service Latency
issues.rootReason: Resource contention, network issues, slow queries/operations on the downstream service.
issues.impactSummary: Increased response times for {component}, potential timeouts, and user experience degradation.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the specific downstream service for performance bottlenecks. Consider optimizing queries, increasing resources, or caching strategies.

issues.cause: A critical downstream dependency is completely offline or unreachable.
issues.title: Downstream Service Unavailability
issues.rootReason: Service crash, network partition, misconfiguration, or deployment failure.
issues.impactSummary: Complete or partial service outage for {component}, leading to functional failures.
issues.recommendation: Restore or repair the unavailable downstream service. Implement robust retry mechanisms and circuit breakers in {component}.

issues.cause: {component} is unable to handle the volume of fan-out requests or retries to slow/failed downstream services, exhausting its own resources (e.g., connections, threads, memory).
issues.title: High Fanout Resource Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: Downstream service degradation/failure combined with high fanout and potentially insufficient resource limits or inadequate circuit breaking/rate limiting.
issues.impactSummary: Performance degradation or cascading failure of {component} itself, even if individual downstream services are not entirely down.
issues.recommendation: Implement more aggressive circuit breaking, backpressure mechanisms, and graceful degradation strategies. Review and adjust resource limits for {component}. Investigate and resolve downstream issues.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Slowdown Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Increased latency from {downstream} is causing {component} to experience performance degradation.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Outage Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The unavailability of {downstream} is causing {component} to experience a service outage.

ruleIntents.name: High Fanout Overload from Downstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High fanout combined with degradation in {downstream} services is causing {component} to exhaust its local resources.

issues.cause: Upstream services writing to the queue are producing messages faster than the queue can safely buffer, persist, or process, leading to increased queue depth and potential resource exhaustion.
issues.title: Queue Backpressure
issues.rootReason: Insufficient queue capacity (e.g., memory, disk I/O, network) on the queue component, or internal queue mechanisms are unable to handle the incoming write load.
issues.impactSummary: Messages may be rejected or lost, causing data integrity issues or service disruptions for upstream producers. Higher latency for message writes.
issues.recommendation: Scale up queue resources (e.g., memory, disk I/O, network bandwidth), implement flow control on upstream producers, or consider sharding the queue.

issues.cause: Messages are remaining in the queue for an unacceptably long period, indicating a build-up of backlog or slow internal processing.
issues.title: Excessive Message Latency
issues.rootReason: Inadequate processing capacity within the queue system itself, or an unexpected surge in upstream production without proportional increase in message consumption (even if not explicitly modeled).
issues.impactSummary: Delayed processing of critical tasks or data, leading to stale information in dependent systems and degraded overall system responsiveness.
issues.recommendation: Increase internal queue processing resources, optimize message processing logic, or investigate consumer scaling/performance (if applicable).

ruleIntents.name: Detect Queue Backpressure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Queue {component} is experiencing high backpressure, indicating that upstream components ({upstream}) are producing messages faster than the queue can buffer. This will lead to increased latency for message writes and potential message rejection by {component}.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Excessive Message Latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Messages in queue {component} are experiencing excessive latency, suggesting a build-up of unacknowledged messages or slow internal processing. This impacts the timely delivery and processing of data originating from {upstream}.

issues.cause: High query latency, resource exhaustion, or increased error rates.
issues.title: Database Performance Degradation
issues.rootReason: Inefficient queries, high contention, insufficient capacity, or underlying infrastructure issues.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency for all operations, potential cascading failures upstream due to timeouts.
issues.recommendation: Optimize queries, scale resources, review indexing strategy, investigate infrastructure.

issues.cause: Database overload or high error rates leading to activation of a circuit breaker on an upstream component.
issues.title: Database Circuit Breaker Tripped
issues.rootReason: Sustained database performance degradation, resource exhaustion, or critical internal failures.
issues.impactSummary: Upstream requests to the database fail fast, reducing database load but increasing upstream error rates.
issues.recommendation: Investigate database health, analyze logs, scale resources, and ensure upstream retry policies are robust.

issues.cause: High write volume on primary, network latency, or resource constraints on replica instance.
issues.title: Database Replica Lag
issues.rootReason: Primary database write pressure, network bottleneck, or insufficient replica capacity.
issues.impactSummary: Stale data served by replica, potential data loss during failover if lag is significant, inconsistent reads.
issues.recommendation: Optimize primary writes, scale replica resources, improve network connectivity, monitor replication health.

issues.cause: Primary database failure and inability to successfully transition to a failover target.
issues.title: Database Failover Failure
issues.rootReason: Failover target unhealthiness, network issues preventing failover, misconfiguration, or inadequate failover automation.
issues.impactSummary: Prolonged database unavailability during primary failures, complete service interruption.
issues.recommendation: Verify failover target health, review network connectivity, test failover procedures regularly, automate failover processes.

ruleIntents.name: Database Latency Spike Affecting Upstream
ruleIntents.impactShape: Increased {component} latency leading to increased {upstream} errors.
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Database {component} is experiencing high query latency, causing {upstream} services to time out or receive delayed responses.

ruleIntents.name: Database Error Rate Spike Affecting Upstream
ruleIntents.impactShape: Increased {component} error rate leading to increased {upstream} errors.
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Database {component} is experiencing a high rate of errors (e.g., connection failures, query failures), causing {upstream} services to fail.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Circuit Breaker to Database Tripped
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The upstream {source} component's circuit breaker to database {component} has tripped due to high error rates or timeouts, protecting the database but causing requests from {source} to fail fast.

ruleIntents.name: Database Replica Lag Detected
ruleIntents.impactShape: Increased replica lag for {component} potentially leading to stale reads for {downstream} consumers.
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Database {component} is experiencing replica lag, causing potential data staleness for {downstream} services consuming replicated data.

ruleIntents.name: Database Failover Target Unhealthy
ruleIntents.impactShape: The failover target for {component} is unhealthy, jeopardizing availability during primary failure.
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The failover target for database {component} is currently unhealthy or unreachable, meaning that a primary failure would result in extended downtime.

issues.cause: One or more instances of the downstream 'api_gateway' service are failing health checks or are reported as unhealthy.
issues.title: Downstream Service Unhealthy
issues.rootReason: Lack of responsiveness or failed internal tests from a target downstream service, indicating it cannot process requests.
issues.impactSummary: The network load balancer will cease sending traffic to unhealthy downstream instances. If too many instances are unhealthy, overall service availability will degrade, potentially causing client-facing errors or service unavailability.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health of the 'api_gateway' instances. This may involve checking resource utilization, application logs, or redeploying the affected services.

issues.cause: One or more instances of the downstream 'api_gateway' service are experiencing high load, high latency, or resource exhaustion.
issues.title: Downstream Service Overloaded
issues.rootReason: The downstream service is receiving more requests than it can efficiently handle, leading to performance degradation or potential failures.
issues.impactSummary: The network load balancer may route traffic away from overloaded instances or shed excess load, leading to increased latency or errors for upstream clients if there isn't sufficient healthy capacity elsewhere.
issues.recommendation: Scale out the 'api_gateway' service, optimize its performance, or implement rate limiting/throttling mechanisms. Review traffic patterns to identify spikes.

assumptions: The networkLoadBalancer is configured with health checks for its downstream services.
The networkLoadBalancer can dynamically adjust its routing based on downstream health and performance metrics.
Downstream 'api_gateway' instances report relevant metrics (e.g., CPU, response time, health status).

ruleIntents.name: RedirectTrafficFromUnhealthyDownstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} detected an unhealthy state in its {downstream} service, causing it to remove the instance from its active target group and re-route traffic.

ruleIntents.name: ReduceTrafficToOverloadedDownstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} detected high resource utilization or increased latency in its {downstream} service, leading it to prioritize traffic to healthier instances or reduce overall throughput to prevent cascading failures.

issues.cause: Data not found in cache, often due to insufficient capacity, improper eviction policies, or a sudden shift in access patterns.
issues.title: High Cache Miss Rate Detected
issues.rootReason: Reduced cache effectiveness, leading to increased demand on downstream database.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency for operations relying on cached data, higher load and potential performance degradation on downstream databases.
issues.recommendation: Optimize cache capacity, review eviction policies, analyze data access patterns, or scale cache infrastructure.

issues.cause: Uneven distribution of requests across cache shards/partitions, often due to a few keys receiving disproportionately high traffic or an ineffective sharding key.
issues.title: Cache Hot Shard/Partition Detected
issues.rootReason: Workload imbalance across cache infrastructure.
issues.impactSummary: Performance degradation on affected shards, potential for resource exhaustion (CPU, memory, network) on hot nodes, and increased latency for requests targeting those shards.
issues.recommendation: Re-evaluate sharding strategy, identify and mitigate hot keys, consider dynamic re-sharding or partitioning to distribute load more evenly.

issues.cause: The upstream 'compute' service is sending more requests than the cache can gracefully handle, leading to internal queueing, increased latency, or error responses.
issues.title: Cache Overload from Upstream Compute
issues.rootReason: Insufficient capacity of the cache or excessive, unthrottled demand from upstream services.
issues.impactSummary: Severe degradation of cache performance, potential for cascading failures if the cache becomes unresponsive, and increased error rates for upstream callers.
issues.recommendation: Scale the cache component, implement rate limiting on the cache itself, or advise upstream services to reduce their request rate or implement backpressure mechanisms.

ruleIntents.name: Detect High Cache Miss Rate
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} cache is experiencing a high miss rate, leading to increased latency for {upstream} requests and higher load on the {downstream} database.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Cache Hot Shard
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: A hot shard has been detected in the {component} cache, causing performance degradation and increased latency for requests routed to it from {upstream}.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Upstream Compute Overload
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} cache is experiencing an overload due to excessive requests from {upstream}, leading to increased latency and error rates.

issues.cause: High latency introduced by the upstream load balancer.
issues.title: Upstream Load Balancer Latency Impacts App Server Response Time
issues.rootReason: Overload, misconfiguration, or degraded health of the upstream load balancer, which is critical for synchronous request paths.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end request latency for all traffic flowing through the app server, potentially leading to client timeouts, degraded user experience, and cascading performance issues for downstream components.
issues.recommendation: Examine the health and performance metrics of the upstream load balancer. Review its configuration for potential bottlenecks or suboptimal settings. Consider scaling up or out the load balancer capacity to handle current demand.

issues.cause: The downstream load balancer is unavailable or unresponsive.
issues.title: Downstream Load Balancer Failure Causes App Server Outage
issues.rootReason: Complete failure, unrecoverable error, or severe resource exhaustion of the downstream load balancer, preventing communication with backend services.
issues.impactSummary: All requests from the app server to backend services routed through this load balancer will fail, leading to critical service unavailability for the app server and its clients.
issues.recommendation: Immediately investigate the status and logs of the downstream load balancer. Confirm its operational state and verify dependencies. Initiate failover or recovery procedures if available for the load balancer.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Load Balancer Latency Propagation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Increased latency in {upstream} (Load Balancer) directly impacts the response time of {component} (App Server), as it's part of a synchronous request path.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Load Balancer Failure Propagation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Failure of the {downstream} (Load Balancer) prevents {component} (App Server) from successfully sending requests to its backend services, leading to complete service unavailability.

issues.cause: High write volume exceeding database capacity or inefficient write operations.
issues.title: Database Write Overload
issues.rootReason: Sustained high write-request load from downstream services or internal database contention due to write-heavy operations.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency for write operations, potential data loss or corruption, and service unavailability for applications dependent on the database.
issues.recommendation: Optimize database schema for write operations, scale database resources (IOPS, CPU, memory), or distribute write load across multiple database instances. Review application write patterns from downstream consumers.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Write-Heavy Database Overload from Downstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} database is experiencing write overload due to high write contention or excessive write requests from its {downstream} consumers.

issues.cause: Increased response times from an upstream database dependency.
issues.title: Serverless component experiencing high latency due to slow upstream database
issues.rootReason: Database performance degradation, resource contention, or inefficient queries.
issues.impactSummary: Users will experience slower response times when interacting with features relying on this serverless component.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the performance of the upstream database ({source}). Optimize database queries or scale database resources.

issues.cause: High volume of incoming requests from an upstream traffic dependency exceeding current processing capacity or scaling limits.
issues.title: Serverless component under excessive load from upstream traffic source
issues.rootReason: Unexpected traffic surge, inefficient request processing, or delayed autoscaling activation.
issues.impactSummary: Users may experience increased latency, request timeouts, or errors as the serverless component struggles to process requests.
issues.recommendation: Review traffic patterns from {source}. Optimize serverless function code for efficiency. Verify autoscaling configuration and resource limits.

issues.cause: Loss of connectivity or complete failure of an essential upstream database dependency.
issues.title: Serverless component unavailable due to upstream database failure
issues.rootReason: Database server crash, network partition, or critical data corruption.
issues.impactSummary: The serverless component will be unable to serve requests, leading to complete service unavailability for affected features.
issues.recommendation: Immediately investigate the health and status of the upstream database ({source}). Initiate database recovery procedures or failover to a redundant instance.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Database Latency Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Increased latency from upstream database {source} is causing {component} to experience elevated response times.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Traffic Overload
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Excessive traffic from upstream {source} is overwhelming {component}, potentially leading to increased latency or errors.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Database Failure

issues.cause: Increased network latency or load on CDN infrastructure impacting content delivery speed for read-heavy workloads.
issues.title: CDN Performance Degradation
issues.rootReason: Network congestion
issues.impactSummary: Increased load times and degraded user experience for applications served by the CDN, directly affecting dependent 'compute' services.
issues.recommendation: Monitor CDN network performance metrics and resource utilization. Consider scaling capacity or optimizing content delivery paths.

issues.cause: Failure of CDN edge nodes, network connectivity to edge locations, or internal CDN service components.
issues.title: CDN Availability Loss
issues.rootReason: Infrastructure failure
issues.impactSummary: Direct unavailability of cached content or endpoints, leading to service outages for 'compute' components relying on the CDN, severely impacting read-heavy requests.
issues.recommendation: Investigate specific CDN edge node status, network health, and failover mechanisms. Implement traffic rerouting.

issues.cause: Misconfiguration of cache invalidation policies or failure in the cache refresh mechanism for read-heavy assets.
issues.title: CDN Stale Cache Content
issues.rootReason: Configuration error
issues.impactSummary: Downstream 'compute' services receive outdated information, potentially leading to data inconsistencies or incorrect application logic.
issues.recommendation: Review and update cache control headers and invalidation strategies. Implement more robust cache validation.

ruleIntents.name: CDN Performance Degradation propagates to Downstream Compute
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Performance degradation in {component} is increasing latency and impacting user experience for connected {downstream} services, specifically affecting read-heavy content delivery.

ruleIntents.name: CDN Availability Loss propagates to Downstream Compute
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Availability loss in {component} is causing direct service disruption for {downstream} services, especially impacting their ability to serve read-heavy content.

ruleIntents.name: CDN Stale Cache impacts Downstream Compute

issues.cause: The DNS server is unable to successfully query its configured upstream DNS resolvers, possibly due to network connectivity issues or upstream resolver failure.
issues.title: DNS Upstream Resolver Unreachable or Failing
issues.rootReason: NETWORK_CONNECTIVITY_ISSUE
issues.impactSummary: DNS queries initiated by this server will fail or experience significant delays, leading to resolution failures for downstream clients.
issues.recommendation: Verify network connectivity from this DNS server to its upstream resolvers. Inspect logs of upstream DNS resolvers for errors or high latency. Consider configuring redundant upstream resolvers.

issues.cause: The DNS server's circuit breaker has opened due to a high rate of failures or timeouts when attempting to resolve queries from upstream DNS servers.
issues.title: DNS Resolver Circuit Breaker Tripped
issues.rootReason: UPSTREAM_COMPONENT_FAILURE
issues.impactSummary: DNS queries will fail immediately without attempting to contact upstream resolvers, leading to resolution failures for downstream clients. While protecting the DNS server from overload, this indicates a severe upstream issue.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the root cause of upstream DNS failures that led to the circuit breaker trip. Review circuit breaker configuration thresholds. Monitor for potential upstream flapping or overload conditions.

issues.cause: The DNS server is experiencing high CPU, memory, or network I/O utilization, or hitting query limits, leading to degraded performance or unresponsiveness.
issues.title: DNS Server Resource Exhaustion or Overload
issues.rootReason: RESOURCE_EXHAUSTION
issues.impactSummary: DNS queries will experience high latency, timeouts, or outright failures, severely impacting any downstream services that rely on this DNS server for name resolution.
issues.recommendation: Scale out DNS server instances, optimize DNS configuration, investigate sudden traffic spikes, or address underlying resource bottlenecks.

ruleIntents.name: PropagateUpstreamDNSFailure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing {issueCode} because its upstream DNS resolvers are unavailable, impacting {downstream} clients.

ruleIntents.name: CircuitBreakerProtectionImpact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component}'s internal circuit breaker has tripped due to persistent upstream failures, preventing it from resolving queries and impacting {downstream} clients.

ruleIntents.name: LocalDNSOverloadImpact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is overloaded due to high resource utilization, causing slow DNS resolution and impacting {downstream} clients.

issues.cause: Inefficient caching strategy, insufficient cache capacity, frequently changing data, or non-optimal key distribution for a sharded cache.
issues.title: High Cache Miss Rate
issues.rootReason: Data access patterns are not suitable for current cache configuration or capacity.
issues.impactSummary: Increased load on origin data sources, higher latency for read operations, potential performance bottlenecks for upstream services.
issues.recommendation: Review cache eviction policy, increase cache capacity, re-evaluate data hotness, optimize key hashing for sharding.

issues.cause: Uneven distribution of keys/traffic across shards, leading to some shards being overloaded while others are underutilized.
issues.title: Cache Shard Imbalance
issues.rootReason: Hashing function not distributing keys uniformly, or specific keys receiving disproportionately high traffic.
issues.impactSummary: Performance degradation on hot shards, increased latency, potential for shard-specific failures, and inefficient resource utilization across the cache cluster.
issues.recommendation: Re-evaluate sharding key strategy, consider dynamic re-sharding, or introduce a load balancing layer aware of shard utilization.

issues.cause: Resource contention (CPU/memory), network issues, high request volume, or slow underlying storage if persistent.
issues.title: Elevated Cache Read/Write Latency
issues.rootReason: Cache component is struggling to process requests efficiently.
issues.impactSummary: Slower responses to upstream compute services, leading to increased overall system latency and potential timeouts.
issues.recommendation: Monitor resource utilization, optimize network configuration, scale cache instances, or investigate potential blocking operations.

ruleIntents.name: Detect High Cache Miss Rate
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing a high cache miss rate, leading to increased load on its downstream data sources and degraded performance for {upstream} services.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Cache Shard Imbalance
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: A specific shard within {component} is experiencing disproportionately high load, potentially impacting availability and performance for {upstream} requests routed to that shard.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Cache Latency Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing elevated latency, directly impacting the response times of {upstream} compute services relying on it.

issues.cause: The primary database instance is unresponsive or has failed, preventing write operations and potentially disrupting read operations if replicas cannot serve requests or failover is delayed.
issues.title: Primary Database Unavailability
issues.rootReason: Hardware failure, software crash, network partition, or critical resource exhaustion on the primary database server.
issues.impactSummary: Severe disruption to write operations. Potential read disruption or increased read latency. Upstream services will experience errors and timeouts.
issues.recommendation: Investigate primary database logs for errors. Initiate failover to a replica if automatic failover has not occurred. If a failover target is unavailable, restore from backup.

issues.cause: One or more database replicas are unresponsive, slow, or have stopped replicating from the primary, reducing read capacity and impacting failover readiness.
issues.title: Database Replica Degradation
issues.rootReason: Hardware failure, software crash, network issues, or resource exhaustion on a replica database server. Replication lag due to high load or misconfiguration.
issues.impactSummary: Reduced read capacity, potentially leading to increased read latency or load on the primary. Compromised failover capabilities if the affected replica was a failover target.
issues.recommendation: Investigate replica logs for errors. Restart failed replicas. Check replication status and resolve any lag issues. If necessary, provision new replicas.

issues.cause: The database is experiencing excessive write load, leading to high write latency, resource exhaustion (CPU, disk I/O), and potential service degradation for write operations.
issues.title: Database Write Saturation
issues.rootReason: Unexpected surge in write requests, inefficient queries, lack of indexing, insufficient database resources, or storage I/O bottleneck.
issues.impactSummary: Significantly increased latency for write operations, potentially causing timeouts and errors for upstream services. May impact overall database responsiveness.
issues.recommendation: Identify and optimize high-impact write queries. Scale database resources (CPU, memory, I/O). Consider sharding or using a write-optimized database solution. Implement rate limiting for writes.

issues.cause: The designated failover target database is unhealthy, unavailable, or experiencing significant replication lag, compromising the database's ability to maintain high availability.
issues.title: Database Failover Target Unhealthy
issues.rootReason: Hardware failure, network isolation, resource exhaustion on the failover target, or replication issues preventing the failover target from being up-to-date.
issues.impactSummary: Increased risk of extended downtime during a primary database failure. Potential data loss if the failover target is not fully synchronized. Reduced confidence in disaster recovery capabilities.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and replication status of the failover target. Restore or rebuild the failover target if necessary. Ensure network connectivity and sufficient resources.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Primary Database Unavailability impacting Upstream Services
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} database is unavailable, leading to failed requests for {upstream} services. This impacts write operations and potentially read operations.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Replica Database Degradation affecting Read Capacity
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: A {component} database replica is degraded or unavailable, potentially affecting read performance for {upstream} services and increasing load on the primary.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Database Write Saturation impacting Upstream Services
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} database is saturated with write operations, leading to high latency and errors for {upstream} services.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Unhealthy Database Failover Target
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} database's failover target is unhealthy or unavailable, which increases the risk of downtime for {upstream} services in the event of a primary failure.

issues.impactSummary: Increased latency for read and write operations, potential timeouts, and service unavailability.
issues.recommendation: Optimize write queries, implement database-level write throttling, consider write-optimized storage or sharding. Review upstream {upstream} components for excessive write traffic.

issues.impactSummary: Data inconsistency between primary and replicas, potential for stale reads, and slower failover times.
issues.recommendation: Monitor replication health, optimize network between primary and replicas, provision more powerful replicas, consider logical replication or sharding to reduce replication burden.

issues.cause: The secrets manager component is unable to retrieve or serve secrets.
issues.title: Secrets Manager Unavailable
issues.rootReason: Underlying infrastructure failure, misconfiguration, or resource exhaustion preventing the secrets manager from operating.
issues.impactSummary: Downstream components, particularly API Gateways, will fail to retrieve necessary secrets, leading to service disruption.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and configuration of the secrets manager. Check logs for errors related to secret retrieval or service availability. Ensure adequate resources are allocated.

issues.cause: Misconfiguration, corruption, or expiration of secrets stored in the secrets manager.
issues.title: Secrets Manager Serving Corrupted or Incorrect Secrets
issues.rootReason: Incorrect secret values, expired credentials, or data corruption within the secrets manager system, leading to invalid data being served.
issues.impactSummary: Downstream components will fail to authenticate or authorize requests, leading to functional failures and potential security breaches.
issues.recommendation: Verify the integrity and correctness of secrets stored in the secrets manager. Review recent changes to secret configurations or deployments. Rollback to a known good state if possible.

ruleIntents.name: Secrets Manager Availability Monitoring
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} secrets manager is unavailable, preventing {downstream} from retrieving necessary secrets and causing service disruption.

ruleIntents.name: Secrets Manager Data Integrity Monitoring
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} secrets manager is serving corrupted or incorrect secrets, causing {downstream} to experience functional failures related to authentication or authorization.

issues.cause: One or more downstream compute instances are reporting unhealthy status or failing health checks.
issues.title: Downstream Compute Targets Unhealthy
issues.rootReason: Degradation or failure of application instances hosted on compute nodes behind the load balancer.
issues.impactSummary: Reduced capacity, increased latency, or complete service outage if all downstream targets are unhealthy.
issues.recommendation: Investigate health of downstream compute instances. Check application logs, resource utilization, and ensure correct health check configuration.

issues.cause: Incoming request rate or connection count exceeds the operational limits of the load balancer, or configured thresholds.
issues.title: Load Balancer Approaching Capacity Limits
issues.rootReason: Surge in upstream traffic from {upstream} or insufficient capacity provisioning for the load balancer itself.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency for requests, connection timeouts, and potential service disruption as the load balancer struggles to process traffic.
issues.recommendation: Scale out the load balancer capacity (if applicable), investigate upstream traffic patterns, or consider applying rate limiting upstream.

issues.cause: The configured failover target for the load balancer is unresponsive or cannot be reached.
issues.title: Failover Target for Load Balancer Unreachable
issues.rootReason: Network connectivity issues to the failover target, or the failover target itself is down.
issues.impactSummary: Loss of redundancy, making the load balancer a single point of failure. Future incidents could lead to broader service outages.
issues.recommendation: Verify network path and health of the designated failover target. Review failover configuration.

issues.cause: One or more replicas of the load balancer are reporting unhealthy status or experiencing operational issues.
issues.title: Load Balancer Replica Health Degraded
issues.rootReason: Issues with individual replica instances, underlying infrastructure, or synchronization problems.
issues.impactSummary: Reduced redundancy and availability. Potential for service disruption if primary instances also fail without healthy replicas to take over.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the specific unhealthy replica instances. Check logs, resource utilization, and replication status.

ruleIntents.name: Detect unhealthy downstream compute instances
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The load balancer detected that one or more {downstream} compute instances are failing health checks, preventing traffic distribution.

ruleIntents.name: Detect load balancer resource exhaustion
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The load balancer is experiencing high resource utilization due to an excessive request load from {upstream}.

ruleIntents.name: Detect unreachable failover target
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The load balancer's designated failover target is unreachable, compromising its resilience.

ruleIntents.name: Detect degraded replica health
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: A replica of the load balancer is reporting unhealthy status, reducing system redundancy.

issues.cause: Downstream compute or load balancer services are unresponsive or offline.
issues.title: CDN Origin Server Unavailability
issues.rootReason: Failure or overload of {downstream} services, preventing content retrieval.
issues.impactSummary: CDN will be unable to fetch fresh content or serve cache misses, leading to increased latency, stale content, or errors for end-users relying on dynamic or uncached content.
issues.recommendation: Investigate health and capacity of {downstream} components (e.g., origin servers, load balancers). Ensure adequate redundancy and failover mechanisms are in place for origins.

issues.cause: Downstream compute or load balancer services are responding slowly.
issues.title: CDN Origin Server Performance Degradation
issues.rootReason: High latency or resource exhaustion within {downstream} services.
issues.impactSummary: CDN cache misses will experience increased latency due to slow origin responses, impacting overall user experience and potentially increasing CDN resource utilization due to longer connection times.
issues.recommendation: Analyze performance metrics and logs for {downstream} components. Consider scaling up resources or optimizing queries/operations on origin servers.

ruleIntents.name: Monitor Downstream Origin Unavailability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing a critical failure due to issues with its {downstream} origin services. This prevents {component} from retrieving fresh content or responding to cache misses, leading to errors for end-users.

ruleIntents.name: Monitor Downstream Origin Performance Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing degraded performance due to slow responses from its {downstream} origin services. This increases latency for content served from {component} during cache misses.

ruleIntents.name: Cache Efficiency Impact by Downstream Performance
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Slower responses from {downstream} origins are causing {component} to maintain connections longer and potentially reduce cache hit ratios, increasing operational overhead and impacting resource utilization.

issues.cause: One or more downstream service instances are failing health checks or reporting errors to the load balancer.
issues.title: Downstream backend instance(s) unhealthy
issues.rootReason: Application error, resource exhaustion, or deployment issue in a downstream service.
issues.impactSummary: Increased error rates for requests routed to unhealthy backends, potential service degradation or outage if many backends are affected.
issues.recommendation: Investigate and resolve issues in affected downstream service instances. Consider auto-scaling or failover strategies for the downstream pool.

issues.cause: High traffic volume, complex request processing, or resource contention causing the load balancer itself to reach its resource limits (CPU, memory, network I/O).
issues.title: Load Balancer approaching capacity limits
issues.rootReason: Insufficient load balancer capacity for current demand or an unexpected traffic surge.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, connection resets, request drops, and potential service unavailability originating from the load balancer itself.
issues.recommendation: Scale up load balancer resources, optimize traffic patterns, or consider a different load balancing solution. Implement robust upstream rate limiting.

issues.cause: An excessive request rate or large request payloads from upstream services, causing the load balancer to buffer more or spend more time processing data.
issues.title: High upstream request rate causing internal load balancer latency
issues.rootReason: Upstream service misbehavior, fan-out issues, or an unmanaged traffic spike originating from an upstream source.
issues.impactSummary: Increased internal load balancer processing time and queue depth, leading to higher end-to-end latency for requests.
issues.recommendation: Implement rate limiting at the upstream service or at the load balancer. Optimize upstream request patterns, or scale load balancer resources.

issues.cause: High latency in one or more downstream services, directly delaying the response from the load balancer back to its upstream caller due to synchronous request path.
issues.title: Downstream service latency impacting overall request duration
issues.rootReason: Performance bottleneck, resource contention, or slow operations within the downstream service responsible for fulfilling the request.
issues.impactSummary: Elevated end-to-end request latency observed by upstream services, potentially leading to upstream timeouts and degraded user experience.
issues.recommendation: Optimize or scale the downstream service. Implement aggressive timeouts and circuit breakers on the load balancer to isolate slow dependencies.

ruleIntents.name: Load Balancer Saturation Detection

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Request Rate Impact

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Latency Propagation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Elevated latency in {downstream} services is propagating through {component}, causing {upstream} to experience increased response times for synchronous requests.

issues.cause: One or more compute instances registered with the network load balancer are failing health checks or are otherwise unavailable to serve traffic.
issues.title: Downstream Compute Backend Unhealthy or Unavailable
issues.rootReason: Application crash, resource exhaustion, network isolation, deployment issues on compute instances.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, failed requests, or service degradation for requests routed through the load balancer, potentially leading to service unavailability if too many backends fail. Upstream services will experience increased error rates.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and operational status of downstream compute instances. Check logs for errors, resource utilization, and network connectivity. Ensure health checks are correctly configured and target appropriate endpoints.

issues.cause: The network load balancer is experiencing high resource utilization (e.g., CPU, memory, connection limits, packet processing capacity) due to a surge in traffic or misconfiguration.
issues.title: Network Load Balancer Resource Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: Unexpected traffic spikes, DoS attack, inefficient routing rules, insufficient capacity provisioning, configuration errors.
issues.impactSummary: Increased request latency, connection timeouts, and dropped connections for client requests. Upstream services will observe higher error rates and degraded performance when attempting to connect.
issues.recommendation: Investigate traffic patterns and resource metrics on the load balancer. Consider scaling up or out the load balancer capacity, optimizing routing rules, or implementing rate limiting. Check for potential upstream misbehavior or malicious traffic.

issues.cause: Requests traversing the network load balancer are experiencing higher than normal end-to-end latency, potentially due to network congestion, backend slowdowns, or load balancer processing delays.
issues.title: Elevated Request Latency Through Network Load Balancer
issues.rootReason: Network congestion between NLB and backends, slow processing at downstream compute, overloaded NLB, transient network issues.
issues.impactSummary: Synchronous requests serviced by the load balancer will experience significant delays, leading to degraded user experience, potential timeouts for upstream services, and cascading failures in tightly coupled systems.
issues.recommendation: Examine latency metrics on the network load balancer, its upstream connections, and its downstream connections. Pinpoint whether the delay originates within the NLB, on the network path, or at the backend servers. Consider optimizing backend performance or reconfiguring network paths.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Compute Unavailability Detection
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} (network load balancer) is reporting that its downstream {downstream} compute instances are unhealthy or unavailable, causing failures for client requests.

ruleIntents.name: Network Load Balancer Overload Detection
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} (network load balancer) is experiencing resource exhaustion, leading to increased latency and dropped connections, which affects {upstream} services.

ruleIntents.name: High Request Latency Detection
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} (network load balancer) is observing elevated latency for requests, likely originating from its {downstream} dependencies or internal processing, impacting {upstream} services.

issues.cause: A critical synchronous downstream dependency (e.g., database, cache) is experiencing high latency or errors.
issues.title: Performance Degradation Due to Synchronous Downstream Dependency
issues.rootReason: The {component} is unable to fulfill requests promptly due to a synchronous dependency on a degraded {downstream} component. This can lead to thread pool exhaustion, connection timeouts, and increased request queueing, as indicated by the 'is_sync_request_path' trait.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency and error rates for requests processed by {component}, potentially leading to cascading failures upstream to components like load balancers.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the performance of the downstream {downstream} components. Consider implementing circuit breakers or retries with backoff for resilient interaction patterns. Optimize database queries or cache access patterns.

issues.cause: An upstream component (e.g., load_balancer) is sending excessive requests, overwhelming the {component}.
issues.title: Overload Due to Excessive Upstream Traffic
issues.rootReason: The {component} is unable to process the incoming request volume from {upstream}, leading to resource exhaustion (CPU, memory, network I/O) and degradation of service. While the 'autoscale' trait can help, initial spikes or sustained high load before scaling completes can still cause severe issues.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, error rates, and potential unavailability for requests handled by {component}. This can lead to a negative feedback loop if upstream components retry failed requests.
issues.recommendation: Implement rate limiting or throttling mechanisms on {component}. Adjust autoscaling policies to be more responsive or provision for higher baseline capacity. Investigate the traffic patterns and configuration of the upstream {upstream} component.

issues.cause: An upstream component identified as 'sketchy' is exhibiting unreliable behavior, such as sending malformed requests or having intermittent connectivity.
issues.title: Unreliable Upstream Causing Local Errors
issues.rootReason: The {component} is receiving problematic requests or encountering connection issues from the 'sketchy' {upstream} component, leading to increased internal error rates and potentially consuming resources for error handling or retries. This degrades the local service health.
issues.impactSummary: Increased internal error rates and resource consumption on {component}. May lead to a reduction in effective throughput as valid requests are delayed by processing faulty ones.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the behavior of the 'sketchy' upstream {upstream} component. Implement robust input validation and error handling in {component}. Consider circuit breaking or adaptive load shedding for requests originating from unreliable sources.

ruleIntents.name: Synchronous Downstream Dependency Slowdown Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: When a synchronous downstream dependency ({downstream}) experiences performance degradation, {component} will exhibit increased latency and errors as it waits for or fails to connect to the dependency. This impact then propagates to its callers ({upstream}) which include load_balancer and sketchy components.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Traffic Overload Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: When an upstream component ({upstream}), such as a load_balancer or a sketchy source, sends an excessive volume of requests, {component} can become overwhelmed, leading to resource exhaustion, increased latency, and errors. This directly affects the service quality provided by {component} locally.

ruleIntents.name: Sketchy Upstream Unreliability Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: When an upstream component ({upstream}) designated as 'sketchy' exhibits unreliable behavior (e.g., malformed requests, intermittent connection issues), {component} will experience an increase in internal errors and potentially increased resource utilization due to error handling and retries.

issues.cause: The network rate limiter has exceeded its configured request threshold, often due to a sudden surge in traffic, misconfigured limits, or an upstream service attempting to send too many requests.
issues.title: Rate Limit Exceeded
issues.rootReason: Overload or misconfiguration leading to active rate limiting.
issues.impactSummary: Requests traversing this rate limiter will be dropped or delayed, leading to elevated error rates and increased latency for upstream services. Downstream services may experience a sudden drop in incoming traffic.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the source of the traffic surge. Adjust rate limit thresholds if current limits are too restrictive for normal operation. Implement back-off and retry strategies in upstream services. Monitor traffic patterns and resource utilization.

assumptions: The rate limiter is configured to drop or reject requests when limits are exceeded, rather than queuing them indefinitely.
Upstream services are designed to handle rejection or timeouts from the rate limiter, potentially implementing retries or circuit breakers.
The primary function of this component is to protect downstream services or resources from excessive load, even at the cost of dropping requests.

ruleIntents.name: RateLimitExceededPropagation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} (networkRateLimiter) has exceeded its configured rate limits, causing requests from {upstream} to fail and potentially starving {downstream} of incoming traffic.

issues.cause: All registered downstream targets are unhealthy or removed from the load balancer target group.
issues.title: No Healthy Targets Registered
issues.rootReason: Backend service instances are failing health checks, scaled down to zero, or misconfigured.
issues.impactSummary: Traffic cannot be routed by the load balancer, resulting in service unavailability.
issues.recommendation: Investigate health of downstream services, check target group configuration, ensure sufficient capacity.

issues.cause: The network path from clients/upstream systems to the network load balancer is impaired or non-existent.
issues.title: Upstream Connectivity Issue
issues.rootReason: Network configuration errors, routing issues, or upstream network component failures.
issues.impactSummary: The load balancer is unreachable, preventing any traffic from reaching downstream services.
issues.recommendation: Check network ACLs, routing tables, and upstream network device status.

issues.cause: The network load balancer has reached its connection or throughput limits.
issues.title: Load Balancer Capacity Exceeded
issues.rootReason: Sudden surge in traffic, insufficient scaling of the NLB, or misconfiguration.
issues.impactSummary: New connections may be dropped, and existing connections may experience high latency or disconnections.
issues.recommendation: Investigate traffic patterns, scale load balancer capacity if possible, optimize client connection behavior, consider sharding traffic across multiple NLBs.

ruleIntents.name: NoHealthyTargetsImpactUpstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is unable to route traffic because it has no healthy {downstream} targets. This makes {component} effectively unreachable for its {upstream} callers.

ruleIntents.name: DownstreamHealthDegradationAffectsNLB
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Health degradation in {downstream} targets is causing {component} to report no healthy instances, leading to routing failures through {component}.

ruleIntents.name: UpstreamNetworkImpactsNLBReachability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: An issue with the {upstream} network is preventing traffic from reaching {component}, making {component} itself unreachable.

ruleIntents.name: CapacityExceededImpactsCallers
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is exceeding its operational capacity, leading to connection failures and degraded performance for its {upstream} callers.

issues.cause: Increased latency or errors from the downstream cache dependency, impacting data retrieval times.
issues.title: Downstream Cache Performance Degradation
issues.rootReason: Cache server overload, network issues between the serverless function and cache, or cache misconfiguration.
issues.impactSummary: Directly increases the latency of serverless function operations requiring cache access, potentially leading to increased execution time and higher resource consumption.
issues.recommendation: Investigate cache server metrics, network connectivity, and cache configuration. Consider increasing cache capacity or optimizing cache queries.

issues.cause: The serverless function's autoscaling mechanism cannot keep pace with rapid changes in upstream request volume or hits its configured concurrency limits.
issues.title: Serverless Autoscaling Bottleneck
issues.rootReason: Burst traffic exceeding scale-up rate, maximum concurrency limits reached, or cold start delays for new instances.
issues.impactSummary: Leads to increased request latency, potential for request throttling or rejections, and degradation of user experience due to insufficient capacity.
issues.recommendation: Review autoscaling configuration (min/max instances, target metrics), pre-warm instances if possible, or implement more advanced load distribution strategies upstream.

issues.cause: The serverless function takes too long to process a synchronous request, exceeding its configured timeout, often due to downstream dependency slowness.
issues.title: Synchronous Request Timeout
issues.rootReason: Excessive latency from downstream dependencies (e.g., cache), long-running business logic within the function, or resource contention.
issues.impactSummary: Results in client-side errors and failed requests, directly affecting the user experience for synchronous interactions.
issues.recommendation: Profile function execution to identify bottlenecks, optimize downstream calls, increase function timeout if appropriate (with caution), or consider asynchronous patterns for long operations.

ruleIntents.name: Impact of Downstream Cache Latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {downstream} cache latency directly impacts {component} processing time for data retrieval, leading to increased {component} request latency.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Load Impact on Autoscaling
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Increased request volume from {upstream} can overwhelm {component}'s autoscaling capabilities, leading to increased {component} request latency and potential throttling.

ruleIntents.name: Synchronous Path Latency Causing Timeout
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Excessive processing time in {component}, often due to {downstream} latency, leads to synchronous request timeouts and an increased {component} error rate.

issues.cause: One or more downstream services are responding slowly.
issues.title: App Server Performance Degradation due to Slow Downstream Dependency
issues.rootReason: External dependency performance degradation.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, potential timeout errors, and resource pressure on the app server.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the performance of {downstream} services. Consider adding circuit breakers or retries with backoff.

issues.cause: High fanout to downstream services combined with their slow responses or errors leads to excessive resource consumption on the app server.
issues.title: App Server Resource Exhaustion due to High Fanout and Downstream Issues
issues.rootReason: Downstream dependency performance or availability issues amplified by fanout.
issues.impactSummary: App server becomes unresponsive, potentially leading to cascading failures.
issues.recommendation: Optimize downstream call patterns, implement throttling, or increase app server capacity. Review downstream service health.

issues.cause: A critical downstream service required by the app server is unavailable or returning errors.
issues.title: App Server Errors due to Critical Downstream Service Failure
issues.rootReason: Core dependency outage.
issues.impactSummary: App server becomes unable to process requests, leading to widespread service impact.
issues.recommendation: Investigate and restore the failed {downstream} service immediately. Implement stronger error handling and fallbacks.

ruleIntents.name: sync_downstream_latency_propagation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing increased latency due to slow responses from its {downstream} dependencies.

ruleIntents.name: fanout_high_resource_exhaustion_from_downstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is consuming excessive resources due to a high fanout pattern combined with slow or erroring responses from its {downstream} dependencies.

ruleIntents.name: critical_downstream_failure_impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing degraded performance or errors due to a failure in a critical {downstream} dependency.

issues.cause: The circuit breaker for a downstream dependency has opened, preventing requests from reaching the dependency.
issues.title: Network API Gateway Circuit Breaker Open
issues.rootReason: Excessive errors or high latency from downstream compute services, indicating a potential failure or overload.
issues.impactSummary: All requests to the affected downstream dependency are immediately failed, leading to increased error rates for upstream callers.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance of downstream compute services. Consider increasing capacity, resolving software defects, or tuning circuit breaker thresholds.

issues.cause: Incoming request rate from upstream services has exceeded configured limits for the API Gateway.
issues.title: Network API Gateway Rate Limit Exceeded by Upstream
issues.rootReason: An upstream service is sending traffic at an unsustainable rate, or the gateway's rate limit configuration is too low for current demand.
issues.impactSummary: Requests from upstream services are being rejected with rate limit errors (e.g., 429 Too Many Requests), potentially causing cascading failures or degraded performance for upstream.
issues.recommendation: Review upstream service behavior and traffic patterns. Adjust API Gateway rate limit configuration if appropriate, or coordinate with upstream teams to reduce traffic. Consider scaling downstream compute services if the limit is intended to protect them.

ruleIntents.name: Detect circuit breaker opening due to downstream compute issues
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} network API gateway's circuit breaker opened because its {downstream} compute dependency is experiencing high error rates or latency.

ruleIntents.name: Detect rate limit being exceeded by upstream callers
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} network API gateway is rejecting requests from {upstream} due to exceeding its configured rate limits.

issues.cause: High network traffic, hardware issues, or misconfigurations within the subnet leading to increased latency and packet loss.
issues.title: Subnet Performance Degradation
issues.rootReason: Degraded network conditions within the subnet.
issues.impactSummary: Causes significant performance degradation and potential service unavailability for connected upstream cache components, leading to slower data access and potential timeouts.
issues.recommendation: Investigate subnet resource utilization, network device health, and configuration. Consider traffic shaping or scaling subnet capacity.

issues.cause: Total failure of core network devices, severe configuration errors, or isolation of the subnet.
issues.title: Subnet Unavailability
issues.rootReason: Complete network outage within the subnet.
issues.impactSummary: Leads to complete loss of connectivity for all connected upstream cache components, rendering them inaccessible and causing service outages.
issues.recommendation: Verify power and physical connections, check network device status and logs, and review recent configuration changes for the subnet.

ruleIntents.name: Subnet Degradation Impacts Upstream Cache Performance
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Performance degradation in {component} (subnet) is causing reduced responsiveness for upstream {upstream} components, particularly affecting cache operations.

ruleIntents.name: Subnet Unavailability Impacts Upstream Cache Connectivity
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} (subnet) is unavailable, resulting in a complete loss of network connectivity for all upstream {upstream} components, critically impacting cache accessibility.

issues.cause: The cache is not effectively serving requests, possibly due to an invalidation storm, insufficient capacity, or a shift in access patterns, forcing more requests to downstream systems.
issues.title: High Cache Miss Rate Detected
issues.rootReason: Increased load on downstream data sources due to requests not being served from cache, leading to overall system performance degradation.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency for read operations, higher load on downstream data sources, potential performance bottlenecks and elevated operational costs.
issues.recommendation: Review cache configuration, capacity, and invalidation strategies. Consider increasing cache size, adjusting eviction policies, or implementing more intelligent caching algorithms.

issues.cause: Network congestion or performance issues between the cache and its downstream dependencies (e.g., database, object storage) are causing delays in data retrieval and storage.
issues.title: Elevated Downstream Network Latency Impacting Cache
issues.rootReason: Slower response times for cache fetches and writes from backend sources, leading to degraded cache performance and increased end-user latency.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end latency for cache operations, potential for timeouts, and perceived slowness for applications relying on the cache.
issues.recommendation: Investigate network infrastructure performance between the cache and its downstream dependencies. Check for bottlenecks, network device health, and consider network path optimization or increased bandwidth.

ruleIntents.name: Detect High Cache Miss Rate
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The cache {component} is experiencing a high miss rate, leading to increased load on its downstream dependencies, such as {downstream}.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Downstream Network Latency Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Elevated network latency originating from {downstream} is impacting {component}'s ability to efficiently fetch and store data, causing performance degradation.
