issues.cause: Increased response times from the API Gateway.
issues.title: Upstream API Gateway Latency Impacting App Server
issues.rootReason: API Gateway overload, misconfiguration, or issues in its own dependencies.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end request latency through the App Server, potentially leading to timeouts and degraded user experience.
issues.recommendation: Investigate API Gateway metrics (latency, error rates, resource utilization). Optimize API Gateway configuration or scale it.

issues.cause: API Gateway errors (e.g., 5xx responses) or unavailability.
issues.title: Upstream API Gateway Failure Impacting App Server
issues.rootReason: API Gateway instance crash, network partitioning, or severe dependency failure.
issues.impactSummary: App Server unable to receive or process requests, leading to complete service unavailability or error responses for users.
issues.recommendation: Investigate API Gateway health checks, logs, and resource status. Restore or restart failed API Gateway instances.

issues.cause: Increased query response times from the Database.
issues.title: Upstream Database Latency Impacting App Server
issues.rootReason: Database overload, inefficient queries, high contention, or slow disk I/O.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end request latency through the App Server, potentially leading to timeouts and degraded user experience.
issues.recommendation: Investigate database query performance, resource utilization, and connection pool status. Optimize database queries, scale database resources, or add read replicas.

issues.cause: Database errors (e.g., connection refused, query errors) or unavailability.
issues.title: Upstream Database Failure Impacting App Server
issues.rootReason: Database instance crash, network partition, deadlock, or resource exhaustion.
issues.impactSummary: App Server unable to retrieve or store data, leading to functional errors, degraded user experience, or complete service unavailability.
issues.recommendation: Investigate database health, logs, and connectivity. Restore or failover database instances.

issues.cause: High CPU, memory, or network utilization on the App Server instances.
issues.title: App Server Resource Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: Increased request volume, inefficient application code, memory leaks, or insufficient instance sizing.
issues.impactSummary: Degraded performance, increased latency, and potential errors or crashes for the App Server, affecting all incoming requests.
issues.recommendation: Optimize application code, increase instance resources, or verify autoscaling policies are effective and responsive.

issues.cause: The VPC is unable to establish outbound connections to external networks or its upstream dependencies.
issues.title: VPC Outbound Connectivity Loss
issues.rootReason: Misconfigured routing tables, network ACLs, security groups, or a failure in an upstream internet gateway/VPN connection associated with the VPC.
issues.impactSummary: All downstream components within the VPC will lose their ability to initiate or respond to outbound network traffic to external destinations, leading to application outages or service degradation.
issues.recommendation: Inspect VPC routing tables, Network ACLs, security group rules, and the status of associated Internet Gateways, NAT Gateways, or VPN connections. Verify BGP routes if using Direct Connect/VPN.

issues.cause: The VPC is unable to receive inbound connections from external networks or its upstream dependencies.
issues.title: VPC Inbound Connectivity Loss
issues.rootReason: Misconfigured routing tables, network ACLs, security groups, or a failure in an upstream internet gateway/VPN connection associated with the VPC, preventing external access.
issues.impactSummary: All downstream components within the VPC that rely on external inbound network traffic will become unreachable, resulting in application outages or service unavailability to external users/systems.
issues.recommendation: Review VPC routing tables, Network ACLs, security group rules, and the status of associated Internet Gateways, VPNs, or Direct Connect. Confirm external DNS resolution and IP reachability.

issues.cause: Internal routing within the VPC or network access control policies are misconfigured or failing, disrupting communication between resources within the VPC.
issues.title: VPC Internal Network Disruption
issues.rootReason: Incorrect VPC routing table entries affecting inter-subnet communication, overly restrictive Network ACLs applied to subnets, or security groups preventing internal traffic flow between component groups.
issues.impactSummary: Connectivity between specific downstream components or subnets within the VPC may be degraded or completely lost, impacting multi-tier applications or internal service dependencies.
issues.recommendation: Examine VPC routing tables for correct routes between subnets and attached gateways (e.g., Transit Gateway). Review Network ACLs and security group rules to ensure they permit necessary internal traffic flows.

ruleIntents.name: VPC-to-Upstream Connectivity Loss
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The VPC {component} is experiencing an issue preventing outbound connectivity, affecting all downstream services within it.

ruleIntents.name: VPC-from-Upstream Connectivity Loss
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Inbound traffic to VPC {component} from its upstream connections is blocked or degraded, impacting all downstream services that rely on external access.

ruleIntents.name: VPC Internal Routing/Security Policy Disruption
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: A misconfiguration within VPC {component}'s internal routing or network access controls is disrupting connectivity for some downstream services.

issues.cause: High latency from a 'sketchy' downstream service directly impacts this appServer's response times and availability due to its synchronous request path nature.
issues.title: Synchronous Request Path Performance Degraded by Sketchy Downstream Latency
issues.rootReason: Poor or inconsistent performance and reliability of the 'sketchy' downstream dependency.
issues.impactSummary: Increased appServer latency, potential request timeouts, and a degraded end-user experience for operations dependent on the 'sketchy' service.
issues.recommendation: Implement circuit breakers, introduce asynchronous patterns, or isolate calls to the 'sketchy' downstream service. Consider timeouts and retries with backoff strategies.

issues.cause: Error rates from a 'sketchy' downstream service are directly propagating up to this appServer, increasing its own observed error rates.
issues.title: Errors Propagating from Sketchy Downstream Service
issues.rootReason: Unreliability and frequent failures within the 'sketchy' downstream dependency.
issues.impactSummary: Increased appServer error rates, reduced service availability, and potential cascading failures affecting upstream services or users.
issues.recommendation: Implement robust error handling, circuit breakers, or retry logic with exponential backoff. Design for graceful degradation when the 'sketchy' downstream is unavailable.

issues.cause: Despite this appServer being configured for autoscaling, persistent performance degradation or resource constraints in the 'sketchy' downstream service prevent actual performance improvements, leading to inefficient resource utilization.
issues.title: Autoscaling Ineffective Due to Downstream Bottleneck
issues.rootReason: The 'sketchy' downstream service acts as a bottleneck, limiting the effective throughput or latency improvements achievable by scaling up this component.
issues.impactSummary: Wasted compute resources, persistent performance issues even under increased scaling, and higher operational costs without proportional gains in performance or availability.
issues.recommendation: Identify and optimize the bottleneck in the downstream service. Implement backpressure mechanisms or request throttling to prevent overwhelming the 'sketchy' dependency. Re-evaluate scaling strategies in light of dependency limitations.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate Sketchy Downstream Latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High latency from {downstream} due to its 'sketchy' nature directly impacts {component} response times because it relies on a synchronous request path.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate Sketchy Downstream Errors
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Failures and high error rates from the 'sketchy' {downstream} service propagate directly to {component}, leading to an increase in its own error metrics.

ruleIntents.name: Autoscaling Bottleneck from Sketchy Downstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Despite {component} being configured for autoscaling, chronic performance degradation from the 'sketchy' {downstream} service prevents actual performance improvement, leading to inefficient resource utilization at {component}.

issues.cause: The network discovery service is offline or unreachable, preventing dependent components from resolving network endpoints.
issues.title: Network Discovery Service Unavailable
issues.rootReason: Service infrastructure failure, network partition, or misconfiguration.
issues.impactSummary: Critical impact on all network-dependent components requiring dynamic endpoint resolution, leading to widespread service unavailability.
issues.recommendation: Verify the operational status of the network discovery service and its underlying infrastructure. Check network connectivity from affected components.

ruleIntents.name: DiscoveryFailurePropagation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} failure prevented {downstream} components from discovering necessary network endpoints, leading to service disruption.

issues.cause: The circuit breaker within the primary node opened due to a high rate of failures or timeouts when attempting to communicate with its upstream database dependency.
issues.title: Circuit Breaker Tripped for Upstream Database
issues.rootReason: Sustained unresponsiveness, errors, or timeouts from the upstream database, leading to the primary node's circuit breaker policy being activated to prevent cascading failures and protect the system.
issues.impactSummary: The primary node cannot access its critical upstream database, resulting in a loss of functionality or complete service unavailability for operations relying on database access. This can cascade to downstream services depending on this primary node.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance of the upstream database. Check network connectivity, database resource utilization, query performance, and error logs. Consider adjusting circuit breaker thresholds if the upstream service is expected to be intermittently slow or unreliable, or implement a fallback mechanism.

issues.cause: The primary node is observing elevated latency, timeouts, or errors when interacting with its upstream database, indicating a performance issue in the database layer.
issues.title: Upstream Database Experiencing Performance Degradation
issues.rootReason: Resource contention, inefficient queries, high load, or underlying infrastructure problems affecting the upstream database's ability to respond promptly or correctly, prior to the circuit breaker activating.
issues.impactSummary: Operations performed by the primary node that rely on the upstream database will experience increased response times and potentially higher error rates, leading to degraded user experience and potential downstream impacts. If unchecked, this could lead to the circuit breaker tripping.
issues.recommendation: Analyze database metrics for CPU, memory, I/O, and connection usage. Review recent query performance and execution plans. Consider scaling database resources or optimizing database operations. Check network path for congestion or errors between the primary node and the database.

ruleIntents.name: detectCircuitBreakerOpenForUpstreamDatabase
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} circuit breaker opened due to sustained errors from its {upstream} database, preventing further calls and degrading {component} availability.

ruleIntents.name: detectUpstreamDatabasePerformanceDegradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} detected elevated latency and error rates from its {upstream} database, impacting {component} performance.

issues.cause: Excessive write requests originating from upstream compute components.
issues.title: Database Experiencing High Write Load
issues.rootReason: Upstream service demands or inefficient write patterns are overwhelming the database's write capacity, leading to resource contention.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency for write operations, potential for timeouts, and overall performance degradation, affecting services dependent on database writes.
issues.recommendation: Implement data sharding, introduce read replicas for read-heavy operations, optimize write queries, or apply throttling mechanisms on upstream services. Scale database write capacity.

issues.cause: The dependent downstream database component is unresponsive or offline.
issues.title: Downstream Database Unavailability
issues.rootReason: Failure of a critical dependency; the current database cannot access necessary data or persist changes through its downstream connection.
issues.impactSummary: Data access failures, internal errors within the database, leading to service degradation or outage for its own upstream callers.
issues.recommendation: Ensure downstream database redundancy, implement robust retry and failover mechanisms, or investigate the health and connectivity of the downstream component.

issues.cause: Too many concurrent connections or long-running queries from upstream compute components.
issues.title: Database Connection Pool Exhaustion
issues.rootReason: The database's resource limits for concurrent connections have been reached due to high demand or inefficient resource management, preventing new connections.
issues.impactSummary: New incoming requests from upstream are rejected or experience severe delays, leading to service outages or significant performance degradation.
issues.recommendation: Optimize database queries, increase connection pool size if appropriate for the workload, implement connection throttling, or scale the database vertically/horizontally.

issues.cause: The internal circuit breaker of the database has tripped due to detected failures or high error rates when interacting with downstream systems or internal resources.
issues.title: Internal Circuit Breaker Tripped
issues.rootReason: Underlying issues (e.g., downstream unavailability, internal resource contention) leading to failure thresholds being crossed, causing the circuit breaker to activate for protection.
issues.impactSummary: Requests (especially to downstream systems) made by the database will fail fast, preventing further load on failing systems but also limiting its own functionality and propagating errors upstream.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the root cause of failures that led to the circuit breaker tripping. This may involve checking downstream dependencies or internal resource utilization.

ruleIntents.name: Detect elevated database write load
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} database is experiencing high write load due to elevated write requests from {upstream} components. This could lead to resource exhaustion and degraded performance.

ruleIntents.name: Monitor downstream database availability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} database is unable to connect to its {downstream} database. This critical dependency failure is causing data access and persistence operations to fail.

ruleIntents.name: Monitor database connection pool utilization
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} database's connection pool is exhausted, likely due to an overwhelming number of concurrent requests from {upstream} components. New connections are being rejected.

ruleIntents.name: Detect database circuit breaker activation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The circuit breaker within the {component} database has tripped, likely due to repeated failures or unresponsiveness when attempting to connect to its {downstream} database, leading to fast-fail errors.

issues.cause: The load balancer is unable to resolve or connect to its upstream DNS discovery service.
issues.title: Upstream DNS Discovery Failure Affecting Load Balancer
issues.rootReason: Misconfiguration of DNS resolution, network connectivity issues to DNS server, or the DNS server itself is unhealthy.
issues.impactSummary: Upstream services (api_gateway) cannot be properly discovered, leading to service unavailability or degraded performance for requests routed through the load balancer.
issues.recommendation: Verify DNS configuration and connectivity from the load balancer. Check health and availability of the DNS discovery service. Review firewall rules.

issues.cause: One or more downstream compute instances are not responding to health checks or service requests from the load balancer.
issues.title: Downstream Compute Instances Unresponsive
issues.rootReason: Compute instance resource exhaustion (CPU, memory), application crash on compute, network isolation, or misconfiguration of compute health check endpoints.
issues.impactSummary: Reduced capacity and potential service unavailability as the load balancer marks compute instances unhealthy and stops routing traffic to them.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and logs of affected downstream compute instances. Review network connectivity between load balancer and compute. Adjust health check thresholds or retry policies.

issues.cause: The upstream API gateway is experiencing high request volumes or latency, leading to slow responses or timeouts for the load balancer.
issues.title: Upstream API Gateway is Overloaded
issues.rootReason: High inbound traffic to the API gateway, resource contention on the API gateway, or downstream services of the API gateway are bottlenecked.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency and error rates for requests passing through the load balancer due to slow or failed responses from the API gateway.
issues.recommendation: Scale out the upstream API gateway. Implement rate limiting or circuit breaking at the load balancer or API gateway. Investigate bottlenecks further upstream or within the API gateway's own dependencies.

ruleIntents.name: Detect DNS Discovery Service Unavailability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is unable to resolve its upstream DNS discovery service. This prevents proper discovery of {upstream} services.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Unresponsive Downstream Compute
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} detected that one or more {downstream} instances are unresponsive or unhealthy, leading to reduced capacity.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Upstream API Gateway Latency/Errors
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing high latency or errors when communicating with the {upstream} API gateway, indicating potential overload.

issues.cause: Excessive requests from upstream compute component(s) are overwhelming the database.
issues.title: Upstream Compute Saturation
issues.rootReason: High load or malfunction in upstream compute applications leading to increased database query rates, connection pooling exhaustion, or I/O contention.
issues.impactSummary: Decreased database response times, increased error rates, potential outages for applications dependent on this database.
issues.recommendation: Investigate upstream compute component(s) for high resource utilization, connection leaks, or inefficient query patterns. Implement rate limiting or circuit breakers at the compute layer.

issues.cause: The primary database instance is unresponsive or has failed.
issues.title: Primary Database Unavailability
issues.rootReason: Hardware failure, software crash, network partition, or critical resource exhaustion on the primary database server.
issues.impactSummary: Applications dependent on this database will experience service interruption. Failover targets may become primary, potentially leading to performance impacts during transition.
issues.recommendation: Initiate failover to a healthy replica if available. Investigate the root cause of the primary failure and restore service. Ensure data consistency during recovery.

issues.cause: High volume of write operations is saturating database I/O, CPU, or memory resources.
issues.title: Database Write Saturation
issues.rootReason: Sustained peak write loads from upstream applications, inefficient write queries, lack of indexing, or insufficient hardware resources for write operations.
issues.impactSummary: Increased write latency, reduced database throughput, potential for blocking read operations, and overall system slowdown.
issues.recommendation: Optimize write queries, improve indexing, scale database resources (vertical or horizontal sharding/replication), consider caching writes or using a write-optimized storage tier. Implement write rate limiting.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream Compute Request Spike
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {upstream} is sending an unusually high volume of requests or errors to {component}, potentially saturating its resources and causing performance degradation.

ruleIntents.name: Database Unavailability Detection
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is unreachable or unhealthy, causing an outage that will affect its {downstream} replicas and dependent systems.

ruleIntents.name: Write Heavy Performance Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing performance degradation due to high write load, impacting its ability to process requests from {upstream} efficiently.

issues.cause: Excessive request volume from upstream compute services, exceeding {component}'s processing capacity.

issues.cause: The connected downstream load balancer is reporting an unhealthy status or is completely unresponsive, preventing traffic from being forwarded.
issues.title: Downstream Load Balancer Unavailable
issues.rootReason: Loss of connectivity, overload, or critical failure of the downstream load balancer component.
issues.impactSummary: All incoming traffic to this ingress will fail to reach its intended destination, resulting in a complete service interruption for dependent applications.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and operational status of the downstream load balancer component. Ensure it is running, correctly configured, and capable of receiving traffic from this ingress.

assumptions: The ingress is configured to actively check the health of its downstream load balancer.
Upon detecting an unhealthy downstream load balancer, the ingress will stop or significantly reduce forwarding traffic and may return errors to clients.

ruleIntents.name: Ingress Reacts to Downstream Load Balancer Failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} cannot forward incoming traffic because its {downstream} load balancer is unavailable or unhealthy.

issues.cause: The serverless function is receiving more requests than it can process efficiently, leading to resource exhaustion or exceeding concurrency limits.
issues.title: Serverless Function Overloaded by Upstream Requests
issues.rootReason: Upstream service (API Gateway) is sending excessive traffic, or the serverless function has insufficient capacity, slow execution times, or cold start issues exacerbating the overload. The autoscale trait may be struggling to keep up.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, HTTP 429/5xx errors from the serverless function, and potential request timeouts for callers.
issues.recommendation: Implement throttling/rate limiting on the upstream API Gateway. Optimize function code for faster execution. Increase provisioned concurrency or memory. Use warm-up techniques to mitigate cold starts.

issues.cause: A critical downstream dependency (e.g., cache, database) is unavailable, experiencing high latency, or returning errors.
issues.title: Serverless Function Impaired by Downstream Dependency Failure
issues.rootReason: Failure or performance degradation of a required external service that the serverless function depends on for state or data retrieval/persistence.
issues.impactSummary: Errors within the serverless function, increased execution time, and potential unavailability for upstream callers.
issues.recommendation: Investigate and resolve issues in the downstream cache or database. Implement robust retry mechanisms with backoff, circuit breakers, and timeouts in the serverless function. Use fallbacks or cached data where possible.

issues.cause: Frequent invocation of the serverless function after periods of inactivity, requiring the runtime environment to be initialized, leading to increased latency for the initial requests.
issues.title: Elevated Latency Due to Serverless Cold Starts
issues.rootReason: Insufficient traffic to keep instances warm or aggressive scaling down by the platform, combined with the inherent nature of serverless execution models. This is particularly impactful due to the 'is_sync_request_path' trait.
issues.impactSummary: Inconsistent and higher-than-expected response times for requests, particularly noticeable after idle periods, negatively impacting user experience for synchronous requests.
issues.recommendation: Optimize function bundles and dependencies to reduce startup time. Increase memory allocation. Implement warm-up routines or use provisioned concurrency for critical synchronous paths. Adjust autoscaling settings if possible.

ruleIntents.name: UpstreamRequestOverloadDetection
ruleIntents.impactShape: Increased latency and errors on {component}, potential resource exhaustion.
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {upstream} is sending too many requests to {component}, exceeding its capacity to process them, leading to errors and increased latency.

ruleIntents.name: DownstreamDependencyFailureDetection
ruleIntents.impactShape: Errors and increased latency on {component} due to dependency issues.
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing errors or high latency due to a degraded or failing downstream dependency, {downstream}.

ruleIntents.name: ColdStartLatencyDetection
ruleIntents.impactShape: Intermittent high latency for initial requests to {component}.
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing increased latency due to frequent cold starts, which is particularly impactful for its synchronous request path.

issues.cause: One or more downstream services are reporting unhealthy status, high error rates, or failing health checks.
issues.title: Downstream Service Unhealthy
issues.rootReason: Insufficient capacity, application errors, or network issues on a downstream dependency, causing it to become unresponsive or faulty.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, errors, or timeouts for requests routed through the load balancer, potentially impacting upstream services (api_gateway) and end-users.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and logs of the affected downstream services. Consider temporarily removing unhealthy instances from the load balancer pool or scaling out the downstream service.

issues.cause: The load balancer itself is overloaded or saturated, or its downstream services are responding slowly, leading to increased request processing times.
issues.title: Load Balancer Experiencing High Latency
issues.rootReason: Insufficient resources on the load balancer, unexpected traffic spikes, or systemic performance issues within downstream compute or api_gateway services.
issues.impactSummary: Requests routed through the load balancer experience significant delays, directly impacting upstream 'api_gateway' responsiveness and overall user experience, especially due to the synchronous request path trait.
issues.recommendation: Investigate load balancer resource utilization (CPU, memory, network I/O) and connection patterns. Review downstream service performance and scale if necessary. Implement rate limiting or circuit breaking if traffic is excessive.

ruleIntents.name: Load Balancer: Handle Downstream Unhealthy
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} detected unhealthiness in its {downstream} services, causing degraded performance and potential request failures for {upstream}. The load balancer is attempting to route around the failures but overall throughput is affected.

ruleIntents.name: Load Balancer: High Latency on Synchronous Path
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing high latency, likely due to slow responses from its {downstream} services or internal resource contention. This is directly impacting the responsiveness of {upstream} services (api_gateway) because of the synchronous request path.

issues.cause: Elevated response times from upstream services, specifically API Gateway, are causing the network load balancer to wait longer for responses.
issues.title: Increased upstream API Gateway latency affecting load balancer performance
issues.rootReason: High contention, resource exhaustion, or processing delays within the upstream API Gateway components.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end request latency, potential timeouts at the client, and reduced throughput for the load balancer.
issues.recommendation: Investigate upstream API Gateway metrics for CPU, memory, concurrent connections, or error rates. Consider scaling upstream services or optimizing their processing logic.

issues.cause: Excessive incoming traffic or connection rates exceeding the load balancer's configured capacity or resource limits.
issues.title: Network Load Balancer experiencing high utilization
issues.rootReason: Spike in client requests, DDoS attack, or insufficient load balancer scaling.
issues.impactSummary: Degraded responsiveness, connection failures, increased latency, and potential service unavailability for clients.
issues.recommendation: Scale out the network load balancer instances, adjust its connection limits, or implement rate limiting upstream.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream API Gateway Latency Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Increased response times from {upstream} (API Gateway) are causing {component} to experience {impactShape} due to longer wait times for upstream operations.

ruleIntents.name: Network Load Balancer Overload Detection
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing {impactShape} due to excessive inbound requests or connections.

issues.cause: Slow responses from upstream DNS discovery service.
issues.title: Upstream DNS Discovery Latency Affecting API Gateway
issues.rootReason: Resource contention, network issues, or misconfiguration in the upstream DNS discovery service.
issues.impactSummary: Increased API Gateway request latency, potentially leading to client timeouts and poor user experience.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the upstream DNS discovery service for performance bottlenecks, network latency, or configuration errors. Consider implementing caching or faster DNS resolvers.

issues.cause: The upstream DNS discovery service is unavailable or returning errors.
issues.title: Upstream DNS Discovery Unavailability Impacting API Gateway
issues.rootReason: Outage, crashing, or critical misconfiguration of the upstream DNS discovery service.
issues.impactSummary: Complete inability for the API Gateway to resolve backend service endpoints, leading to a service outage.
issues.recommendation: Immediately investigate the health and status of the upstream DNS discovery service. Verify its configuration and ensure it is reachable and responsive.

issues.cause: The downstream load balancer is not responding or failing health checks from the API Gateway.
issues.title: Downstream Load Balancer Unresponsive to API Gateway
issues.rootReason: Load balancer outage, overload, or misconfiguration, or issues with backend services causing health check failures.
issues.impactSummary: Requests from the API Gateway cannot be forwarded to backend services, leading to increased error rates and potential partial service outages.
issues.recommendation: Inspect the health and resource utilization of the downstream load balancer. Check its configuration and the health of the backend services it manages. Ensure sufficient capacity.

issues.cause: Slow responses from the downstream load balancer.
issues.title: Downstream Load Balancer Latency Affecting API Gateway
issues.rootReason: Load balancer resource contention, network issues, or backend service performance degradation propagated through the load balancer.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end request latency for the API Gateway, degrading user experience.
issues.recommendation: Examine the downstream load balancer's performance metrics, including CPU, memory, and network I/O. Investigate the latency of the backend services it is distributing traffic to.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream DNS Discovery Latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing increased latency due to slow DNS resolution from its {upstream} DNS discovery service. This could lead to delayed request processing and potential timeouts.

ruleIntents.name: Upstream DNS Discovery Unavailability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is unable to resolve service endpoints due to its {upstream} DNS discovery service being unavailable or returning errors. This prevents new connections and will eventually lead to a service outage.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Load Balancer Unresponsive
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is unable to forward requests to backend services because its {downstream} load balancer is unresponsive or failing health checks. This will lead to increased errors and dropped requests for clients.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Load Balancer Latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing increased end-to-end latency due to slow responses from its {downstream} load balancer. This directly impacts the user experience.

issues.cause: One or more downstream targets registered with the network load balancer are not responding to health checks or are otherwise unreachable.
issues.title: Downstream Target Unhealthy or Unreachable
issues.rootReason: Service instance failure, network partition, or misconfiguration of target group.
issues.impactSummary: Reduced capacity to serve requests, potential service degradation or outage for clients accessing the load-balanced service.
issues.recommendation: Investigate health of registered downstream targets, check network connectivity, and review target group configuration. Consider scaling out healthy instances or removing unhealthy ones.

issues.cause: The network load balancer itself is experiencing high resource utilization, connection limits, or internal health issues, preventing it from effectively distributing traffic.
issues.title: Network Load Balancer Overloaded or Unhealthy
issues.rootReason: Sudden surge in traffic, misconfiguration of NLB limits, or internal component failure within the NLB infrastructure.
issues.impactSummary: Requests may be dropped or experience high latency. Potential for service outage for all traffic attempting to pass through the load balancer.
issues.recommendation: Monitor NLB metrics (e.g., active connections, processed bytes, health checks). If overloaded, investigate upstream traffic sources and consider scaling NLB capacity if applicable, or optimizing downstream target handling. If unhealthy, investigate platform issues.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate Downstream Target Health Issue
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} (Network Load Balancer) is impacted by unhealthy or unreachable downstream targets. This is likely due to an issue with {downstream}.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate NLB Overload/Unhealthiness Upstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} (Network Load Balancer) is experiencing an overload or health issue, preventing it from processing traffic. This directly impacts {upstream} components.

issues.cause: A critical downstream dependency is experiencing high latency, impacting the performance of the {component}.

issues.impactSummary: Increased request processing time and error rates for {component}, potentially leading to cascading failures.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the performance of downstream services or resources. Optimize database queries, reduce network latency, or scale downstream components.

issues.cause: An upstream component is sending a high rate of errors or malformed requests to the {component}.

issues.impactSummary: Increased error rates and potentially reduced availability for the {component} as it processes faulty requests or handles retries.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the upstream service for issues. Check logs, metrics, and configurations of the upstream component.

issues.cause: The {component} is experiencing sustained high CPU utilization, indicating potential processing bottlenecks.

issues.cause: The API Gateway failed to resolve DNS for an upstream service endpoint.
issues.title: Upstream DNS Resolution Failure
issues.rootReason: Misconfiguration of DNS server, network connectivity issues to DNS server, or DNS server outage preventing service discovery.
issues.impactSummary: Requests to upstream services will fail, leading to service unavailability.
issues.recommendation: Verify DNS server configuration, check network reachability to DNS servers, and ensure DNS records for upstream services are correct and propagated.

issues.cause: The API Gateway encountered errors when establishing or maintaining connections with downstream clients.
issues.title: Downstream Client Connection Error
issues.rootReason: Client-side network issues, client misconfiguration, or severe network congestion between the gateway and clients.
issues.impactSummary: Clients cannot connect to the API Gateway, preventing access to services.
issues.recommendation: Investigate client network conditions, client-side configuration (e.g., firewall rules, proxy settings), and network path health.

issues.cause: Slow responses from DNS servers or network latency during DNS resolution for upstream services.
issues.title: High Latency from Upstream DNS Discovery
issues.rootReason: Overloaded DNS servers, network congestion on DNS path, or inefficient DNS caching by the API Gateway.
issues.impactSummary: Increased request processing time, potentially leading to client timeouts and degraded user experience.
issues.recommendation: Monitor DNS server performance, analyze network latency to DNS servers, and optimize API Gateway's DNS caching strategy.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Upstream DNS Failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} detected a failure in resolving upstream service DNS, indicating an issue with the {upstream} 'dns_discovery' dependency.

ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is encountering connection errors when communicating with a {downstream} 'client' dependency.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Upstream DNS Latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High latency from the {upstream} 'dns_discovery' dependency is causing delays in the {component}'s request processing.

issues.cause: The upstream API Gateway service is exhibiting high latency or an increased error rate, directly impacting synchronous client requests.
issues.title: Client experiences performance degradation due to upstream API Gateway slowness
issues.rootReason: Resource contention, network issues, or internal processing delays within the API Gateway service are causing it to respond slowly or with errors.
issues.impactSummary: Synchronous client requests will experience increased response times, leading to a degraded user experience. In severe cases, requests may time out.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health, resource utilization, and logs of the API Gateway service. Consider scaling the API Gateway or optimizing its internal processes.

issues.cause: The downstream DNS Discovery service is unavailable, returning incorrect or error responses, or experiencing significant latency, preventing the client from locating its target services.
issues.title: Client fails to resolve services due to DNS Discovery issues
issues.rootReason: The DNS Discovery service is down, misconfigured, overloaded, or there are network connectivity problems preventing communication between the client and DNS Discovery.
issues.impactSummary: The client will be unable to establish connections to its necessary services, leading to complete service unavailability from the client's perspective and functional failure.
issues.recommendation: Check the operational status, configuration, and logs of the DNS Discovery service. Verify network connectivity and DNS resolution paths from the client.

ruleIntents.name: ClientUpstreamAPIGatewayLatencyImpact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing increased latency and potential errors because its upstream dependency, the API Gateway ({upstream}), is responding slowly or with errors.

ruleIntents.name: ClientDownstreamDNSDiscoveryFailureImpact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} cannot connect to its required services because its downstream dependency, the DNS Discovery service ({downstream}), is failing to provide name resolution.

issues.cause: The DNS component is unable to resolve domain names, leading to connection failures for dependent services.
issues.title: DNS Resolution Failure
issues.rootReason: Underlying infrastructure issues, resource exhaustion, or misconfiguration in the DNS server preventing it from processing queries or reaching authoritative servers.
issues.impactSummary: Prevents all downstream services from initiating new connections by failing to resolve domain names, effectively halting application traffic relying on DNS.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and configuration of the DNS server. Ensure it has connectivity to authoritative DNS servers and sufficient resources. Check logs for errors related to query processing or zone lookups.

issues.cause: The DNS component is experiencing high latency when resolving domain names.
issues.title: High DNS Resolution Latency
issues.rootReason: Overload on the DNS server, network congestion to upstream DNS providers, or slow processing of queries due to resource contention.
issues.impactSummary: Increases the setup time for new connections and can lead to timeouts for services sensitive to network latency, degrading overall application performance.
issues.recommendation: Monitor the resource utilization of the DNS server (CPU, memory, network I/O). Check network path latency to upstream DNS servers. Consider scaling out DNS resolvers or optimizing query processing.

ruleIntents.name: DNS Failure Impacts Downstream Connectivity
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: DNS resolution failures from {component} are preventing {downstream} services from establishing connections.

ruleIntents.name: High DNS Latency Impacts Downstream Performance
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High DNS resolution latency from {component} is degrading the performance of {downstream} services.

issues.cause: The API Gateway is returning an elevated number of errors, indicating an internal problem or overload.
issues.title: API Gateway Experiencing High Error Rate
issues.rootReason: Possible causes include misconfiguration, resource exhaustion (CPU, memory, network), or internal service failures within the API Gateway itself. This directly impacts its downstream clients.
issues.impactSummary: Clients attempting to access services via the API Gateway will experience a high rate of failed requests, leading to application unavailability or severe degradation.
issues.recommendation: Investigate API Gateway logs for specific error messages. Check resource utilization metrics (CPU, memory, network I/O) on the gateway instances. Review recent configuration changes. Consider scaling out the API Gateway instances if resource limits are being hit due to traffic.

issues.cause: Requests through the API Gateway are taking an unusually long time to process.
issues.title: API Gateway Experiencing High Latency
issues.rootReason: This could be due to API Gateway overload, resource contention, slow processing logic within the gateway, or network congestion impacting the gateway's ability to communicate quickly. This affects the experience of its downstream clients.
issues.impactSummary: Clients will experience noticeable delays when interacting with services behind the API Gateway, potentially leading to timeouts and a degraded user experience.
issues.recommendation: Review API Gateway queue lengths, CPU/memory usage, and network I/O. Investigate any recent configuration changes or deployments. Consider increasing the capacity of the API Gateway or optimizing its internal processing.

issues.cause: Loss of connectivity or complete failure of the upstream external dependency component that the memory fabric relies upon.
issues.title: Upstream External Dependency Unavailable for Memory Fabric
issues.rootReason: Critical external infrastructure or service failure, network partitioning, or misconfiguration preventing the memory fabric from accessing necessary resources.
issues.impactSummary: The memory fabric will be unable to perform its core functions due to the unavailability of essential external resources, leading to a complete service disruption for dependent AI workloads.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health, availability, and connectivity of the identified upstream external dependency. Verify network paths and ensure the dependency is operational and reachable.

issues.cause: Increased latency, reduced throughput, or intermittent errors from the upstream external dependency component.
issues.title: Upstream External Dependency Performance Degradation for Memory Fabric
issues.rootReason: Overload, resource contention, internal bottlenecks, or transient failures within the external dependency, impacting its ability to serve the memory fabric efficiently.
issues.impactSummary: The memory fabric's overall performance will suffer, leading to slower data access, increased processing times for AI models, and potentially reduced quality of service or training convergence.
issues.recommendation: Monitor performance metrics of the upstream external dependency (e.g., latency, throughput, error rates). Identify and address bottlenecks, resource exhaustion, or inefficiencies causing the degradation.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Upstream External Dependency Unavailability Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} (memory fabric) is experiencing unavailability because its upstream {source} (external dependency) is unavailable, preventing essential data or control plane operations.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Upstream External Dependency Performance Degradation Impact
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} (memory fabric) is experiencing performance degradation due to high latency or low throughput from its upstream {source} (external dependency).

issues.cause: The AI model service that the LLM Gateway relies on is unresponsive or unavailable.
issues.title: Downstream AI Model Unavailability
issues.rootReason: Service outage, deployment failure, resource exhaustion in the AI model service.
issues.impactSummary: LLM Gateway requests will fail, leading to service degradation for users.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and availability of the downstream AI model. Verify network connectivity.

issues.cause: The AI model service is responding slowly to requests from the LLM Gateway.
issues.title: Downstream AI Model High Latency
issues.rootReason: Resource contention, heavy load, performance bottlenecks within the AI model.
issues.impactSummary: Increased response times for LLM Gateway requests, potentially leading to timeouts or poor user experience.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the performance metrics of the downstream AI model. Scale resources if necessary.

issues.cause: The LLM Gateway has exceeded its configured request rate limit.
issues.title: LLM Gateway Rate Limiting Engaged
issues.rootReason: High inbound request volume, misconfigured rate limit, or resource exhaustion within the gateway.
issues.impactSummary: Requests to the LLM Gateway will be throttled or rejected, leading to direct service unavailability for some users.
issues.recommendation: Review the traffic patterns to the LLM Gateway. Consider adjusting rate limit thresholds or scaling the gateway.

issues.cause: The circuit breaker within the LLM Gateway has tripped due to repeated failures or unhealthy responses from its downstream dependency (AI model).
issues.title: LLM Gateway Circuit Breaker Open
issues.rootReason: Persistent downstream service failure, misconfigured circuit breaker thresholds.
issues.impactSummary: All requests to the LLM Gateway will immediately fail until the circuit breaker closes, protecting the downstream service but impacting all upstream consumers.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health of the downstream AI model. Once the downstream service recovers, the circuit breaker should reset automatically. Review circuit breaker configuration if it opens too aggressively.

ruleIntents.name: Detect AI Model Unavailability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing failures due to unavailability of the downstream {downstream} service.

ruleIntents.name: Detect AI Model High Latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing elevated latency due to slow responses from the downstream {downstream} service.

ruleIntents.name: Detect LLM Gateway Rate Limiting
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is actively rate-limiting incoming requests, causing some requests to be rejected.

ruleIntents.name: Detect LLM Gateway Circuit Breaker Open
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} has opened its circuit breaker, preventing requests to the unhealthy downstream {downstream} service and failing all incoming requests.

issues.cause: The LLM Gateway's circuit breaker has opened to protect itself and upstream services from a failing downstream LLM service.
issues.title: LLM Gateway Circuit Breaker Open
issues.rootReason: Sustained high error rates or timeouts from the designated downstream LLM service, triggering the circuit breaker threshold.
issues.impactSummary: All requests routed through this gateway to the affected downstream LLM will fail fast, leading to 100% unavailability for these requests from the perspective of upstream callers. This prevents resource exhaustion on both the gateway and the downstream LLM.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance of the downstream LLM service. Once the downstream service recovers, manually reset the circuit breaker or wait for its automatic reset.

issues.cause: The LLM Gateway has exceeded its configured rate limit for incoming requests.
issues.title: LLM Gateway Rate Limit Exceeded
issues.rootReason: A surge in request traffic from upstream components, or a misconfiguration of the rate limit threshold, leading to the gateway rejecting requests to maintain stability.
issues.impactSummary: Requests from upstream components attempting to access the LLM Gateway will be rejected with a rate limit error, leading to partial or complete unavailability for those requests depending on the traffic volume beyond the limit. This protects the gateway and downstream LLMs from overload.
issues.recommendation: Review upstream traffic patterns and application behavior. Consider adjusting the rate limit if the current threshold is too low, or investigate if upstream applications are making excessive calls. Scale gateway or downstream resources if traffic is legitimate.

issues.cause: Requests from the LLM Gateway to its downstream LLM service are experiencing elevated response times.
issues.title: LLM Gateway Downstream Latency
issues.rootReason: The downstream LLM service is slow to process requests, possibly due to high load, complex query processing, resource contention, or network delays between the gateway and the LLM.
issues.impactSummary: Upstream services calling this LLM Gateway will experience increased latency for their requests, potentially leading to timeouts, degraded user experience, or cascading performance issues if upstream components are sensitive to response times.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the performance metrics of the downstream LLM service, including CPU, memory, and query processing times. Optimize LLM queries, scale the LLM service, or check network connectivity and latency.

ruleIntents.name: Circuit Breaker Tripped Due to Downstream Failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The circuit breaker in {component} opened due to sustained errors or timeouts from {downstream}.

ruleIntents.name: Rate Limit Exceeded by Upstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is rejecting requests from {upstream} because its rate limit has been exceeded.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream LLM Latency impacting Upstream Experience
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing high latency when calling {downstream}, which is causing slow responses for {upstream}.

issues.cause: Network connectivity issues, firewall blocking, or downstream compute instances are completely unresponsive to health check probes.
issues.title: Health Check Service unable to reach downstream compute targets
issues.rootReason: Failure to establish connection or receive timely responses from downstream compute services during health check operations.
issues.impactSummary: Health checks for specific downstream compute targets will report them as unhealthy or unknown, potentially leading to incorrect traffic routing or service unavailability.
issues.recommendation: Verify network path and firewall rules between the health check service and downstream compute targets. Check resource utilization and logs on affected compute instances.

assumptions: The healthCheckService's own health status is directly affected by its ability to successfully probe downstream compute services.
A failure to reach downstream compute is considered a degradation of the healthCheckService's core function.

ruleIntents.name: Degraded due to downstream compute unreachability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} reports degradation because it cannot successfully perform health checks on its downstream {downstream} compute targets, leading to inaccurate health status reporting.

issues.cause: Upstream components are writing data to the queue faster than the queue can process or persist it, or faster than downstream components can consume it. This is exacerbated by the 'write_heavy' trait.
issues.title: Queue backlog due to high write pressure from upstream
issues.rootReason: Increased demand on the upstream system, a bottleneck in the queue's write throughput, or insufficient downstream consumer capacity.
issues.impactSummary: Increased message latency, potential data loss if queue capacity is exceeded, degraded system responsiveness, and cascading failures to dependent services.
issues.recommendation: Consider scaling up the queue's write capacity, optimizing upstream write patterns, implementing backpressure mechanisms on upstream writers, or scaling up downstream consumers.

issues.cause: Downstream components are consuming and processing messages from the queue too slowly, causing messages to remain in the queue for extended periods. This is critical for 'is_async_path' components.
issues.title: Message latency increase due to slow downstream processing
issues.rootReason: A bottleneck in downstream processing, insufficient downstream capacity, or spikes in message volume overwhelming downstream consumers.
issues.impactSummary: Delayed message processing, stale data in downstream systems, potential service degradation if dependent on timely message processing, and reduced overall system throughput.
issues.recommendation: Scale up downstream consumers, optimize downstream processing logic, implement dead-letter queues for problematic messages, or adjust queue configuration (e.g., visibility timeout).

ruleIntents.name: Detect high upstream write pressure causing queue backlog
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {source} is experiencing high write pressure into {component}, potentially causing message backlog and increased write latency on the queue side.

ruleIntents.name: Detect slow downstream processing causing queue latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Messages in {component} are experiencing high latency due to slow processing by {source}, indicating a potential bottleneck in the asynchronous path.

issues.cause: One or more downstream services are failing health checks or not responding to requests.
issues.title: Downstream Service Unresponsive
issues.rootReason: Application failure, resource exhaustion, or network issues in downstream compute services.
issues.impactSummary: Increased error rates and latency for traffic served by the load balancer, potential service degradation for upstream components.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and resources of downstream 'compute' instances. Verify network connectivity.

issues.cause: The load balancer is processing a higher volume of traffic or more complex requests than it can handle.
issues.title: Load Balancer Capacity Exceeded
issues.rootReason: Spike in upstream traffic (from api_gateway/network), insufficient load balancer resources, or misconfiguration.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency, connection errors, and dropped requests for all traffic passing through the load balancer, impacting upstream 'api_gateway' and 'network'.
issues.recommendation: Scale out or scale up the load balancer. Review upstream traffic patterns and application behavior. Implement rate limiting upstream if applicable.

issues.cause: The load balancer is receiving a high rate of malformed or invalid requests from upstream services.
issues.title: Upstream Malformed Requests
issues.rootReason: Misconfiguration in upstream 'api_gateway' or 'network', or malicious traffic patterns.
issues.impactSummary: Increased error rates and resource consumption on the load balancer, potentially affecting legitimate traffic.
issues.recommendation: Investigate upstream 'api_gateway' and 'network' configurations. Implement input validation and filtering at the load balancer or upstream.

issues.cause: Downstream 'compute' services are experiencing elevated processing times for requests.
issues.title: Downstream Service Latency Increase
issues.rootReason: Resource contention, database bottlenecks, or slow external dependencies within downstream compute services.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end request latency for all upstream callers ('api_gateway', 'network') serviced by this load balancer, potentially leading to timeouts.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the performance metrics of downstream 'compute' instances, focusing on CPU, memory, and I/O. Optimize application code or scale downstream services.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Downstream Service Unresponsive
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} detected unresponsive instances in its {downstream} pool, leading to increased error rates and latency for requests.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Load Balancer Capacity Exceeded
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing high resource utilization, leading to degraded performance for traffic from {upstream} to {downstream}.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Upstream Malformed Requests
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} detected a high rate of malformed requests originating from {upstream}, impacting its ability to process legitimate traffic.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Downstream Latency Increase
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is observing increased response times from its {downstream} services, causing higher end-to-end latency for requests originating from {upstream}.

issues.cause: One or more downstream services (e.g., Load Balancer, Network) are unavailable or unresponsive, preventing the API Gateway from forwarding requests.
issues.title: Downstream Service Unavailability
issues.rootReason: Loss of connectivity or operational failure of a critical downstream dependency.
issues.impactSummary: The API Gateway will be unable to process requests, leading to failed client connections and service disruption.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the status and health of downstream Load Balancers and Network components. Ensure network connectivity and correct routing. Implement health checks and retry mechanisms at the API Gateway.

issues.cause: One or more downstream services (e.g., Load Balancer, Network) are experiencing high latency or slow response times, impacting the API Gateway's ability to process requests efficiently.
issues.title: Downstream Service Performance Degradation
issues.rootReason: Overload, resource exhaustion, or internal performance issues within a downstream dependency.
issues.impactSummary: Client requests processed by the API Gateway will experience increased latency, potentially leading to timeouts and a degraded user experience.
issues.recommendation: Examine performance metrics for downstream Load Balancers and Network components. Implement rate limiting, circuit breakers, and timeouts at the API Gateway to prevent cascading failures. Scale or optimize downstream services.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Service Unavailability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} cannot forward requests because {downstream} is unavailable.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream Performance Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: {component} is experiencing increased latency due to performance degradation in {downstream}.

issues.cause: The API Gateway is unable to establish connections or is receiving a high rate of error responses from a configured upstream service.
issues.title: Upstream Service Unavailability Detected
issues.rootReason: Failure or misconfiguration of an upstream backend service.
issues.impactSummary: Requests routed through the API Gateway to the affected upstream service will fail, resulting in client errors and service degradation.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and logs of the upstream services connected to the API Gateway. Verify network connectivity and service configurations.

issues.cause: The API Gateway is experiencing slow response times from one or more upstream services.
issues.title: High Latency from Upstream Service
issues.rootReason: Performance bottlenecks or overload within an upstream backend service.
issues.impactSummary: Requests processed by the API Gateway will experience increased latency, leading to degraded user experience and potential client timeouts.
issues.recommendation: Examine performance metrics and logs for the identified upstream service. Consider scaling or optimizing the upstream service.

issues.cause: Downstream clients are experiencing timeouts when attempting to connect to or receive responses from the API Gateway.
issues.title: Downstream Client Timeout Issues
issues.rootReason: Could be due to high latency within the API Gateway itself, slow upstream services, or client-side timeout configurations.
issues.impactSummary: Clients are unable to successfully complete requests, leading to service disruption and poor user experience.
issues.recommendation: Review API Gateway's internal metrics (CPU, memory, request queue) and upstream service performance. Advise clients to adjust timeout settings if the gateway is healthy.

assumptions: The API Gateway acts as a reverse proxy and/or load balancer.
The API Gateway routes client requests to various upstream backend services.
The API Gateway is a primary entry point for external or internal clients.
The API Gateway processes incoming requests and may apply policies like authentication, authorization, or rate limiting.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Upstream Unavailability
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} detected a high rate of connection failures or error responses from {upstream} service, indicating its unavailability.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Upstream High Latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is observing significantly increased response times from the {upstream} service, suggesting performance degradation.

ruleIntents.name: Observe Downstream Client Timeouts
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Downstream clients of {component} are reporting increased timeouts, likely due to {component}'s internal processing or slow responses from its own upstream dependencies.

reviewWarnings: The profile signature 'up:none|down:none' indicates no explicit upstream/downstream roles were provided, so rules are based on typical API Gateway behavior.
Default severity levels are suggested and may need adjustment based on specific system context and business impact.

issues.cause: Failure of an upstream 'compute' component
issues.title: Upstream compute dependency failure
issues.rootReason: Loss of availability or performance in critical upstream compute services impacts {component}'s ability to process requests.
issues.impactSummary: Direct service degradation or outage for {component} due to unavailability or severe performance issues from its upstream compute dependencies.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and performance of upstream 'compute' components. Ensure sufficient capacity, redundancy, and proper configuration.

issues.cause: Failure of a downstream 'database' component
issues.title: Downstream database dependency failure
issues.rootReason: Loss of availability or performance in critical downstream database services prevents {component} from storing or retrieving necessary data, leading to service disruption.
issues.impactSummary: Loss of data persistence or retrieval capabilities for {component}, leading to functional failure or errors propagated to its own clients.
issues.recommendation: Examine the health and performance of downstream 'database' components. Check for connection issues, query performance, and database availability. Implement retry mechanisms and circuit breakers.

ruleIntents.name: Impact from Downstream Database Failure

issues.cause: The CDN is unable to resolve the hostname of its origin server via upstream DNS discovery.
issues.title: Upstream DNS Resolution Failure for Origin
issues.rootReason: Failure or high latency in the upstream DNS resolver prevents the CDN from locating or connecting to its origin service.
issues.impactSummary: The CDN will fail to fetch new or updated content from its origin, leading to increased cache misses and potentially serving stale content or returning errors for new requests.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health and configuration of the DNS discovery service responsible for resolving origin server hostnames. Ensure DNS records are correct and accessible.

issues.cause: The CDN's downstream origin services (load balancer or object store) are experiencing high latency, errors, or are unreachable.
issues.title: Downstream Origin Service Degradation
issues.rootReason: Failure, overload, or network issues affecting the services from which the CDN fetches its content (e.g., origin load balancer or object store).
issues.impactSummary: The CDN will suffer increased cache misses, slower content updates, and higher latency for uncached content requests, potentially leading to user-facing performance degradation and errors.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health, capacity, and network connectivity of the downstream load balancer and object store services that act as the CDN's origins. Ensure they are responding within expected latency and error thresholds.

issues.cause: The CDN is experiencing internal resource constraints, network saturation, or software issues, affecting its ability to serve content efficiently.
issues.title: CDN Service Performance Degradation
issues.rootReason: Overload, misconfiguration, or failure within the CDN's own infrastructure.
issues.impactSummary: Users will experience increased latency and/or errors when requesting content served by the CDN, leading to poor user experience and potential application unavailability.
issues.recommendation: Monitor CDN internal metrics (CPU, memory, network I/O), scale capacity if needed, and review CDN configuration for optimizations. Investigate any recent deployments or changes.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Upstream DNS Resolution Failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing high error rates or timeouts when attempting to resolve origin hostnames, indicating a problem with its {upstream} DNS discovery.

ruleIntents.name: Detect Downstream Origin Service Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is experiencing increased latency and/or error rates when fetching content from its {downstream} origin (load balancer or object store), indicating a problem with the origin service itself.

ruleIntents.name: Detect CDN Self Performance Degradation
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} is reporting high local latency or error rates, suggesting an internal performance issue affecting content delivery to its consumers.

issues.cause: The DNS server is taking too long to resolve queries, likely due to high load, resource contention, or network issues affecting its ability to communicate with authoritative servers.
issues.title: DNS Resolution Latency Exceeds SLO
issues.rootReason: High DNS query latency impacting upstream services.
issues.impactSummary: Upstream services (clients) relying on DNS name resolution will experience increased latency for connection establishment, potentially leading to application timeouts or service degradation.
issues.recommendation: Investigate DNS server resource utilization (CPU, memory, network I/O). Check for network congestion or issues between the DNS server and its upstream authoritative DNS servers. Consider scaling DNS resources or optimizing query caching.

issues.cause: The DNS server is failing to resolve names, possibly due to configuration errors, an inability to reach authoritative servers, or an internal server fault.
issues.title: DNS Resolution Failure
issues.rootReason: Failed DNS queries impacting upstream services.
issues.impactSummary: Upstream services (clients) will be unable to establish connections to services whose names cannot be resolved, leading to complete service unavailability or hard failures.
issues.recommendation: Verify DNS server configuration, check network connectivity to authoritative DNS servers, and review DNS server logs for errors. Ensure the DNS service process is running and healthy.

ruleIntents.name: DNS_SLO_BREACH_PERFORMANCE
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} DNS server is experiencing high query latency, causing {source} (clients) to observe slow name resolutions.

ruleIntents.name: DNS_FAILURE_AVAILABILITY
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} DNS server is failing to resolve queries, causing {source} (clients) to be unable to establish connections.

issues.cause: The DNS server is not responding to queries or is down.
issues.title: DNS Service Unavailable
issues.rootReason: Service outage, network partition, or misconfiguration.
issues.impactSummary: Clients dependent on this DNS service will experience hostname resolution failures, leading to service unavailability.
issues.recommendation: Verify DNS server status, network connectivity, and configuration. Ensure redundant DNS resolvers are available.

issues.cause: DNS server cache poisoning, misconfiguration, or failure to update records from authoritative sources.
issues.title: DNS Providing Incorrect Resolutions
issues.rootReason: Data consistency issue, security compromise, or configuration drift.
issues.impactSummary: Clients may attempt to connect to incorrect services or endpoints, leading to service failures, security risks, or degraded performance.
issues.recommendation: Inspect DNS zone files, cache health, and authoritative server sync status. Implement DNSSEC where appropriate.

issues.cause: DNS server overload, network congestion, or slow upstream authoritative servers.
issues.title: High DNS Query Latency
issues.rootReason: Performance bottleneck, resource exhaustion, or external dependency latency.
issues.impactSummary: Clients will experience increased request latency, potentially leading to timeouts and degraded user experience.
issues.recommendation: Monitor DNS server resources, network path latency, and cache hit rates. Consider deploying more DNS resolvers or optimizing caching strategy.

issues.cause: The DNS server cannot reach its configured authoritative DNS servers or recursive resolvers, or those servers are failing.
issues.title: DNS Unable to Resolve Due to Downstream Issues
issues.rootReason: External dependency failure, network connectivity issues from DNS server to external resolvers, or misconfiguration.
issues.impactSummary: The DNS server will fail to resolve queries it cannot answer from cache, leading to hostname resolution failures for its clients.
issues.recommendation: Verify network connectivity from the DNS server to its configured upstream resolvers. Check health and availability of authoritative DNS sources.

ruleIntents.name: DNS Unavailable
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: DNS service {component} is unavailable, preventing {upstream} from resolving hostnames.

ruleIntents.name: Incorrect DNS Resolution
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: DNS service {component} is returning incorrect or stale records, directing {upstream} to invalid or unintended destinations.

ruleIntents.name: High DNS Latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: DNS service {component} is experiencing high query latency, delaying hostname resolution for {upstream} and increasing overall request times.

ruleIntents.name: Downstream DNS Resolution Failure
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: DNS service {component} is unable to resolve names due to issues with its downstream authoritative servers or recursive resolvers. This impacts {upstream} clients attempting to resolve hostnames.

issues.cause: One or more downstream components connected to the load balancer are reporting unhealthy status or high error rates.
issues.title: Downstream Instance Unhealthy
issues.rootReason: Unhealthy downstream instances can lead to degraded service quality, increased latency, or complete service unavailability for requests routed to them. The load balancer must detect and isolate these instances.
issues.impactSummary: Degraded service performance, increased error rates, or partial service unavailability for clients accessing services through this load balancer.
issues.recommendation: Investigate the health of downstream components. Ensure proper health checks are configured on the load balancer and that unhealthy instances are removed from the rotation promptly. Consider auto-scaling or failover strategies.

issues.cause: The upstream component is sending requests at a rate that exceeds the configured or practical capacity of the load balancer or its aggregated downstream capacity.
issues.title: Upstream Request Rate Exceeded
issues.rootReason: Excessive upstream traffic can overwhelm the load balancer, leading to increased queueing, higher latency, and potential saturation of downstream services. This can result in cascading failures.
issues.impactSummary: Increased request latency, higher error rates (e.g., 503 Service Unavailable) from the load balancer, and potential for cascading failures in downstream services.
issues.recommendation: Implement rate limiting or throttling mechanisms on the load balancer or upstream components. Scale out the load balancer and its downstream dependencies to handle increased load. Consider backpressure mechanisms.

issues.cause: Requests passing through the load balancer, particularly on the synchronous path, are experiencing significantly increased end-to-end latency.
issues.title: High Latency Detected on Synchronous Request Path
issues.rootReason: For synchronous request paths, high latency directly impacts user experience and can cause timeouts in upstream services. This could be due to overloaded load balancer instances, slow downstream services, or network congestion.
issues.impactSummary: Poor user experience due to slow responses, upstream service timeouts, and potential degradation of system responsiveness.
issues.recommendation: Investigate network latency, load balancer resource utilization (CPU, memory), and the response times of individual downstream services. Optimize downstream service performance or scale out resources.

ruleIntents.name: Isolate Unhealthy Downstream
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: When {component} detects that a {downstream} service instance is unhealthy, it will stop routing traffic to that instance to maintain overall service reliability.

ruleIntents.name: Handle Upstream Overload
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: If the {upstream} component sends a high volume of requests that exceeds {component}'s capacity, {component} will shed excess load to protect itself and its {downstream} services.

ruleIntents.name: Monitor Synchronous Path Latency
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Elevated end-to-end latency observed on the synchronous request path through {component} indicates performance degradation, potentially originating from {upstream} congestion, {component} overload, or slow {downstream} responses.

issues.cause: The client component failed to resolve the DNS name of its downstream dependencies, specifically related to the 'dns_discovery' service.
issues.title: Client unable to resolve downstream services via DNS
issues.rootReason: Failure in the 'dns_discovery' service, incorrect DNS configuration, or network connectivity issues preventing DNS resolution.
issues.impactSummary: Client requests to downstream services will fail due to inability to find their network addresses, leading to a complete service outage for the user.
issues.recommendation: Verify the health and availability of the 'dns_discovery' service. Check network connectivity between the client and DNS resolvers. Ensure DNS records for downstream services are correctly configured and accessible.

ruleIntents.name: Detect DNS resolution failure for downstream services
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: The {component} detected issues resolving DNS for its downstream dependencies, specifically involving {downstream} roles like 'dns_discovery'. This may indicate an issue with the {downstream} service itself or the network path.

issues.cause: Downstream compute instances are responding slowly.
issues.title: High backend latency impacting request processing
issues.rootReason: Resource contention, database issues, or slow external dependencies in backend services are causing delays.
issues.impactSummary: Increased end-to-end request latency, potential for client timeouts, and degraded user experience.
issues.recommendation: Investigate performance of downstream compute instances, optimize backend service logic, or scale downstream resources.

issues.cause: Downstream compute instances are unhealthy or not responding to health checks.
issues.title: Unavailable backend services detected
issues.rootReason: Service crashes, deployment failures, network issues, or excessive load causing unresponsiveness on backend compute instances.
issues.impactSummary: Failed requests, increased error rates for clients, and potential service disruption.
issues.recommendation: Check health and logs of downstream compute instances, review deployment status, and ensure proper health check configuration.

issues.cause: Excessive incoming request rate or resource exhaustion on the load balancer itself.
issues.title: Load balancer experiencing high resource utilization
issues.rootReason: Sudden traffic surge, misconfigured client behavior, or insufficient load balancer capacity for current demand.
issues.impactSummary: Increased latency through the load balancer, dropped connections, and 5xx errors returned to upstream clients.
issues.recommendation: Scale load balancer capacity, implement rate limiting at an upstream layer, or optimize load balancer configuration.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate Backend Latency to Load Balancer
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High latency in downstream {downstream} services is causing {component} to experience increased request processing times.

ruleIntents.name: Propagate Backend Unavailability to Load Balancer Error Rate
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: Unavailability of downstream {downstream} services leads to {component} returning errors for client requests.

ruleIntents.name: Load Balancer Overload Impacts Upstream Request Success
ruleIntents.causalNarrativeTemplate: High traffic from {upstream} is overloading {component}, causing it to drop requests or return errors.
